Title: Senior Strategist, Ads and Shopping, Scams
Description:
In accordance with Washington state law, we are highlighting our comprehensive benefits package, which is available to all eligible US based employees. Benefits for this role include:


Health, dental, vision, life, disability insurance
Retirement Benefits: 401(k) with company match
Paid Time Off: 20 days of vacation per year, accruing at a rate of 6.15 hours per pay period for the first five years of employment
Sick Time: 40 hours/year (increased to 69 hours/year for Seattle) including 5 discretionary sick days per instance
Maternity Leave (Short-Term Disability + Baby Bonding): 28-30 weeks
Baby Bonding Leave: 18 weeks
Holidays: 13 paid days per year


Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
4 years of experience in data analytics, trust and safety, policy, cybersecurity, or related fields.


Preferred qualifications:


Master's degree.
Education in, or experience with, machine learning.
Experience in SQL, building dashboards, data collection/transformation, visualization/dashboards, or experience in a scripting/programming language (e.g., Python).
Excellent communication and presentation skills (written and verbal) and the ability to influence cross-functionally at various levels.


About the jobTrust and Safety team members are tasked with identifying and taking on the biggest problems that challenge the safety and integrity of our products. They use technical know-how, excellent problem-solving skills, user insights, and proactive communication to protect users and our partners from abuse across Google products like Search, Maps, Gmail, and Google Ads. On this team, you're a big-picture thinker and strategic team-player with a passion for doing what’s right. You work globally and cross-functionally with Google engineers and product managers to identify and fight abuse and fraud cases at Google speed - with urgency. And you take pride in knowing that every day you are working hard to promote trust in Google and ensuring the highest levels of user safety.

The Trust and Safety (T&S) Ads and Shopping - Scams team is leading the way in reducing scams while improving user experience on the platform. Our goal is integral to our day-to-day work. As a team, we work across multiple policy areas and focus on using various solutions to address the ever changing scam landscape.

As a Trust and Safety Ads Scams Analyst, you will be responsible for protecting users from offline scams - scams which take place outside the Google Ads Platform. You will use data to provide recommendations to products and devise solutions (global and hyperlocal) to stay ahead of the fraudsters. You will also be responsible for ensuring good advertisers are provided the right opportunity to serve. You will use Applied AI, machine learning or other techniques to optimize human review cost and efficiency while continuously identifying enforcement gaps and automation opportunities.

At Google we work hard to earn our users’ trust every day. Trust and Safety is Google’s team of abuse fighting and user trust experts working daily to make the internet a safer place. We partner with teams across Google to deliver bold solutions in abuse areas such as malware, spam and account hijacking. A team of Analysts, Policy Specialists, Engineers, and Program Managers, we work to reduce risk and fight abuse across all of Google’s products, protecting our users, advertisers, and publishers across the globe in over 40 languages.Individual pay is determined by factors including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

US: $116000 - $167000 (USD) + 15% bonus target + equity + benefits

Responsibilities

Learn more about benefits at Google .


Collaborate with cross-functional groups such as PM, Engineering, Sales and Legal to drive projects that increases users' safety and provide better online experience through Google Ads.
Execute complex queries and analyses, build visual models, and construct presentations on abuse trends to aid in fraud prevention and influence change in existing abuse detection systems without compromising the advertiser experience.
Conduct investigations to catch fraudsters, enforce our product policies, learn scam patterns/trends and identify product vulnerabilities.
Identify automation and efficiency opportunities and drive solutions through analysis or cross-functional partnerships.
Perform on-call responsibilities on a rotating basis, including weekend coverage/holidays. This role works with sensitive content or situations and may be exposed to graphic, controversial, or upsetting topics or content.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst (PowerBI Focused)
Description:
Business Intelligence Analyst (PBI Focused)

At AST, we enhance the efficiency, productivity, and safety of flexible aseptic manufacturing processes for the worldwide Life Science Industry by offering innovative products, services, and solutions. 

Do you want to contribute to a shared vision and mission? Would you like to bring your unique talents to have a significant impact with a growing company? At AST we strive to build a workplace that enables every team member to meet and exceed their capabilities. We set our expectations high and provide the environment and resources necessary to bring out the very best.

Competitive compensation for onsite WA applicants is $75,000-$90,000. All applicants are welcome to apply.  

Employee benefits include: Medical (HSA & PPO), Paid Dental, Vision, HSA account with employer contribution, (eligible the first of the month following your date of hire), Long/Short term disability, 401(K) (Traditional & Roth options) with up to 5% company match (eligible 90 days after date of hire), Education Assistance, Paid Time Off (PTO), Paid Holiday, and bonus potential. All benefits on this page are subject to change.

What can I expect in this role?
AST is seeking a motivated and analytical Business Intelligence Analyst to support reporting, data analysis, and business decision-making across the organization. This role is ideal for someone early in their analytics career who enjoys working with data, solving problems, and building reports that help teams better understand business performance.

The Business Intelligence Analyst will work closely with business leaders to develop reports, dashboards, and data visualizations that provide actionable insights and support strategic decision-making. This position offers an opportunity to grow technical skills while gaining exposure to a variety of business functions and data sources.

Key Qualifications:

Build, maintain, and enhance Power BI reports and dashboards that support business decision-making.
Create engaging and user-friendly data visualizations that communicate trends, performance, and opportunities.
Partner with business stakeholders to understand reporting needs and translate requirements into reporting solutions.
Analyze data to identify trends, patterns, risks, and opportunities.
Support ad hoc reporting and analytical requests across departments.
Assist leaders in understanding business performance through meaningful data insights.
Develop and track key performance indicators (KPIs).
Use Power Query and other tools to prepare, organize, and transform data for reporting.
Validate data accuracy and investigate discrepancies to ensure reporting reliability.
Conduct routine audits of reports and dashboards.
Maintain documentation for reports, dashboards, and data sources.

Support special projects and other analytics initiatives as needed.

Additional duties as assigned.

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.

At AST, we enhance the efficiency, productivity, and safety of flexible aseptic manufacturing processes for the worldwide Life Science Industry by offering innovative products, services, and solutions.

 What can I expect in this role?

Title: Data Engineer, Product Analytics
Description:
As a Data Engineer at Meta, you will shape the future of people-facing and business-facing products we build across our entire family of applications (Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, WhatsApp, Reality Labs, Threads). Your technical skills and analytical mindset will be utilized designing and building some of the world's most extensive data sets, helping to craft experiences for billions of people and hundreds of millions of businesses worldwide.In this role, you will collaborate with software engineering, data science, and product management teams to design/build scalable data solutions across Meta to optimize growth, strategy, and user experience for our 3 billion plus users, as well as our internal employee community.You will be at the forefront of identifying and solving some of the most interesting data challenges at a scale few companies can match. By joining Meta, you will become part of a world-class data engineering community dedicated to skill development and career growth in data engineering and beyond.Data Engineering: You will guide teams by building optimal data artifacts (including datasets and visualizations) to address key questions. You will refine our systems, design logging solutions, and create scalable data models. Ensuring data security and quality, and with a strong focus on efficiency, you will suggest architecture and development approaches and data management standards to address complex analytical problems.Product leadership: You will use data to shape product development, identify new opportunities, and tackle upcoming challenges. You'll ensure our products add value for users and businesses, by prioritizing projects, and driving innovative solutions to respond to challenges or opportunities.Communication and influence: You won't simply present data, but tell data-driven stories. You will convince and influence your partners using clear insights and recommendations. You will build credibility through structure and clarity, and be a trusted strategic partner.

Data Engineer, Product Analytics Responsibilities:


Conceptualize and own the data architecture for multiple large-scale projects, while evaluating design and operational cost-benefit tradeoffs within systems
Create and contribute to frameworks that improve the efficacy of logging data, while working with data infrastructure to triage issues and resolve
Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and data scientists to understand data needs, representing key data insights in a meaningful way
Define and manage Service Level Agreements for all data sets in allocated areas of ownership
Determine and implement the security model based on privacy requirements, confirm safeguards are followed, address data quality issues, and evolve governance processes within allocated areas of ownership
Design, build, and launch collections of sophisticated data models and visualizations that support multiple use cases across different products or domains
Solve our most challenging data integration problems, utilizing optimal Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) patterns, frameworks, query techniques, sourcing from structured and unstructured data sources
Assist in owning existing processes running in production, optimizing complex code through advanced algorithmic concepts
Optimize pipelines, dashboards, frameworks, and systems to facilitate easier development of data artifacts
Influence product and cross-functional teams to identify data opportunities to drive impact
Mentor team members by giving/receiving actionable feedback


Minimum Qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience
4+ years of experience where the primary responsibility involves working with data. This could include roles such as data analyst, data scientist, data engineer, or similar positions
4+ years of experience with SQL, ETL, data modeling, and at least one programming language (e.g., Python, C++, C#, Scala, etc.)


Preferred Qualifications:


Master's or Ph.D degree in a STEM field
Demonstrated ability to integrate AI tools to optimize/redesign workflows and drive measurable impact (e.g., efficiency gains, quality improvements)
Experience adhering to and implementing responsible, ethical AI practices (e.g., risk assessment, bias mitigation, quality and accuracy reviews)
Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies


About Meta:

Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

Meta is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, sexual stereotypes, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Meta participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law. Please note that Meta may leverage artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies in connection with applications for employment.

Meta is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities in our recruiting process. If you need any assistance or accommodations due to a disability, please let us know at accommodations-ext@meta.com.

$147,000/year to $208,000/year + bonus + equity + benefits

Individual compensation is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. In addition to base compensation, Meta offers benefits. Learn more about benefits at Meta.

Title: Program Analyst
Description:
About TransWest: At TransWest, we design, implement, and manage world-class transportation systems for some of the most recognizable organizations globally. With a relentless focus on reliability, safety, and exceptional client service, we are redefining the way people commute while supporting our clients' sustainability and operational goals.

As a Transportation Business Analyst at TransWest, your mission is to transform data into decisions. You'll be the storyteller behind the strategy—providing the insights that power everything from daily operations to quarterly planning. If you're driven to deliver clarity, inspire confidence, and solve complex problems through data, this is your chance to shape the future of how we move.

Compensation:


$90,000 - $110,000 / year
Annual Discretionary Bonus Program


Benefits:


Medical, Dental, Vision & Life Insurance
401k with matching
Annual pay raises
Holiday pay
Paid time off up to 80 hours per year with increasing amounts based on your years of service


Responsibilities:

Analytical Leadership


Gather, validate, and analyze data sets coming from a range of source across commute, campus, and on-demand programs
Identify trends, develop forecasts, and offer strategic recommendations to improve service and efficiency.
Monitor performance metrics and SLA's including ridership, OTP, GPS, fleet maintenance intervals, parking occupancy, and incident reports.
Collaborate with clients and client stakeholders to fulfill reporting requests and provide actionable insights.


Program Reporting & Storytelling


Develop dashboards and scorecards in Power BI for internal teams and external client use.
Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports to inform MBRs, QBRs, and client presentations.
Create compelling data visualizations and presentations to communicate key findings and outcomes.


Operational Collaboration


Partner with Dispatch and Operations to monitor and validate incidents and service reliability.
Deliver actionable insights to Program Managers to support client communications and decisions.
Field incoming questions and issues across chat, email, phone, and tech platforms, supporting real-time decision-making.


Optimization & Insight Delivery


Proactively identify opportunities to enhance service performance and client value.
Recommend operational changes to Program Team based on historical data, current metrics, and predictive modeling.
Support new business planning efforts by contributing data-informed logistics strategies.


Culture & Core Values


Model TransWest's culture of safety, inclusion, and data-informed decision making.
Be a strong communicator and collaborator across all levels of the organization.
Embrace our entrepreneurial, fast-paced environment while balancing rigor and creativity.
Other duties as needed by our team.


Requirements:


Associate's degree or equivalent work experience in Business, Data Analytics, Operations, Transportation, or a related field.
Minimum of 1 year of experience analyzing data, interpreting trends, and developing actionable recommendations.
Proficiency in Microsoft 365, including advanced skills in Excel and working knowledge of Word, PowerPoint, Teams, and Outlook.
Experience supporting programs, projects, or operational initiatives in a professional environment.
Experience in transportation operations, transportation demand management, transit, mobility services, logistics, or a related field.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities independently while collaborating effectively within cross-functional teams.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to present complex information in a clear, concise manner.


Preferences:


1+ years of experience building and using PowerBI dashboarding and data visualization.
Proven ability to identify trends, develop strategies and implement solutions to drive results.
Knowledge of Transportation technology like Tripshot, and systems or ability to learn quickly.


TransWest Culture: Come join our close-knit team. We enjoy having summer BBQ's, holiday parties, games, contests and so much more at our bus yards and in the office.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, and Belonging: At TransWest, we pride ourselves on building a diverse workforce and collaborative spaces where people of different lived experiences, backgrounds, abilities, and identities can thrive. Everyone on the team at TransWest emphasizes and practices our core values every day that are based on continued growth, reliability, safety, caring for our work, clients, and each other. Our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion is an evolving area where we will continue to listen and learn from our team members, business partners, community partners, and thought leaders to optimize our impact.

Title: Data Engineer, E-Commerce
Description:
Responsibilities
As a data engineer in the Data Platform E-Commerce team, you will have the opportunity to build, optimize and grow one of the largest data platforms in the world. You'll have the opportunity to gain hands-on experience on all kinds of systems in the data platform ecosystem. Your work will have a direct and huge impact on the company's core products as well as hundreds of millions of users.

Responsibilities - What You'll Do
• Design and build data transformations efficiently and reliably for different purposes (e.g. reporting, growth analysis, multi-dimensional analysis);
• Design and implement reliable, scalable, robust and extensible big data systems that support core products and business;
• Establish solid design and best engineering practice for engineers as well as non-technical people.

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications:
• BS or MS degree in Computer Science or related technical field or equivalent practical experience;
• Experience in the Big Data technologies(Hadoop, M/R, Hive, Spark, Metastore, Presto, Flume, Kafka, ClickHouse, Flink etc.);
• Experience with performing data analysis, data ingestion and data integration;
• Experience with ETL(Extraction, Transformation & Loading) and architecting data systems;
• Experience with schema design, data modeling and SQL queries;
• Passionate and self-motivated about technologies in the Big Data area.

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.​
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​
​
Diversity & Inclusion​
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​


TikTok Accommodation
TikTok is committed to providing reasonable accommodations in our recruitment processes for candidates with disabilities, pregnancy, sincerely held religious beliefs or other reasons protected by applicable laws. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation, please reach out to us at https://tinyurl.com/RA-request​


Job Information
【For Pay Transparency】Compensation Description (Annually)
The base salary range for this position in the selected city is $148200 - $300960 annually.​
Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.​
Benefits may vary depending on the nature of employment and the country work location. Employees have day one access to medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) savings plan with company match, paid parental leave, short-term and long-term disability coverage, life insurance, wellbeing benefits, among others. Employees also receive 10 paid holidays per year, 10 paid sick days per year and 17 days of Paid Personal Time (prorated upon hire with increasing accruals by tenure).​
The Company reserves the right to modify or change these benefits programs at any time, with or without notice.​
For Los Angeles County (unincorporated) Candidates:​
Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with all federal, state, and local laws including the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Our company believes that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship on the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of the conditional offer of employment:​
1. Interacting and occasionally having unsupervised contact with internal/external clients and/or colleagues;​
2. Appropriately handling and managing confidential information including proprietary and trade secret information and access to information technology systems; and​
3. Exercising sound judgment.​

Title: Workforce Insights Strategist, Career Choice
Description:
Description

Amazon Career Choice empowers employees to build skills for new careers at Amazon or elsewhere.

This work begins by understanding which career paths are most in demand and viable to support through education and training experiences that can be completed while employees continue to work in their current roles. We currently offer nearly 50 different career options and 500+ schools to over one million eligible employees in 14 countries. For us, success is an employee completing an educational program and then being placed in a new job using the skills they learned through Career Choice.

We are looking to expand our policy & strategy team to welcome someone who can dive deep into data about the future of work and bring insights forward to the big picture, answering the fundamental question of which future career paths we can build successfully for our employees. This role will have primary responsibility for collecting and curating data on labor market trends, industry hiring, and workforce evolution, making informed predictions about where the most essential skills and careers will create the most hiring opportunity.

We expect that you come to the team with a background in quantitative analytics, research, and statistics, with prior applied experience in labor, workforce, or employment strategy or a parallel field (e.g., education, talent management, public policy). Comfort with AI tools and the ability to validate output is key. You will be providing guidance and support to program managers with portfolios covering diverse geographies and industries, helping them to optimize for the best employment outcomes we can achieve together.

Key job responsibilities


Identify and manage public and vendor-contracted data sources providing insights on hiring trends, labor demand, and in-demand skills
Produce regular and ad-hoc analyses of that data for different industries, geographies, and employers
Keep peers and senior leadership informed of trends showing both emerging needs and declining demand for high-velocity decision support around program offerings
Analyze and share insights from program surveys to surface voice of customer issues for further investigation or intervention by program teams
Analyze effectiveness of different educational programs against hiring requirements to indicate where different educational offerings may be required
Prepare inputs for technical documents, white papers, and AI knowledge base/model training
Coordinate with Central Science and other research teams for access to internal data and cross-team evaluation projects


A day in the life

Every day is different. You might be working on integrating an external data source with our tech and product teams, drafting a document showing recent and projected trends in a particular industry, or helping the program teams isolate actionable insights and themes from individual opinions expressed in survey responses.

About The Team

Our mission is simple: help people build careers they're excited about. We manage a network of 600+ educational partners and work directly with employers to create hiring pathways for graduates. We measure success not by enrollment, but by whether someone completes their program and lands a new job. We're a small, collaborative team that operates with a startup mentality inside a large company — moving fast, using data to make decisions, and constantly iterating on what works. You'll join a group of people who care deeply about workforce development and believe that access to education can change lives.

Basic Qualifications


Experience analyzing large datasets and communicating findings to stakeholders and senior leaders
Experience in data analysis and extracting insights to make data-backed business decisions
Can develop ad-hoc reporting to meet specific business needs
Can communicate effectively with all levels of the organization
6+ years of program or project management experience in quantitative research or analytical sciences


Preferred Qualifications


Master's degree or above in BI, finance, engineering, statistics, computer science, mathematics or equivalent quantitative field
Knowledge of analytics & statistical tools such as SAS, PowerBI, SQL & ETL DW concepts


Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

Title: Document Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Document Reviewer

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.

Key Responsibilities


Carefully review large volumes of structured and unstructured data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Identify and flag issues related to data quality, consistency, and potential risks involving Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Annotate, classify, and, where necessary, redact sensitive information to maintain privacy and compliance standards.
Follow strict guidelines, taxonomies, and workflow processes for data categorization and validation.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks to verify data integrity and validate outputs.
Maintain detailed records of reviewed documents and actions taken to ensure traceability and accountability.
Collaborate with the customer's team, providing clear and concise written and verbal communication about findings and recommendations.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience in document review, auditing, annotation, redaction, or data classification at scale.
Exceptional attention to detail with a high level of accuracy in repetitive tasks.
Familiarity with handling and protecting Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to document findings and communicate effectively with team members.
Demonstrated ability to follow complex guidelines and adhere to established taxonomies.
Experience performing QA or validation work on large data sets.
Ability to balance efficiency with precision in a remote work environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in legal, compliance, privacy, auditing, compliance or information security domains.
Prior experience supporting data-driven AI or machine learning projects.
Comfort with collaboration tools and digital workflow management platforms.

Title: Pharmacy Data Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

This organization operates within the nonprofit healthcare sector, focusing on delivering health services through a large system of associates. It addresses the complex challenges of healthcare delivery by fostering a culture grounded in service, growth, care, and connection. The organization supports a workforce of over 97,000 associates and emphasizes inclusive and supportive environments, aiming to impact healthcare meaningfully.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Pharmacy Data Analyst role is designed to support quality improvement initiatives by collecting, analyzing, and interpreting complex data related to pharmacy operations. This position plays a crucial role in identifying strategic trends and providing data-driven recommendations to enhance service quality. The role also involves managing the scope and format of statistical reports and educating staff on accessing and interpreting data to support informed decision-making within the organization.




3. Responsibilities:

• Collect, analyze, and interpret complex data to identify strategic trends and support quality improvement.

• Define the scope and format for all statistical and analytical reports, including ad hoc requests.

• Ensure accuracy and reliability of all source data through regular review and reconciliation.

• Provide expertise and education to staff on accessing and interpreting report data.




4. Requirements:

• High school diploma equivalency with 2 years of cumulative experience, or an Associate''s or Bachelor''s degree, or 4 years of applicable cumulative job-specific experience.

• Experience as a 340B analyst.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Salary range: $65,954 - $90,683.37 per year.




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Paid time off (PTO).

• Various health insurance options and wellness plans.

• Retirement benefits including employer match plans.

• Long-term and short-term disability coverage.

• Employee assistance programs (EAP).

• Parental leave and adoption assistance.

• Tuition reimbursement.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About The Company

Government Employees Health Association, Inc. (G.E.H.A) is a distinguished nonprofit member organization dedicated to providing comprehensive health and dental benefits to millions of federal employees, retirees, military retirees, and their families since 1937. Recognized as one of the largest health and dental benefit providers in the United States, G.E.H.A offers an extensive network that ensures members receive quality care when they need it most. The organization’s core mission is to empower federal workers to achieve optimal health and wellness by delivering accessible, reliable, and member-centric benefits. Headquartered in Lee's Summit, Missouri, G.E.H.A emphasizes a culture of inclusivity, innovation, and service excellence, fostering an environment where employees can grow professionally while making a meaningful impact on the lives of its members.

About The Role

The Business Data Analyst at G.E.H.A will play a pivotal role in supporting the organization’s data-driven decision-making processes. This position involves identifying business problems, gathering detailed requirements, and designing data solutions that align with organizational goals and enterprise data strategies. The analyst will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, technical teams, and data architects to ensure that data solutions meet business needs, are compliant with regulatory standards, and withstand audit scrutiny. Responsibilities include creating comprehensive documentation of business rules, data workflows, and technical specifications, as well as supporting the development of data models and transformations within cloud data frameworks. The role requires active participation in Agile and Scrum practices, ensuring continuous improvement and adherence to company standards. The Business Data Analyst will also champion data governance, quality, and security initiatives, contributing to the organization’s commitment to trusted and accurate data management.

Qualifications


3-5 years of relevant experience in data analysis, business analysis, or related fields, preferably within the healthcare industry.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Healthcare Administration, Mathematics, or a related discipline; equivalent work experience may be considered.
Proficiency in Microsoft Azure Data tools or Snowflake Technologies.
Strong skills in Data Querying, Data Profiling, Data Transformation, and Reporting.
Experience in problem solving, root cause analysis, and troubleshooting data issues.
Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and Agile/Scrum methodologies.
Knowledge of compliance, security, and regulatory requirements, especially related to healthcare data.
Willingness to learn healthcare and analytics industries if not already familiar.
Preferred certifications in Azure, Snowflake, Databricks, or similar cloud platforms.
Ability to provide a dedicated home office space with high-speed internet (minimum 30x5 Mbps) and low latency.


Responsibilities


Review, analyze, and document detailed business rules and data needs to ensure clarity and completeness.
Facilitate discussions with stakeholders and technical teams to capture use cases, requirements, and technical specifications.
Collaborate with developers and architects to define and document data solutions that meet business needs.
Secure business/stakeholder approval for data solutions and ensure compliance with audit standards.
Document data ingestion, transformation workflows, data models, and sharing processes to maintain data integrity and trustworthiness.
Maintain up-to-date documentation reflecting current data states and unresolved issues.
Support the implementation of cloud data frameworks aligned with enterprise strategies and policies.
Validate that data and processes fulfill documented business rules and requirements.
Participate in Agile ceremonies such as sprint planning, backlog grooming, and reviews.
Present technical and business documentation to stakeholders and team members.
Ensure adherence to departmental standards for documentation and compliance for audit readiness.
Perform peer reviews of documentation and data models to promote quality and consistency.
Create data models based on solution decisions in collaboration with data architects and developers.
Perform other duties as assigned to support organizational data initiatives.


Benefits


Competitive salary range, with potential for growth based on performance.
Incentive plans to reward contributions and achievements.
Health, Vision, and Dental insurance coverage effective from day one.
401(k) retirement plan with dollar-for-dollar company match up to 4% of employee contributions and a 6% annual company contribution.
Robust employee well-being programs promoting health and work-life balance.
Paid Time Off and Personal Community Enrichment Time to support personal and professional growth.
Company-provided Basic Life and AD&D insurance, along with Short-Term and Long-Term Disability coverage.
Tuition Assistance Program to support ongoing education and professional development.


Equal Opportunity

G.E.H.A is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to fostering an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate against any individual based on sex, race, color, national origin, disability, religion, age, military status, genetic information, veteran status, pregnancy, marital status, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. We value diversity and are dedicated to providing equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants and employees. G.E.H.A ensures a workplace free from discrimination and harassment and encourages applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply.

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Our well-known client in the gaming industry is seeking a Marketing Data Analyst to support content performance tracking, reporting, and insights development across multiple social content and channel optimization initiatives. This role will partner closely with the Audience Development Manager and play a key role in delivering data driven insights, shaping channel strategies, and supporting marketing performance reporting.

This position offers the opportunity to work in a collaborative and fast paced environment, contributing to marketing effectiveness through actionable analysis across earned, owned, and user generated content.

This is a hybrid role with a combination of onsite and remote work. The primary location for this position is Irvine, California, though alternative arrangements may be considered.

 

Key Responsibilities

Own reporting tied to major campaign and release milestones
Build and maintain advanced Boolean queries to support social listening and analysis
Design and manage dashboards to track content and campaign performance
Ensure key performance indicators are reflected in reporting and aligned with overall program goals
Partner with analytics teams to deliver accurate and actionable insights
Develop and maintain knowledge of audience communities and engagement trends
Track content cycles including major updates, releases, and campaign phases

 

Qualifications:

Three or more years of experience in data analysis within media planning, social media, or digital marketing analytics
Experience managing projects in a fast-changing environment
Strong understanding of social and video platform ecosystems and content formats
Experience using social analytics and listening tools such as Sprinklr or similar platforms
Experience building dashboards using tools such as Tableau, Looker, or Power BI
Proven ability to conduct social listening and build detailed topic queries for analysis
Strong analytical, written, and verbal communication skills with the ability to explain complex ideas clearly
Proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets and presentation tools

 

Preferred Qualifications

Experience working in digital marketing or entertainment related environments
Educational background in marketing, data analytics, or media
Experience with data science or statistical analysis
Working knowledge of SQL, including data querying tools such as BigQuery
Interest in gaming or digital entertainment

In this position, you may have access to client or customer systems, confidential and/or proprietary information or data.

Benefits

Creative Circle's Freelance Employee benefits package includes eligibility for Minimum Essential Coverage (MEC) medical plan, dental/vision/term life package, discount prescription program, critical illness, accident, tele-behavioral health, and 401(k) plan. Sick leave is provided to Candidates whose assignment work location is in a state or city subject to sick leave laws. A Minimum Value (MV) PPO medical plan, Employee Stock Purchase Plan, and paid holiday eligibility are based on length and dates of service.

Title: Virtual Assistant - Remote
Description:
About The Company

Decentralized Masters is a pioneering organization at the forefront of DeFi education globally. Established just two years ago, the company has experienced rapid growth from a small team of co-founders to over 150 dedicated professionals. Recognized as one of the fastest-growing enterprises in the DeFi sector, Decentralized Masters is poised for continued success, with industry insiders projecting its evolution into a unicorn company by 2030. Operating on a bootstrapped model, the company is on track to generate an impressive $50 million in revenue this year alone. Its mission is to empower individuals with the knowledge and tools necessary to succeed in the rapidly evolving world of decentralized finance and Web3 technology.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly organized and professional Virtual Assistant to join our dynamic team. This role is vital in supporting our operational efficiency and ensuring seamless communication with our clients and internal teams. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing emails and communications, escalating customer queries appropriately, drafting reports and presentations, and maintaining clear and continuous communication with clients. Additionally, the Virtual Assistant will handle general administrative duties, facilitate customer onboarding, and assist in scheduling and calendar management. This position offers an exciting opportunity to be part of a rapidly growing company that is shaping the future of DeFi education and Web3 innovation. The successful candidate will work remotely from South America, contributing to a global team committed to excellence and innovation.

Qualifications


High school diploma; additional qualifications as an Administrator or Executive Assistant are a plus
Proven experience as a Virtual Assistant or in a relevant administrative role
Customer Service experience is mandatory
Native English speaker or at least a professional level of proficiency in English
Must be located in South America
Excellent experience with word-processing software and spreadsheets (e.g., Google Suite, MS Excel)
Knowledge of online calendars and scheduling tools (e.g., Google Calendar)
Previous experience working with Circle is an advantage
Familiarity with DeFi or cryptocurrency concepts is an added advantage
Excellent communication skills via phone, email, and instant messaging
Strong time management and organizational skills
Ability to work independently and as part of a remote team


Responsibilities


Manage and respond to emails and other communications promptly and professionally
Escalate customer queries to the appropriate departments for resolution
Draft and prepare reports, presentations, and other documentation as required
Maintain clear, consistent, and professional communication with clients and team members
Assist with onboarding new customers and clients
Handle general administrative duties to support daily operations
Coordinate scheduling and calendar management for team members and clients
Ensure timely follow-up on pending tasks and communications
Support team projects and initiatives as needed


Benefits


Competitive salary package
Full-time employment opportunity
Unlimited paid time off (PTO)
Participation in team off-site events and retreats
Work in a young, dynamic culture with team members across more than 30 countries
Fully remote work environment, allowing flexibility to work from anywhere in the world
Opportunity to be part of a rapidly expanding company influencing the future of DeFi and Web3


Equal Opportunity

We are proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, or any other basis protected by federal, state, or local law.

Title: Administrative Assistant (Remote)
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates in the online auction and liquidation industry, focusing on the sale of consumer goods, electronics, and surplus inventory. It addresses the challenge of providing a streamlined digital bidding experience for customers, facilitating efficient asset liquidation and resale through an online platform.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Administrative Assistant role is essential to maintaining smooth daily business operations in a remote, fast-paced environment. This position supports the administrative needs of the team by managing communication, scheduling, and documentation, contributing to the overall efficiency and effectiveness of the organization.




3. Responsibilities:

• Provide comprehensive administrative support to ensure efficient office operations.

• Manage and respond to emails, correspondence, and customer service inquiries professionally and promptly.

• Schedule meetings, manage calendars, and coordinate appointments for senior staff.

• Assist in preparing regular reports, presentations, and documents.

• Perform data entry and maintain accurate digital records related to auctions, sales, and customer information.

• Liaise with internal teams to handle requests and queries.

• Organize and maintain digital filing systems for easy information access.

• Support auction and liquidation processes by assisting with listing coordination and post-sale administration.




4. Requirements:

• Proven experience as an administrative assistant or in office support.

• Exceptional organizational skills with effective task prioritization and time management.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills with professionalism.

• Proficient in MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) or equivalent G Suite applications.

• Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

• Ability to work independently and proactively in a remote setting.

• Experience in e-commerce, online retail, or auction environments is desirable but not required.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Competitive hourly wage of $25 - $39 USD, commensurate with experience.

• Fully remote working arrangement.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Production Data Analyst for Feature Animation - ILM Vancouver
Description:
This role is considered Hybrid, which means the employee will work 2-3 days onsite at a Company designated location and occasionally from home.

Position Summary

The Production Data Analyst for Feature Animation oversees production data and analysis for assigned projects. This position requires a comprehensive understanding of the feature animation production lifecycle. Working closely with Producers and other production staff, you will manage the day-to-day activity of a project as well as analysis and reporting including both internal and client report templates.

What You’ll Do



Manage and optimize internal and client reporting templates across the feature animation portfolio.
Support Producer in creating and managing reports.
Develop and maintain show(s) master sequence, quota schedule, and scenario planner with the Producers and Production staff.
Prepare standardized weekly and monthly reports and other special reports as needed.
Compile and deliver internal weekly status reports for the Producer(s).
Responsible for reviewing weekly detail reports for accuracy and making reclassifications as required.
Partner with Producers and Production Managers to ensure show inventory is flowing through the pipeline and that all departments monitor their daily inventory levels, providing accurate output figures and forecasts.
Pro-actively make recommendations for improvements to production and workflows, particularly with respect to scheduling, flow of information, milestones, and client reporting.
Support Feature Animation forecasting and planning.
Work to understand the wants and needs of all teams to help prioritize workloads on a short- and long-term basis.
Appropriately elevate issues and solutions to Producer(s).
Mentor Production Staff on analyzing their bids and schedules and generating reports.



What We’re Looking For



3–4 years of production experience, including a minimum of 2 years of specialized experience within feature animation.
Expert proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets, with a proven track record of managing and analyzing large datasets.
Strong, demonstrated analytical skills.
Energetic, resourceful, and hands-on individual with a strong service orientation.
Ability to thrive in fast-paced, high-pressure environments, where you handle multiple tasks while meeting strict deadlines.
Adaptable and flexible, possessing a deep understanding of how to navigate and support a creatively driven production environment.
Exceptional cross-functional collaborator who uses diplomacy to handle challenging issues, solve problems, and align multiple stakeholders.
Strong communication and active listening skills.
Growth-oriented mindset focused on driving improvements.



JoinILM

The hiring range for this position in Vancouver is C$67,000 to C$85,700 per year based on a 40-hour work week. The amount of hours scheduled per week may vary based on business needs. The base pay actually offered will take into account internal equity and also may vary depending on the candidate’s geographic region, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. A full range of medical, financial, and/or other variable pay or benefits, may be offered dependent on the level and position offered.

Title: Data Entry Specialist
Description:
Job Description

Location: Wilmington, DE

In this office-based position you’ll experience a supportive approach to your wellbeing and continuous learning opportunities in our friendly and inclusive workplace. Find out more at computershare.com/flex.

We give you a world of potential

Our Document Services team plays a critical role in supporting our corporate clients by acting as a statutory agent for service of process both nationwide and internationally. The team is responsible for receiving, imaging, processing, and forwarding legal documents and government notices, as well as managing incoming and outgoing mail and other administrative functions. In this role, you’ll ensure documents are processed accurately and on time, helping mitigate legal and service‑level risks while supporting strong client relationships.

‌

If you’re passionate about delivering high‑quality service, thrive in a fast‑paced, detail‑driven environment, and are eager to be part of a collaborative and globally diverse organization, this could be an excellent next step in your career.

A role you will love

We are a global business with an entrepreneurial spirit, and we are proud of that. What that comes with this is a fast-paced environment and lots of change so you will be resilient in nature and able to adapt quickly and embrace the pace of change we often work at.

As a detail‑driven professional with strong organizational and problem‑solving skills, you will serve as a key point of control within our Document Services operation, helping ensure accuracy, timeliness, and consistency in how documents are processed on behalf of our clients. You will be responsible for receiving, processing, and sending legal and government documents on behalf of our clients, and making sure the information is accurate and completed on time.

Some Of Your Key Responsibilities Will Also Include


Prioritizing incoming service of process and government documents based on urgency, client‑specific requirements, and deadlines.
Performing pre‑send and post‑send quality checks to ensure accurate data entry, routing, and document imaging.
Identifying and resolving routine exceptions, escalating more complex issues in line with established procedures.
Preparing daily end‑of‑day summaries and maintaining basic workflow and quality dashboards.
Supporting team leaders by identifying recurring errors or bottlenecks and contributing improvement ideas.
Assisting with cross‑location workload balancing and backfilling processing or mailroom tasks as needed to meet service‑level.


What will you bring to the role?

We’re looking for a highly detail‑oriented professional who thrives in a fast‑paced environment and takes pride in delivering accurate, high‑quality work. You’ll bring a strong sense of ownership, excellent time management skills, and the ability to prioritize effectively while working collaboratively with others.

Other Key Skills Required For The Role Include


A degree is not required; relevant industry experience will be strongly considered
Exceptional attention to detail with accurate data entry skills.
Experience handling high volumes of documents in a data entry or processing environment.
Strong computer skills, including typing, scanning, email, and internet research.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Self‑motivated with strong organizational and time‑management abilities
Ability to work efficiently under deadlines in a fast‑paced office setting


Rewards designed for you

Paid parental leave, flexible working and a caring and inclusive culture.

Health and wellbeing rewards that can be tailored to support you and your family, including medical, dental and vision.

Invest in your future with 401k matching and tax-advantaged flexible spending plans, including healthcare, dependent care and commuter.

Income protection. Our package includes short and long-term disability benefits, life insurance and supplemental life insurance.

And more. Ours is a welcoming and close-knit community, with experienced colleagues ready to help you grow. Our careers hub will help you find out more about our rewards and life at Computershare, visit computershare.com/careershub.

Compensation. The typical base pay range for this role is $21 - $22/hour. This base pay range is specific to Delaware and may not be applicable to other locations.

About Us

A company to be proud of

We're a global leader in financial administration with over 11,000 employees across more than 22 different countries. At Computershare, it’s more than just a job, our open and inclusive culture means that we will help you to grow, to move forward and make the most of our world of opportunities.

Fairness and culture

We're dedicated to providing you with the opportunity to succeed on your own merits, starting from the application process and continuing throughout your career with us. Our goal is to create an environment where everyone feels valued, to remove barriers and obstacles and ensure equal opportunities for all. For support with accommodations or adjustments during our recruitment process please visit computershare.com/access for further information.

About The Team

Since 1978, we’ve been trusted by companies around the globe to manage their shareholder registry, corporate actions and shareholder meetings and we’re now the number one transfer agency in the world. As client needs evolved, so has our portfolio of services; extending our Issuer Services into equity and entity governance services to companies both public and private worldwide. Careers in Issuer Services could mean anything from assisting shareholders with their portfolios, enabling access to global markets or helping clients deliver on their equity and entity compliance requirements everywhere they operate.

As a detail‑driven professional with strong organizational and problem‑solving skills, you will serve as a key point of control within our Document Services operation, helping ensure accuracy, timeliness, and consistency in how documents are processed on behalf of our clients. You will be responsible for receiving, processing, and sending legal and government documents on behalf of our clients, and making sure the information is accurate and completed on time.

Some Of Your Key Responsibilities Will Also Include

  • Prioritizing incoming service of process and government documents based on urgency, client‑specific requirements, and deadlines.
  • Performing pre‑send and post‑send quality checks to ensure accurate data entry, routing, and document imaging.
  • Identifying and resolving routine exceptions, escalating more complex issues in line with established procedures.
  • Preparing daily end‑of‑day summaries and maintaining basic workflow and quality dashboards.
  • Supporting team leaders by identifying recurring errors or bottlenecks and contributing improvement ideas.
  • Assisting with cross‑location workload balancing and backfilling processing or mailroom tasks as needed to meet service‑level.

What will you bring to the role?

We’re looking for a highly detail‑oriented professional who thrives in a fast‑paced environment and takes pride in delivering accurate, high‑quality work. You’ll bring a strong sense of ownership, excellent time management skills, and the ability to prioritize effectively while working collaboratively with others.

Other Key Skills Required For The Role Include

  • A degree is not required; relevant industry experience will be strongly considered
  • Exceptional attention to detail with accurate data entry skills.
  • Experience handling high volumes of documents in a data entry or processing environment.
  • Strong computer skills, including typing, scanning, email, and internet research.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Self‑motivated with strong organizational and time‑management abilities
  • Ability to work efficiently under deadlines in a fast‑paced office setting

Rewards designed for you

Paid parental leave, flexible working and a caring and inclusive culture.

Health and wellbeing rewards that can be tailored to support you and your family, including medical, dental and vision.

Invest in your future with 401k matching and tax-advantaged flexible spending plans, including healthcare, dependent care and commuter.

Income protection. Our package includes short and long-term disability benefits, life insurance and supplemental life insurance.

And more. Ours is a welcoming and close-knit community, with experienced colleagues ready to help you grow. Our careers hub will help you find out more about our rewards and life at Computershare, visit computershare.com/careershub.

Compensation. The typical base pay range for this role is $21 - $22/hour. This base pay range is specific to Delaware and may not be applicable to other locations.

About Us

 A company to be proud of

We're a global leader in financial administration with over 11,000 employees across more than 22 different countries. At Computershare, it’s more than just a job, our open and inclusive culture means that we will help you to grow, to move forward and make the most of our world of opportunities.

Fairness and culture

We're dedicated to providing you with the opportunity to succeed on your own merits, starting from the application process and continuing throughout your career with us. Our goal is to create an environment where everyone feels valued, to remove barriers and obstacles and ensure equal opportunities for all. For support with accommodations or adjustments during our recruitment process please visit computershare.com/access for further information.

About The Team

Since 1978, we’ve been trusted by companies around the globe to manage their shareholder registry, corporate actions and shareholder meetings and we’re now the number one transfer agency in the world. As client needs evolved, so has our portfolio of services; extending our Issuer Services into equity and entity governance services to companies both public and private worldwide. Careers in Issuer Services could mean anything from assisting shareholders with their portfolios, enabling access to global markets or helping clients deliver on their equity and entity compliance requirements everywhere they operate.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates at the vibrant forefront of the game development industry, focusing exclusively on creating deeply immersive, high-fidelity video games. The studio directly addresses the core industry challenge of producing compelling, player-centric titles by fostering an intensely collaborative ecosystem that empowers creative freedom and decentralizes artistic ownership among its development teams. Built for modern creators, the enterprise fully supports highly flexible work arrangements—including in-studio presence, dynamic hybrid setups, and fully remote options tailored for top-tier talent distributed across North America.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Senior Data Analyst will step into a highly critical, cross-functional role designed to serve as the structural bridge between core software engineering, telemetry analytics, and executive studio strategy. This position is responsible for driving data-informed game design, converting complex player behavioral telemetry into actionable insights that directly influence real-time game systems, monetization models, and long-term business decisions. Serving as the primary steward of the studio's data ecosystem, this role ensures that data acquisition infrastructure and advanced analytical modeling align perfectly with overarching game launch milestones and organizational growth goals.




3. Responsibilities:

• Take complete operational ownership of the end-to-end data lifecycle, managing everything from initial game event instrumentation and telemetry collection to final executive-level presentation

• Partner closely with multidisciplinary game departments and senior studio leadership to architect predictive data collection protocols and long-term analysis strategies

• Define, model, and track core player performance indicators (KPIs)—specifically targeting player engagement, long-term retention curves, and churn predictive modeling—and build the data systems to capture them

• Architect and maintain robust database schemas, continuously optimizing query performance, execution plans, and overall data warehouse health

• Develop, scale, and maintain performant ETL/ELT data pipelines and the underlying infrastructure required to handle high-velocity telemetry streams

• Automate operational reporting workflows and build intuitive, interactive visualization dashboards tailored precisely for diverse technical and creative game teams

• Play and engage with the studio's game titles directly to gain deep contextual familiarity, blending personal gameplay experience with quantitative analysis to inform design recommendations




4. Requirements:

• Minimum of five (5) years of progressive, professional experience in data analysis, analytics engineering, or business intelligence (preferably within the gaming, mobile apps, or high-transaction consumer entertainment sectors)

• Expert-level proficiency in SQL, demonstrating advanced capabilities in complex querying, scalable schema design, indexed views, and query execution performance tuning

• Strong programming skills in languages critical to backend automation and quantitative analysis, with specialized mastery in Python, R, or Java

• Proven history designing, building, orchestrating, and troubleshooting complex ETL processes across modern data warehouses

• Solid mathematical foundations and a deep understanding of statistical modeling techniques, including regression analysis, A/B testing, hypothesis validation, and predictive forecasting algorithms

• Exceptional communication and narrative skills, with a proven ability to translate complex technical metrics, statistical margins, and data anomalies clearly to creative directors and non-technical stakeholders

• Highly self-motivated, mission-driven mindset characterized by the ability to manage competing project priorities independently across cross-functional studio teams




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Target baseline salary framework: $100,000 to $140,000 USD annually

• Final base salary placement within this competitive tier is calibrated dynamically based upon the candidate's core analytics depth, engineering capabilities, geographic location, and specialized database proficiencies

• Total rewards portfolio features a highly lucrative baseline pay structure paired with an expansive enterprise-level benefits package and career trajectory programs




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Comprehensive healthcare security featuring 100% employer-covered Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance plans for employees and their immediate families

• Secure future financial planning backed by a corporate Retirement Savings Plan complete with competitive matching contributions

• Dedicated Career Improvement Plans offering structured corporate resources, training, and financial backing for continuous professional development

• Supreme geographic flexibility through fully supported flexible work arrangements (in-house studio spaces, hybrid schedules, or 100% remote setups across North America)

• A generous, refreshing Time Off Policy promoting true work-life balance and creative recharge

• Active community culture supported by vibrant company events held regularly across both in-person and virtual spaces

• Premium on-site amenities, including a fully stocked kitchen, curated snacks, and company-sponsored local food truck visits




7. Equal Opportunity Statement:

Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Finance Analyst
Description:
Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: New York, NY, USA; Mountain View, CA, USA.Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Statistics, or another quantitative field, or equivalent practical experience.
7 years of experience in financial planning and analysis (FP&A), consulting, or a related function, or an advanced degree.
Experience executing full-cycle FP&A functions including budgeting, forecasting, variance analysis, and modeling within a multinational company setting.
Experience with programming (i.e. SQL).


Preferred qualifications:


Experience writing SQL queries and retrieving data from relational databases.
Ability to self-start and self-directed work in an unstructured environment; comfortable dealing with ambiguity and a fast-paced environment
Ability to plan and manage at both the strategic and operational level.
Ability to create and foster strong cross-functional networks and manage without authority; excellent leadership, influencing and communication skills.
Excellent problem-solving and analysis skills, combined with impeccable business judgment and the ability to communicate and partner with a executive management team.
Excellent spreadsheet skills with experience creating financial dashboards or models.


About the jobThe Media Incentives team ensures that Google makes sound financial decisions for Media Incentives programs. As a Financial Analyst, you will analyze modeling investment scenarios and track performance metrics used by business leaders to make strategic decisions.Individual pay is determined by factors including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

US: $141000 - $206000 (USD) + 15% bonus target + equity + benefits

Responsibilities

Learn more about benefits at Google .


Provide thoughtful leadership, drive insights and analysis to support the Media Incentives team in managing the financial performance of volume-based incentives programs targeting video growth.
Leverage technical expertise to provide quantitative analysis around incentives programs, including support for return on investment and incrementality studies, working in partnership with other teams.
Interact with other Finance, Product, and partner teams to assure strategic and analytical alignment, as well as data consistency and efficiency.
Support regional Finance teams across the globe in crafting incentive portfolios aligned to their business goals.
Support product financial planning and analysis (FP&A) process by forecasting profit and loss (P&L) impact of incentive programs on an ongoing basis.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

Title: Production Data Analyst for Feature Animation - ILM Vancouver
Description:
This role is considered Hybrid, which means the employee will work 2-3 days onsite at a Company designated location and occasionally from home.

Position Summary

The Production Data Analyst for Feature Animation oversees production data and analysis for assigned projects. This position requires a comprehensive understanding of the feature animation production lifecycle. Working closely with Producers and other production staff, you will manage the day-to-day activity of a project as well as analysis and reporting including both internal and client report templates.

What You’ll Do


Manage and optimize internal and client reporting templates across the feature animation portfolio.
Support Producer in creating and managing reports.
Develop and maintain show(s) master sequence, quota schedule, and scenario planner with the Producers and Production staff.
Prepare standardized weekly and monthly reports and other special reports as needed.
Compile and deliver internal weekly status reports for the Producer(s).
Responsible for reviewing weekly detail reports for accuracy and making reclassifications as required.
Partner with Producers and Production Managers to ensure show inventory is flowing through the pipeline and that all departments monitor their daily inventory levels, providing accurate output figures and forecasts.
Pro-actively make recommendations for improvements to production and workflows, particularly with respect to scheduling, flow of information, milestones, and client reporting.
Support Feature Animation forecasting and planning.
Work to understand the wants and needs of all teams to help prioritize workloads on a short- and long-term basis.
Appropriately elevate issues and solutions to Producer(s).
Mentor Production Staff on analyzing their bids and schedules and generating reports.


What We’re Looking For


3–4 years of production experience, including a minimum of 2 years of specialized experience within feature animation.
Expert proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets, with a proven track record of managing and analyzing large datasets.
Strong, demonstrated analytical skills.
Energetic, resourceful, and hands-on individual with a strong service orientation.
Ability to thrive in fast-paced, high-pressure environments, where you handle multiple tasks while meeting strict deadlines.
Adaptable and flexible, possessing a deep understanding of how to navigate and support a creatively driven production environment.
Exceptional cross-functional collaborator who uses diplomacy to handle challenging issues, solve problems, and align multiple stakeholders.
Strong communication and active listening skills.
Growth-oriented mindset focused on driving improvements.


JoinILM

The hiring range for this position in Vancouver is C$67,000 to C$85,700 per year based on a 40-hour work week. The amount of hours scheduled per week may vary based on business needs. The base pay actually offered will take into account internal equity and also may vary depending on the candidate’s geographic region, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. A full range of medical, financial, and/or other variable pay or benefits, may be offered dependent on the level and position offered.
