Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst

Location: Toronto, ON

Duration: 6 Months with possible extension




Role Overview

We are seeking a highly analytical and business-focused Data Analyst to transform complex data into actionable insights that support strategic decision-making and business growth. This role requires end-to-end ownership of business dashboards, analytics initiatives, and data-driven projects while partnering closely with cross-functional stakeholders.

As a key analytics partner, you will collaborate with business leaders, analytics teams, and data engineering teams to scope, prioritize, and deliver impactful insights that improve business performance.




Key Responsibilities

Own, maintain, and optimize key business insights dashboards, serving as the primary point of contact between business users and analytics/data engineering teams.
Identify opportunities to improve dashboard functionality, reporting accuracy, and overall data quality.
Lead analytics projects from initiation to delivery, managing priorities, clarifying ambiguous business requests, defining timelines, and ensuring successful execution.
Perform complex ad-hoc analysis using SQL, including:
Customer segmentation
Cohort analysis
Funnel performance analysis
Root cause analysis
Translate analytical findings into clear, actionable recommendations to support business strategy.
Build, enhance, and maintain scalable dashboards (primarily in Tableau) for KPI tracking, business health monitoring, and funnel visibility.
Communicate insights effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, including senior leadership, through compelling storytelling and data visualization.
Drive data-informed decision-making by synthesizing complex datasets into meaningful business narratives.

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field.
3–6 years of experience in Data Analysis or Business Analysis with increasing ownership and complexity.
Expert-level proficiency in SQL, including:
Complex joins
Window functions
CTEs
Query optimization
Strong hands-on experience with Tableau for building and maintaining production dashboards.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills including pivot tables, advanced formulas, and large dataset manipulation.
Proficiency in Python for data analysis, scripting, and automation.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities with experience handling ambiguous business problems independently.
Excellent communication and storytelling skills with the ability to present insights clearly to business stakeholders.
Strong project ownership with the ability to manage multiple priorities in fast-paced environments.
High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and quality.
Proven ability to collaborate cross-functionally with analytics, product, engineering, and business teams.

Preferred Qualifications

Master’s degree in Analytics, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field.
Experience with A/B testing, experimentation design, and performance measurement.
Prior experience in e-commerce, product analytics, marketing analytics, or customer analytics.
Understanding of data engineering concepts and working knowledge of data pipelines and data quality management.

  • Excellent communication and storytelling skills with the ability to present insights clearly to business stakeholders.
  • Strong project ownership with the ability to manage multiple priorities in fast-paced environments.
  • High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and quality.
  • Proven ability to collaborate cross-functionally with analytics, product, engineering, and business teams.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in Analytics, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field.
  • Experience with A/B testing, experimentation design, and performance measurement.
  • Prior experience in e-commerce, product analytics, marketing analytics, or customer analytics.
  • Understanding of data engineering concepts and working knowledge of data pipelines and data quality management.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
An opportunity has opened for a Data Analyst to join a global commodities trading business, facing off directly to traders and supporting the business as the first technology hire within their Houston location.




Reporting into the Head of Data Science, this role will serve as a key link between front-office stakeholders and an international data function. You’ll get exposure across the whole firm and inherit a clean slate from a technology perspective – ready for your input.




This position sits at the intersection of trading, analytics, and technology. You'll work closely with traders and analysts to understand business requirements, deliver data-driven solutions. The primary responsibilities include collecting, transforming, and organizing critical datasets to support business needs, developing automated reporting solutions, and ensuring clear communication and alignment between local stakeholders and the data team.




A significant part of the role will involve building and maintaining data solutions within Microsoft Fabric, supporting analytical workflows, integrating third-party and real-time data sources, and helping ensure users have access to reliable, scalable data.




The team is ideally looking for someone with experience in a data-focused role (think strong SQL and Python skills) alongside hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric. Experience within commodities, energy markets, trading, or financial services is highly preferred.




Ready to play a lead technology role within a high performing global commodities firm? Apply now.

Title: Data Analyst, Data Intelligence
Description:
Austin, TX (Onsite 4 days per week)

Note: This is a full-time role and we do not offer C2C or C2H employment and are not able to sponsor visas for this position.

About Acrisure

Acrisure Enterprise Technology Group is where human expertise meets advanced technology.

A global fintech leader, Acrisure empowers millions of ambitious businesses and individuals with the right solutions to grow boldly forward. Bringing cutting-edge technology and top-tier human support together, we connect clients with customized solutions across a range of insurance, reinsurance, payroll, benefits, cybersecurity, mortgage services – and more.

In the last twelve years, Acrisure has grown in revenue from $38 million to almost $5 billion and employs over 19,000 colleagues in more than 20 countries. Acrisure was built on entrepreneurial spirit. Prioritizing leadership, accountability, and collaboration, we equip our teams to work at the highest levels possible.

The Role

The Data Intelligence team is chartered with advancing data driven decision making across Acrisure. As a member of the Data Intelligence Team, you will be responsible for leading the requirements gathering, design, implementation and delivery of reporting and analytics capabilities for Finance and Accounting Business Functions. You will be comfortable working across a wide variety of people including senior executive leadership, analysts, program management, and technical team members.

Here are some of the ways in which you’ll achieve impact:


Drive the development of efficient and reliable reporting and analytics capabilities with a customer-first mindset.
Advance the transition of users from legacy means of accessing information to the future state strategies of data enablement and self-service.
Create presentation/visualization of data to see beyond the numbers while transforming raw data into actionable insights and recommendations.
Identify, adopt, and evangelize data analysis best practices and technical standards.
Advocate for and identify creative implementations to optimize business impact.
Empower others to grow understanding of the complexity of data by creatively scaling how teams’ knowledge is shared and applied
Help identify better practices, tools, and relevant trends that can improve data quality across the business.
Design and build BI applications with embedded workflows that automate decision points and enable action directly from data — moving teams beyond passive reporting toward active, data-driven operations.


You may be a fit for this role if you have:


5+ years of industry experience as a Data Analyst or Business Intelligence Professional serving the finance and accounting business functions.
A passion for building remarkable things with data.
Demonstrated ability to be a data storyteller and can go deep in building data visualizations and reporting using third party tools (Tableau and PowerBI).
Strong understanding of data models, structures, theories, principles, and practices.
Proficiency working with stakeholders to gather requirements, understand process and translate them to data and create/evaluate associated KPIs.
Experience with SQL, Python, PowerBI, Tableau, dbt, BigQuery, Git, Palantir and data modeling
Demonstrated experience leveraging AI tools for natural language querying, including crafting effective prompts to extract insights from data
Nice to have: Exposure to product management and/or experience building applications or data-driven tools
Experience with agile project execution and business tools such as Jira & ServiceNow
Have excellent communication and leadership skills and can unpack complex problems in order to find solutions.
Ability to understand the business purpose or problem behind a request.
Positive & creative problem solving in the face of data challenges.
Flexibility to successfully multi-task in a fast-paced environment with a positive attitude.
Strong teamwork, solution-centric, and customer-service-oriented attitude.
Possess empathy, kindness towards others, a positive attitude, and self-awareness.
Have a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Math, Statistics, Business, or related field, or equivalent experience.


Candidates should be comfortable with an on-site presence to support collaboration, team leadership, and cross-functional partnership. 

Benefits and Perks:


Competitive compensation
Generous vacation policy, paid holidays, and paid sick time
Medical Insurance, Dental Insurance, and Vision Insurance (employee-paid)
Company-paid Short-Term and Long-Term Disability Insurance
Company-paid Group Life insurance
Company-paid Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and Calm App subscription
Employee-paid Pet Insurance and optional supplemental insurance coverage
Vested 401(k) with company match and financial wellness programs
Flexible Spending Account (FSA), Health Savings Account (HSA) and commuter benefits options
Paid maternity leave, paid paternity leave, and fertility benefits
Career growth and learning opportunities
…and so much more!

LTIMindtree is a global technology consulting and digital solutions company that enables enterprises across industries to reimagine business models, accelerate innovation, and maximize growth by harnessing digital technologies. As a digital transformation partner to more than 700+ clients, LTIMindtree brings extensive domain and technology expertise to help drive superior competitive differentiation, customer experiences, and business outcomes in a converging world. Powered by nearly 90,000 talented and entrepreneurial professionals across more than 30 countries, LTIMindtree — a Larsen & Toubro Group company — combines the industry-acclaimed strengths of erstwhile Larsen and Toubro Infotech and Mindtree in solving the most complex business challenges and delivering transformation at scale. Location: Charlotte NC

Duration: Fulltime




Job Description




Mandatory Skills

Key Responsibilities

Job Summary

Experience in Python for Data within the Data Analysis domain

Utilize expertlevel Python programming skills to develop maintain and optimize data processing and analysis solutions Design implement and enhance data workflows and pipelines to support comprehensive data analysis Work with large datasets to perform data cleaning transformation and extraction of actionable insights Collaborate with crossfunctional teams to integrate diverse data sources and improve data quality Apply best practices in coding testing and deployment to ensure scalable maintainable and efficient data solutions Stay up to date with emerging trends tools and techniques in Python programming and data analysis to continuously improve processes




Roles and Responsibilities

Lead the development and optimization of data analysis solutions using Python to support business objectives Drive data integration efforts ensuring consistency accuracy and reliability across multiple data sources Mentor and provide technical guidance to junior team members on Python programming and data analysis best practices Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements translate them into technical specifications and deliver effective data solutions Monitor and optimize the performance of data pipelines and scripts to enhance efficiency Implement robust data quality checks and troubleshoot datarelated issues promptly Participate actively in code reviews and contribute to improving coding standards and team knowledge sharing

Title: Data Analyst (PowerBI, Excel)
Description:
No Corp-to-corp. No 3rd party firms
.

Position: Data Analyst (Advanced Excel, PowerBI)
Location: 100% onsite in Irvine, CA (Only local candidates will be considered)
Terms: Direct-hire
Compensation: $35.00 to 42.00 per hour (DOE)
Benefits: Annual discretionary bonus + benefits + 3 weeks PTO
.

Partnered with a client who is hiring an experienced Data Analyst to support the customer experience team by delivering actionable insights that improve customer service performance, on-time delivery, and operational efficiency.
.

This is a unique, business-facing analytics role where you will combine technical expertise (Power BI, Excel, ERP, CRM systems) with strong business acumen and communication skills to influence decision-making.

.

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Insights

Analyze customer service metrics (order fulfillment, backlog, on-time delivery, queue performance, expedite requests)
Identify trends and translate findings into actionable recommendations
Ensure data integrity and validate sources

Reporting & Dashboards

Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports
Deliver ad hoc and recurring insights to leadership and cross-functional teams
Continuously improve reporting and identify gaps/opportunities

Operational Support

Monitor backlog and delivery risk; proactively flag issues and recommend mitigation
Partner with customer experience, sales, and operations teams
Understand end-to-end order flow from entry to reporting

Business Contribution

Fulfill reporting requests from leadership
Proactively identify new metrics, trends, and improvement opportunities
Recommend dashboards, enhancements, and process improvements

.
Required Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Analytics, Business, Finance, Supply Chain, or related field
3+ years in data analytics or similar role
Advanced Power BI and Excel skills
Experience with ERP systems (SAP preferred) and CRM platforms
Strong analytical, data validation, and problem-solving skills
Ability to clearly communicate insights and challenge assumptions
Background working with data in customer service, sales, or order management

.
.


All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, age, ancestry, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, pregnancy, or military or veteran status. We consider all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, in a manner consistent with state and local laws, including the California Fair Chance Act, City of Los Angeles' Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, and Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance.

Title: Analista de datos
Description:
¿Te apasiona la resolución de problemas complejos, analizar información y convertir los datos en decisiones de negocio?




En Bravo estamos en búsqueda de un Analista de datos con un alto potencial de desarrollo, pensamiento analítico y orientación a resultados, que quiera formar parte de un equipo dinámico y con grandes oportunidades de crecimiento.




¿A quién buscamos?

Profesionistas provenientes de carreras con un fuerte enfoque cuantitativo, como:

• Actuaría, Matemáticas, Ingeniería Industrial, Ingeniería en Sistemas , Ingeniería Financiera, Finanzas, Economía, Estadística, Física o carreras afines.




Lo que esperamos de ti

✔ Pensamiento analítico y estructurado.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The Houston-Galveston Area Council is one of the largest regional planning commissions in the country with a diverse service area of 13 counties and more than 7 million people. We are the pulse of our region addressing issues that cross city limits and county lines every single day.  

We make decisions that affect our transportation system, ensure the safety and well-being of our seniors, connect people to jobs, help families recover from natural disasters, preserve water quality for our children, and so much more. We work to make the region a great place to live, work, and thrive.

Job Duties


Support regional planning efforts through advanced land use modeling, data analysis, and forecasting.
Apply strong analytical and programming skills to evaluate demographic, economic, and geographic trends that inform long-term planning in transportation, land use, environmental sustainability, and economic development.
Apply advanced knowledge in economics, statistics, and data science to develop forecasting models and simulations that guide policy and project development.
Manage and analyze large datasets, build scenario-based models, and communicate findings through technical reports and visualizations, demonstrating excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Develop and maintain land use forecasting models using SAS and Python, incorporating time-series and spatial analysis techniques to support scenario planning.
Conduct simulations to evaluate future land use patterns based on socioeconomic trends and policy drivers.
Collect, clean, and validate demographic, economic, and geographic data from public and private sources.
Ensure transparency and reproducibility by maintaining data dictionaries and process documentation.
Perform quality assurance checks to ensure accuracy of datasets and models, produce technical outputs such as reports and dashboards, and support internal and external stakeholders in interpreting model results.
Respond to data requests related to population, employment, and land use from local governments, research institutions, consultants, and the public, and serve as the primary contact for external data inquiries.
Present technical concepts clearly to non-technical audience.


Key Qualifications

Do you have...


Minimum Education Requirement: Bachelor’s degree in Urban Planning, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, Data Science, or a related field.
Minimum Experience Requirement: 36 months of experience developing forecasting models, conducting statistical and spatial analysis, and managing complex datasets, including experience applying proficiency in SAS, and experience in land use modeling, simulation, or scenario analysis.


All employees of H-GAC are required to reside within the agency’s region of service to support our commitment to excellence in service of our region.

H-GAC is an equal opportunity/ADA employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, national origin, or protected veteran status.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Publicis is an omni-channel communications agency with over 600 employees across our Canadian operations. The office is the largest in our industry in Canada and boasts talent across various disciplines of marketing and advertising expertise. Publicis carries a balanced split of Canadian, U.S. and Global clients for which we are Agency of Record. Publicis supports a range of D&I actions through our Driving Change team as well as giving back to the community through a long-standing participation in the Out of the Cold Program. With a strong, active and familial culture, Pub United is the agency’s social club, hosting events as wide reaching as Curling, Trivia Nights and more.




Overview




We’re looking for a Junior Data Analyst to join our team and help bring data to life across our clients’ digital experiences. In this role, you won’t just report numbers - you’ll interrogate them, asking why a metric moved, what it means for the business, and what we should do about it.




You’ll support the implementation and optimization of web analytics solutions using Google Analytics 4 (GA4), turning data into insights that drive smarter decisions. You’ll monitor performance, uncover trends, and deliver meaningful recommendations on both a regular and ad-hoc basis. As you do so, you’ll ensure your data is accurate and reliable, while taking a strategic approach to uncover opportunities that drive the business forward.




Responsibilities




Provide technical expertise on Google Analytics 4 (GA4), Google Tag Manager (GTM), and other relevant digital analytics platforms.
Design and implement detailed tracking and tagging plans, partnering with development teams to ensure accurate and comprehensive tracking of user behaviour across client web properties.
Contribute to a range of analytics initiatives; including report automation, analytics tagging, data visualization, requirements gathering and documentation, and ad hoc analysis, proactively surfacing the “so what” behind the data
Assist in developing KPIs and measurement frameworks aligned to business objectives, asking the right questions during requirements gathering to ensure we’re measuring what matters
Identify opportunities to improve how we collect, integrate, and analyze data
Generate routine reporting, scorecards, and interactive dashboards (including weekly, monthly, quarterly and ad-hoc requests), while actively identifying & sharing key insights from the data
Collaborate cross-functionally with various teams across the organization and contribute to client-facing communication that builds strong, trusted relationships




Qualifications




About You




You’re curious by nature and excited about using data to improve experiences, performance and decision-making. You enjoy digging into data, asking questions, and finding insights that make an impact.




This might be the right fit if you:




Find yourself asking “but why?” when others accept the surface-level answer
Feel uncomfortable presenting data without having a point of view on what it means
Get a thrill from untangling confusing datasets or spotting new trends




You’ll succeed in this role if you:




Have exceptional time management skills & can juggle multiple priorities effectively
Are proactive, hard-working, and excited by digital possibilities, finding smart solutions to deliver results
Set high quality standards for yourself and care about getting the details right
Are accountable, and do what it takes to deliver on your commitments
Welcome feedback & continuously look for ways to grow




Qualifications:




1+year(s) of hands-on experience using Google Analytics 4
Basic understanding of HTML, CSS and JavaScript
Proficient in MS Excel for data analysis
Ability to design and build dashboards in Tableau, Looker Studio, PowerBI, or similar data visualization tools.
Strong written and verbal communication skills are essential. The ideal candidate should feel comfortable presenting and participating in group discussions involving both internal stakeholders and clients
Experience using Google Tag Manager and BigQuery is a plus




Additional information




Salary




Transparency matters to us. The salary range for this position is CAD $55,000 - CAD $65,000 per year. Actual compensation within this range will be based on a variety of factors, including relevant experience, knowledge, skills, and applicable certifications. This range reflects what we reasonably expect to offer based on current market data.




AI Use




We use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of our hiring process, such as reviewing applications or analyzing resumes. These tools assist our recruitment team but never replace human decision-making. We believe in a human-first approach, where your experience and potential are recognized by people.




Publicis Canada is committed to building a diverse workforce representative of our community. We encourage and are pleased to consider all qualified candidates, without regard to race, colour, citizenship, religion, sex, marital / family status, sexual orientation, gender identity, aboriginal status, age, disability or persons who may require an accommodation, to apply. If you require a specific accommodation please contact Human Resources.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

The Data Service Delivery, Securities Master File (SMF) team, is accountable and responsible for the accurate and timely daily collection, validation and dissemination of market data to support RBC Investor Services’ core products. The combination of our product suite and the downstream impact of our outputs create a unique and challenging function supporting RBC I&TS operational teams. As the Data Analyst, you will ensure that all securities are set up and delivered to our various applications in a timely and accurate manner in order to meet our clients’ requirements. You will be responsible for the quality of the production delivery and respect of the deadlines.

What will you do?


Provide a high level of service by responding to all client inquiries within a reasonable timeframe in order to maintain the company’s image and strengthen client retention.
Verify and confirm with vendor sources for any discrepancies in set up information.
Establish communication networks with all business units for the receipt of security set up data.
Set up various types of public and private securities (funds, bonds, money markets, etc.).
Ensure that the end of day checking procedure for any same day requests are completed; and, existing procedures are followed and kept informed on all policies and compliance issues.
Review/monitor all set up issues to ensure timely follow up and resolution
Act as a team player, participate in the team’s cross training activities, and provide coverage during staff absences.
Identify issues and problems and escalate immediately.


What do you need to succeed?

Must-have


University degree
3+ years financial services experience
Good knowledge of securities industry, PC and MS Excel skills
Strong analytical thinking and ability to work effectively and efficiently under pressure
Team player with strong verbal and written communications skills


Nice-to-have


Canadian Securities Course designation


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable.
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Ability to make a difference and lasting impact.
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team.
Flexible work/life balance options.
Opportunities to do challenging work.
Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities.
Access to a variety of job opportunities across business.


Job Skills

Active Learning, Communication, Critical Thinking, Customer Service, Operational Delivery, Process Improvements, Time Management

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Wilson Elser is a leading defense litigation law firm with more than 1400 attorneys in 46 offices throughout the United States. Founded in 1978, we rank among the top 100 law firms identified by The American Lawyer and 36 in the National Law Journal’s survey of the nation’s largest law firms. 

Our firm is committed to attracting and retaining professionals who value each other and the service we provide by embracing Teamwork, Collaboration, Client Service, and Innovation.  If you are a motivated professional looking for a long-term fit where you can grow in a role, and will be valued and empowered, then we invite you to apply to our Data Analyst position in our New York, NY Office.

This position offers a flexible, hybrid working arrangement.

The Position

This hands-on Data Analyst position will reside within the Firm’s Business Solutions & Innovations department, where 20+ years of legacy technology and business processes are undergoing a complex and strategic overhaul, focused on efficiency, sustainability and providing a competitive industry advantage.

Key Responsibilities:

Create and maintain reporting processes to support firm stakeholders
Develop an understanding and knowledge of databases supporting firm operation
Utilize Power BI Analysis Services for data visualization and reporting
Leverage Aderant Database & Software for financial systems reporting
Perform initial analysis and refinement of data
Effectively communicate with internal/external resources
Respond to ad hoc data requests
Effectively analyze, reverse engineer, and document legacy scripting routines

Qualifications

1-3 years of Experience in Financial Systems Administration
1-3 years of recent Experience with Aderant Financial System
Demonstrated Ability to Managing Data & Performing Data Analysis
Working Knowledge Power BI Analysis Services
MS SQL/SSRS/SQL Query skills required
Exceptional MS Excel understanding, ability to debug, and develop a must
VBA/Macro understanding and ability to debug are preferred skills 
Familiar with using JIRA software or comparable software for project and task management
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Multi-tasker with ability to successfully manage numerous projects and tasks concurrently
Strong oral communication skills
Ability to draft succinct, informative, and accurate reports and recommendations
Organizational skills to manage toward deadlines and deliverables
Experience leading the transition, integration, and implementation of legal e-billing platforms a plus
Knowledge of Sequel Viewpoint Systems a plus
Prior industry experience with mid to large sized Law Firm(s) a plus
Bachelor’s degree (preferably in a Computer Science or related field), or substantial industry experience/expertise 

A variety of factors are considered in making compensation decisions, including but not limited to experience, education, licensure and/or certifications, geographic location, market demands, other business and organizational needs, and other factors permitted by law. Final salary wages offered may be outside of this range based on other reasons and individual circumstances.  This position is considered full-time and therefore qualifies for benefits including 401(k) retirement savings plan, medical, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance. Details of participation in these benefit plans will be provided if an employee receives an offer of employment. 

Salary Range: : $80,000 USD - $90,000 USD

Why Should You Apply? 

Benefits: Outstanding benefits package, including 401k match and generous PTO plan
Career Growth: Ample opportunities for professional development and advancement

The Position

This hands-on Data Analyst position will reside within the Firm’s Business Solutions & Innovations department, where 20+ years of legacy technology and business processes are undergoing a complex and strategic overhaul, focused on efficiency, sustainability and providing a competitive industry advantage.

Title: Data Entry Clerk
Description:
About The Company

HVUD is dedicated to providing safe drinking water and is committed to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality. The organization values teamwork, safety consciousness, and a strong work ethic. HVUD strives to create a collaborative environment where employees are encouraged to contribute their skills and expertise to achieve the company's mission of delivering clean, safe water to communities. With a focus on employee development and safety, HVUD is an employer that prioritizes its workforce and the vital work they do.

About The Role

We are seeking a diligent and efficient remote Data Entry Clerk to join our team. This role involves accurately transcribing information, preparing documents, and ensuring timely submission of assignments. The ideal candidate will demonstrate meticulous attention to detail, possess strong organizational skills, and be capable of working independently in a remote setting. This position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals seeking flexible work arrangements while contributing to the operational efficiency of HVUD. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing various data entry tasks, maintaining records, and staying updated with relevant software tools to ensure accuracy and productivity.

Qualifications


High school diploma or equivalent
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook)
Previous experience in data entry or administrative roles is advantageous
Strong typing skills with high accuracy and speed
Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision
Own personal or laptop computer
Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace


Responsibilities


Gather and organize source material for data entry tasks
Type memorandums, reports, and other documents using prescribed word processing software
Prepare documents in accordance with specified formatting guidelines
Proofread assignments to identify and correct errors before submission
Submit completed work via email or postal services as required
File and store copies of completed assignments for record-keeping
Maintain an accurate record of tasks completed and log hours worked
Stay updated with any changes or updates in relevant software tools
Coordinate with team members to ensure deadlines are met and quality standards are maintained


Benefits


Flexible remote work environment
Opportunity to work independently and develop organizational skills
Potential for professional growth within the organization
Access to company resources and support for remote work
Competitive compensation based on experience and performance


Equal Opportunity

HVUD is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status. All qualified applicants are encouraged to apply.

Title: Pharmacy Data Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

This organization operates within the nonprofit healthcare sector, focusing on delivering health services through a large system of associates. It addresses the complex challenges of healthcare delivery by fostering a culture grounded in service, growth, care, and connection. The organization supports a workforce of over 97,000 associates and emphasizes inclusive and supportive environments, aiming to impact healthcare meaningfully.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Pharmacy Data Analyst role is designed to support quality improvement initiatives by collecting, analyzing, and interpreting complex data related to pharmacy operations. This position plays a crucial role in identifying strategic trends and providing data-driven recommendations to enhance service quality. The role also involves managing the scope and format of statistical reports and educating staff on accessing and interpreting data to support informed decision-making within the organization.




3. Responsibilities:

• Collect, analyze, and interpret complex data to identify strategic trends and support quality improvement.

• Define the scope and format for all statistical and analytical reports, including ad hoc requests.

• Ensure accuracy and reliability of all source data through regular review and reconciliation.

• Provide expertise and education to staff on accessing and interpreting report data.




4. Requirements:

• High school diploma equivalency with 2 years of cumulative experience, or an Associate''s or Bachelor''s degree, or 4 years of applicable cumulative job-specific experience.

• Experience as a 340B analyst.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Salary range: $65,954 - $90,683.37 per year.




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Paid time off (PTO).

• Various health insurance options and wellness plans.

• Retirement benefits including employer match plans.

• Long-term and short-term disability coverage.

• Employee assistance programs (EAP).

• Parental leave and adoption assistance.

• Tuition reimbursement.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About The Company

Government Employees Health Association, Inc. (G.E.H.A) is a distinguished nonprofit member organization dedicated to providing comprehensive health and dental benefits to millions of federal employees, retirees, military retirees, and their families since 1937. Recognized as one of the largest health and dental benefit providers in the United States, G.E.H.A offers an extensive network that ensures members receive quality care when they need it most. The organization’s core mission is to empower federal workers to achieve optimal health and wellness by delivering accessible, reliable, and member-centric benefits. Headquartered in Lee's Summit, Missouri, G.E.H.A emphasizes a culture of inclusivity, innovation, and service excellence, fostering an environment where employees can grow professionally while making a meaningful impact on the lives of its members.

About The Role

The Business Data Analyst at G.E.H.A will play a pivotal role in supporting the organization’s data-driven decision-making processes. This position involves identifying business problems, gathering detailed requirements, and designing data solutions that align with organizational goals and enterprise data strategies. The analyst will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, technical teams, and data architects to ensure that data solutions meet business needs, are compliant with regulatory standards, and withstand audit scrutiny. Responsibilities include creating comprehensive documentation of business rules, data workflows, and technical specifications, as well as supporting the development of data models and transformations within cloud data frameworks. The role requires active participation in Agile and Scrum practices, ensuring continuous improvement and adherence to company standards. The Business Data Analyst will also champion data governance, quality, and security initiatives, contributing to the organization’s commitment to trusted and accurate data management.

Qualifications


3-5 years of relevant experience in data analysis, business analysis, or related fields, preferably within the healthcare industry.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Healthcare Administration, Mathematics, or a related discipline; equivalent work experience may be considered.
Proficiency in Microsoft Azure Data tools or Snowflake Technologies.
Strong skills in Data Querying, Data Profiling, Data Transformation, and Reporting.
Experience in problem solving, root cause analysis, and troubleshooting data issues.
Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and Agile/Scrum methodologies.
Knowledge of compliance, security, and regulatory requirements, especially related to healthcare data.
Willingness to learn healthcare and analytics industries if not already familiar.
Preferred certifications in Azure, Snowflake, Databricks, or similar cloud platforms.
Ability to provide a dedicated home office space with high-speed internet (minimum 30x5 Mbps) and low latency.


Responsibilities


Review, analyze, and document detailed business rules and data needs to ensure clarity and completeness.
Facilitate discussions with stakeholders and technical teams to capture use cases, requirements, and technical specifications.
Collaborate with developers and architects to define and document data solutions that meet business needs.
Secure business/stakeholder approval for data solutions and ensure compliance with audit standards.
Document data ingestion, transformation workflows, data models, and sharing processes to maintain data integrity and trustworthiness.
Maintain up-to-date documentation reflecting current data states and unresolved issues.
Support the implementation of cloud data frameworks aligned with enterprise strategies and policies.
Validate that data and processes fulfill documented business rules and requirements.
Participate in Agile ceremonies such as sprint planning, backlog grooming, and reviews.
Present technical and business documentation to stakeholders and team members.
Ensure adherence to departmental standards for documentation and compliance for audit readiness.
Perform peer reviews of documentation and data models to promote quality and consistency.
Create data models based on solution decisions in collaboration with data architects and developers.
Perform other duties as assigned to support organizational data initiatives.


Benefits


Competitive salary range, with potential for growth based on performance.
Incentive plans to reward contributions and achievements.
Health, Vision, and Dental insurance coverage effective from day one.
401(k) retirement plan with dollar-for-dollar company match up to 4% of employee contributions and a 6% annual company contribution.
Robust employee well-being programs promoting health and work-life balance.
Paid Time Off and Personal Community Enrichment Time to support personal and professional growth.
Company-provided Basic Life and AD&D insurance, along with Short-Term and Long-Term Disability coverage.
Tuition Assistance Program to support ongoing education and professional development.


Equal Opportunity

G.E.H.A is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to fostering an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate against any individual based on sex, race, color, national origin, disability, religion, age, military status, genetic information, veteran status, pregnancy, marital status, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. We value diversity and are dedicated to providing equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants and employees. G.E.H.A ensures a workplace free from discrimination and harassment and encourages applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply.

Title: Data Analyst, User Analytics & Insights
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates within the data analytics and user insights space, addressing the challenge of extracting actionable insights from user behavior and business performance data. It supports cross-functional teams by providing data-driven analysis to improve decision-making in areas such as marketing and product development. The company partners with teams including Product, Marketing, Growth, Engineering, and Business to measure performance, support experimentation, and track progress against key goals.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Data Analyst, User Analytics & Insights is responsible for enabling data-informed decisions by analyzing user behavior and business performance. This role supports either Marketing or Apps business areas by partnering with multiple teams to translate data into insights, develop reporting and dashboards, and guide experimentation efforts. The position contributes to the organization by delivering clear, actionable analysis that helps teams understand trends, test hypotheses, and improve outcomes.




3. Responsibilities:

• Collaborate with Product, Marketing, Growth, Engineering, and Business teams to analyze user engagement, conversion, retention, and performance metrics.

• Translate business questions into analytical plans using SQL, visualization, and statistical methods.

• Perform routine and ad hoc data analysis to identify trends and improvement opportunities.

• Communicate findings and recommendations to technical and non-technical audiences.

• Build and maintain dashboards and self-service reports for tracking key metrics.

• Monitor core KPIs and investigate data quality or trend changes.

• Partner with Analytics and Data Engineering to enhance data quality and reporting accuracy.

• Support experiment setup, including hypothesis development, metric selection, and sample sizing.

• Analyze A/B test results, including segment-level performance and statistical significance.

• Create and maintain user segments and cohorts to support analysis, experiments, and reporting.

• Assist in audience creation for marketing campaigns or app feature adoption analysis.




4. Requirements:

• 2+ years of experience in analytics, data analysis, or related fields.

• Proficiency in SQL for data manipulation and reporting.

• Experience with dashboard and visualization tools such as Hex, Tableau, Looker, Grafana, or Mode.

• Knowledge of A/B testing concepts including hypothesis formulation, metric selection, and result interpretation.

• Experience creating user segments or cohorts for analysis and targeting.

• Ability to analyze funnels, retention, engagement, conversion, and campaign or app metrics.

• Strong attention to detail and data quality awareness.

• Effective communication skills for diverse audiences.

• Ability to manage analytical requests independently and escalate issues appropriately.

• Comfort working in fast-paced environments with evolving priorities.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Virtual Assistant - Remote
Description:
About The Company

Decentralized Masters is a pioneering organization at the forefront of DeFi education globally. Established just two years ago, the company has experienced rapid growth from a small team of co-founders to over 150 dedicated professionals. Recognized as one of the fastest-growing enterprises in the DeFi sector, Decentralized Masters is poised for continued success, with industry insiders projecting its evolution into a unicorn company by 2030. Operating on a bootstrapped model, the company is on track to generate an impressive $50 million in revenue this year alone. Its mission is to empower individuals with the knowledge and tools necessary to succeed in the rapidly evolving world of decentralized finance and Web3 technology.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly organized and professional Virtual Assistant to join our dynamic team. This role is vital in supporting our operational efficiency and ensuring seamless communication with our clients and internal teams. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing emails and communications, escalating customer queries appropriately, drafting reports and presentations, and maintaining clear and continuous communication with clients. Additionally, the Virtual Assistant will handle general administrative duties, facilitate customer onboarding, and assist in scheduling and calendar management. This position offers an exciting opportunity to be part of a rapidly growing company that is shaping the future of DeFi education and Web3 innovation. The successful candidate will work remotely from South America, contributing to a global team committed to excellence and innovation.

Qualifications


High school diploma; additional qualifications as an Administrator or Executive Assistant are a plus
Proven experience as a Virtual Assistant or in a relevant administrative role
Customer Service experience is mandatory
Native English speaker or at least a professional level of proficiency in English
Must be located in South America
Excellent experience with word-processing software and spreadsheets (e.g., Google Suite, MS Excel)
Knowledge of online calendars and scheduling tools (e.g., Google Calendar)
Previous experience working with Circle is an advantage
Familiarity with DeFi or cryptocurrency concepts is an added advantage
Excellent communication skills via phone, email, and instant messaging
Strong time management and organizational skills

Title: Junior Data Analyst (Manufacturing)
Description:
Company Information

Elbit America is a leading provider of high-performance products, system solutions, and support services focusing on the defense, homeland security, commercial aviation, and medical instrumentation markets. With facilities throughout the United States, Elbit Systems of America is dedicated to supporting those who contribute daily to the safety and security of the United States. Elbit Systems of America, LLC is wholly owned by Elbit Systems Ltd. (NASDAQ: ESLT and TASE: ESLT), a global high-technology company engaged in a wide range of programs for innovative defense and commercial applications. For additional information, visit: ElbitAmerica.com or follow us on YouTube.

Fueled by more than 800 talented employees and a proud 65-year legacy of night vision production excellence, Elbit America’s Warfighter Systems business is rooted in the heart of Virginia’s Roanoke Valley. Surrounded by the scenic beauty of the Blue Ridge Mountains, you’ll find a unique blend of southern hospitality, outdoor adventure, and the energy of a thriving city – all alongside a purpose you can be proud of coming to work each day.

As a world leader in low-light sensor technology, the Warfighter Systems team is engineering the future of image intensifiers and night vision goggle systems as we enter a new era of the networked battlespace. Trusted by the U.S. and nearly 100 allied countries, our technology provides support to those in harm's way during the most critical moments with one goal in mind: bring them home safely.

The work our employees perform each day plays a direct role in national security, and our shared purpose is what truly makes Warfighter Systems so extraordinary. Come join our collaborative team where each voice is valued in a setting where you can thrive, develop, and make a true impact!

Job Summary

The Data Analyst I is responsible for supporting data-driven decision-making by gathering, analyzing, and interpreting data to deliver actionable insights for business stakeholders. This role involves routine data management tasks and basic analytical assignments under the supervision of more experienced analysts or managers. As a foundational role, it offers exposure to best practices in data analysis and contributes to continuous improvement efforts within the organization.

Responsibilities And Tasks


Collect, clean, and validate data from various sources to ensure accuracy and reliability.
Conduct basic data analysis using statistical methods and tools to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Prepare reports, dashboards, and visualizations that communicate findings to internal stakeholders.
Support data quality initiatives by identifying discrepancies and assisting with data remediation tasks
Maintain documentation of analysis procedures, data sources, and results for knowledge sharing and compliance.
Adhere to established data governance policies and confidentiality requirements
Directly support high-priority LEAN and Lean Six Sigma (LSS) projects following Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control (DMAIC) through collection, processing, and analysis (including trends, patterns) of project data
Support collection, processing, and analysis of project data with supervision in support of meeting project deadlines and critical business needs.
Support analysis of proposed projects and continuous improvement efforts to determine cost-benefit and return on investment


Education, Experience/Knowledge & License/Certification


Bachelor’s degree in mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, Business, or a related field.
0-2 years of experience in a data analyst or similar role preferred.
Lean Six Sigma green belt is preferred
Ability to obtain and maintain a U.S. security clearance


Skills And Abilities


Fundamental understanding of data analysis concepts and statistical techniques.
Proficiency in Excel and basic experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau).
Familiarity with querying databases (e.g., SQL) is a plus.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
Effective communication skills and ability to present information clearly.
Problem-solving mindset with willingness to learn and adapt.


#onsite

Why Roanoke, Virginia?


Lower cost of living
Family-friendly area
Career and economic growth
Scenic beauty
Outdoor enthusiast’s dream


Here Are Some Of The Great Benefits We Offer


Most locations offer a 9/80 schedule, providing every other Friday off
Competitive compensation & 401(k) program to plan for your future
Robust medical, dental, vision, & disability coverage with qualified wellness discounts
Basic Life Insurance and Additional Life & AD&D Insurances are available
Flexible Vacation & PTO
Paid Parental Leave
Generous Employee Referral Program
Voluntary Benefits Available: Longer Term Care, Legal, Identity Theft, Pet Insurance, and more
Voluntary Tricare Supplement available for military retirees

Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions of this position. Elbit America is an equal opportunity employer as to all protected groups, including protected veterans and individuals with disabilities.

Title: Administrative Assistant (Remote)
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates in the online auction and liquidation industry, focusing on the sale of consumer goods, electronics, and surplus inventory. It addresses the challenge of providing a streamlined digital bidding experience for customers, facilitating efficient asset liquidation and resale through an online platform.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Administrative Assistant role is essential to maintaining smooth daily business operations in a remote, fast-paced environment. This position supports the administrative needs of the team by managing communication, scheduling, and documentation, contributing to the overall efficiency and effectiveness of the organization.




3. Responsibilities:

• Provide comprehensive administrative support to ensure efficient office operations.

• Manage and respond to emails, correspondence, and customer service inquiries professionally and promptly.

• Schedule meetings, manage calendars, and coordinate appointments for senior staff.

• Assist in preparing regular reports, presentations, and documents.

• Perform data entry and maintain accurate digital records related to auctions, sales, and customer information.

• Liaise with internal teams to handle requests and queries.

• Organize and maintain digital filing systems for easy information access.

• Support auction and liquidation processes by assisting with listing coordination and post-sale administration.




4. Requirements:

• Proven experience as an administrative assistant or in office support.

• Exceptional organizational skills with effective task prioritization and time management.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills with professionalism.

• Proficient in MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) or equivalent G Suite applications.

• Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

• Ability to work independently and proactively in a remote setting.

• Experience in e-commerce, online retail, or auction environments is desirable but not required.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Competitive hourly wage of $25 - $39 USD, commensurate with experience.

• Fully remote working arrangement.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Global Data Coordinator (Hybrid)
Description:
RRD is a leading global provider of marketing, packaging, print, and supply chain solutions that elevate engagement across the complete customer journey. The company offers the industry’s most trusted portfolio of creative execution and world-wide business process consulting, with services designed to lower environmental impact. With 22,000 clients, including 93% of the Fortune 100, and 32,000 employees across 28 countries, RRD brings the expertise, execution, and scale designed to transform customer touchpoints into meaningful moments of impact.





Job Description



RR Donnelley is adding a Global Data Coordinator to their team!



This is a Hybrid role requiring 3 days in office, 2 days remote.



The Global Data Coordinator supports records management operations and special projects team by compiling, validating, analyzing, and reporting data that informs day-to-day decision-making and operational performance. This role works across multiple data sources to ensure accuracy, identify discrepancies, and deliver clear, actionable reporting using Excel and other reporting tools. The position also supports records-related operational processes, helps maintain data integrity and inventory accuracy, and contributes to process improvements that enhance reporting quality and team efficiency.



Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:



Full department backup and knowledge base of data operations
Support operational and special project data reporting and technology needs e.g. test cases creations, report findings/discrepancies
Support user acceptance testing of technology
Generate and analyze reporting for clients and internal procedural requirements
Interpret data, analyze results using statistical techniques and provide ongoing reports
Compare data from multiple sites, target inconsistencies and offer proposals for resolution
Interpret data, analyze results and provide ongoing reporting deliverables

Provide operational feedback on workflow including recommendations for efficiency opportunities



Review procedures as they relate to current processes and track updates as they evolve



Respond directly to clients and stakeholders regarding reporting requirements and to related questions as directed



Track and complete projects within scope by the agreed upon deadlines





Qualifications


Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, Data Analytics, or a related field preferred

3-5 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or a related operations support role



Strong Excel skills, including formulas, lookups, sorting, filtering, PivotTables, and charting



Experience using tools to prepare recurring and ad hoc reports for business stakeholders



Ability to compile, analyze, and present data accurately and clearly, with strong attention to detail



Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to explain findings to non-technical audiences



Ability to identify trends, discrepancies, and process improvement opportunities



Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines



Professional, collaborative approach working effectively across teams and with stakeholders



Experience in records management, inventory controls, or operational processes is a plus





Additional Information



The salary range for this role at the noted RRD location is $52,500-76,500/year. Starting pay decisions are determined based on multiple factors including but not limited to relevant education, qualifications, skills, experience, certifications, proficiency, performance, shift, location, and other business needs. Typically, roles follow step progressions to a target rate or set increments over time. Depending on the role, in addition to the hourly rate of pay, the total compensation package may also include overtime, shift differential, call-in, and/or stand-by pay. RRD’s benefit offerings include medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid time off, disability insurance, 401(k) with company match, life insurance and other voluntary supplemental insurance coverages, plus parental leave, adoption assistance, tuition assistance and employer/partner discounts.



#WLNAT











All employment offers are contingent upon the successful completion of both a pre-employment background and drug screen.






RRD is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including disability/veterans

Title: Senior Executive
Description:
Company Description San Diego Padres Store is an apparel and fashion retailer based in Encinitas, California, specializing in merchandise for fans of the San Diego Padres. Located at 1042 N El Camino Real, the store offers a range of team-branded clothing and accessories designed to enhance the fan experience. The company focuses on quality products, customer service, and a strong connection to the local sports community. Team members contribute to a culture that values collaboration, integrity, and a passion for sports and retail.
Role Description The Senior Executive role is a full-time, remote position responsible for leading strategic and operational initiatives for the San Diego Padres Store. This role oversees business planning, sales performance, and brand development, ensuring alignment with the company’s growth objectives. Day-to-day responsibilities include setting goals, managing budgets, analyzing performance metrics, and guiding cross-functional teams in merchandising, marketing, and customer experience. The Senior Executive collaborates with leadership to identify opportunities for expansion, improve operational efficiency, and strengthen the store’s presence across digital and physical channels. This position also involves building partnerships, supporting talent development, and maintaining high standards of ethical and inclusive leadership.
Qualifications

Demonstrated leadership experience in retail, apparel, fashion, or a related consumer-facing industry.
Strong strategic planning and business management skills, including budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis.
Experience overseeing merchandising, inventory, and pricing strategies to drive sales and profitability.
Background in brand management, marketing, or e-commerce, with the ability to support digital and in-store growth.
Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills, with a collaborative working style.
Proven ability to lead remote teams, set clear priorities, and make data-informed decisions.
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Management, Marketing, or a related field; advanced degree or equivalent experience is beneficial.
Commitment to inclusive leadership, ethical business practices, and continuous improvement.
Knowledge of sports merchandising or fan-based retail environments is an advantage.

Title: Data Entry Specialist
Description:
Job Description

Location: Wilmington, DE

In this office-based position you’ll experience a supportive approach to your wellbeing and continuous learning opportunities in our friendly and inclusive workplace. Find out more at computershare.com/flex.

We give you a world of potential

Our Document Services team plays a critical role in supporting our corporate clients by acting as a statutory agent for service of process both nationwide and internationally. The team is responsible for receiving, imaging, processing, and forwarding legal documents and government notices, as well as managing incoming and outgoing mail and other administrative functions. In this role, you’ll ensure documents are processed accurately and on time, helping mitigate legal and service‑level risks while supporting strong client relationships.

‌

If you’re passionate about delivering high‑quality service, thrive in a fast‑paced, detail‑driven environment, and are eager to be part of a collaborative and globally diverse organization, this could be an excellent next step in your career.

A role you will love

We are a global business with an entrepreneurial spirit, and we are proud of that. What that comes with this is a fast-paced environment and lots of change so you will be resilient in nature and able to adapt quickly and embrace the pace of change we often work at.

As a detail‑driven professional with strong organizational and problem‑solving skills, you will serve as a key point of control within our Document Services operation, helping ensure accuracy, timeliness, and consistency in how documents are processed on behalf of our clients. You will be responsible for receiving, processing, and sending legal and government documents on behalf of our clients, and making sure the information is accurate and completed on time.

Some Of Your Key Responsibilities Will Also Include


Prioritizing incoming service of process and government documents based on urgency, client‑specific requirements, and deadlines.
Performing pre‑send and post‑send quality checks to ensure accurate data entry, routing, and document imaging.
Identifying and resolving routine exceptions, escalating more complex issues in line with established procedures.
Preparing daily end‑of‑day summaries and maintaining basic workflow and quality dashboards.
Supporting team leaders by identifying recurring errors or bottlenecks and contributing improvement ideas.
Assisting with cross‑location workload balancing and backfilling processing or mailroom tasks as needed to meet service‑level.


What will you bring to the role?

We’re looking for a highly detail‑oriented professional who thrives in a fast‑paced environment and takes pride in delivering accurate, high‑quality work. You’ll bring a strong sense of ownership, excellent time management skills, and the ability to prioritize effectively while working collaboratively with others.

Other Key Skills Required For The Role Include


A degree is not required; relevant industry experience will be strongly considered
Exceptional attention to detail with accurate data entry skills.
Experience handling high volumes of documents in a data entry or processing environment.
Strong computer skills, including typing, scanning, email, and internet research.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Self‑motivated with strong organizational and time‑management abilities
Ability to work efficiently under deadlines in a fast‑paced office setting

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates at the vibrant forefront of the game development industry, focusing exclusively on creating deeply immersive, high-fidelity video games. The studio directly addresses the core industry challenge of producing compelling, player-centric titles by fostering an intensely collaborative ecosystem that empowers creative freedom and decentralizes artistic ownership among its development teams. Built for modern creators, the enterprise fully supports highly flexible work arrangements—including in-studio presence, dynamic hybrid setups, and fully remote options tailored for top-tier talent distributed across North America.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Senior Data Analyst will step into a highly critical, cross-functional role designed to serve as the structural bridge between core software engineering, telemetry analytics, and executive studio strategy. This position is responsible for driving data-informed game design, converting complex player behavioral telemetry into actionable insights that directly influence real-time game systems, monetization models, and long-term business decisions. Serving as the primary steward of the studio's data ecosystem, this role ensures that data acquisition infrastructure and advanced analytical modeling align perfectly with overarching game launch milestones and organizational growth goals.




3. Responsibilities:

• Take complete operational ownership of the end-to-end data lifecycle, managing everything from initial game event instrumentation and telemetry collection to final executive-level presentation

• Partner closely with multidisciplinary game departments and senior studio leadership to architect predictive data collection protocols and long-term analysis strategies

• Define, model, and track core player performance indicators (KPIs)—specifically targeting player engagement, long-term retention curves, and churn predictive modeling—and build the data systems to capture them

• Architect and maintain robust database schemas, continuously optimizing query performance, execution plans, and overall data warehouse health

• Develop, scale, and maintain performant ETL/ELT data pipelines and the underlying infrastructure required to handle high-velocity telemetry streams

• Automate operational reporting workflows and build intuitive, interactive visualization dashboards tailored precisely for diverse technical and creative game teams

• Play and engage with the studio's game titles directly to gain deep contextual familiarity, blending personal gameplay experience with quantitative analysis to inform design recommendations




4. Requirements:

• Minimum of five (5) years of progressive, professional experience in data analysis, analytics engineering, or business intelligence (preferably within the gaming, mobile apps, or high-transaction consumer entertainment sectors)

• Expert-level proficiency in SQL, demonstrating advanced capabilities in complex querying, scalable schema design, indexed views, and query execution performance tuning

• Strong programming skills in languages critical to backend automation and quantitative analysis, with specialized mastery in Python, R, or Java

• Proven history designing, building, orchestrating, and troubleshooting complex ETL processes across modern data warehouses

• Solid mathematical foundations and a deep understanding of statistical modeling techniques, including regression analysis, A/B testing, hypothesis validation, and predictive forecasting algorithms

• Exceptional communication and narrative skills, with a proven ability to translate complex technical metrics, statistical margins, and data anomalies clearly to creative directors and non-technical stakeholders

• Highly self-motivated, mission-driven mindset characterized by the ability to manage competing project priorities independently across cross-functional studio teams




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Target baseline salary framework: $100,000 to $140,000 USD annually

• Final base salary placement within this competitive tier is calibrated dynamically based upon the candidate's core analytics depth, engineering capabilities, geographic location, and specialized database proficiencies

• Total rewards portfolio features a highly lucrative baseline pay structure paired with an expansive enterprise-level benefits package and career trajectory programs




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Comprehensive healthcare security featuring 100% employer-covered Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance plans for employees and their immediate families

• Secure future financial planning backed by a corporate Retirement Savings Plan complete with competitive matching contributions

• Dedicated Career Improvement Plans offering structured corporate resources, training, and financial backing for continuous professional development

• Supreme geographic flexibility through fully supported flexible work arrangements (in-house studio spaces, hybrid schedules, or 100% remote setups across North America)

• A generous, refreshing Time Off Policy promoting true work-life balance and creative recharge

• Active community culture supported by vibrant company events held regularly across both in-person and virtual spaces

• Premium on-site amenities, including a fully stocked kitchen, curated snacks, and company-sponsored local food truck visits




7. Equal Opportunity Statement:

Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Finance Analyst
Description:
Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: New York, NY, USA; Mountain View, CA, USA.Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Statistics, or another quantitative field, or equivalent practical experience.
7 years of experience in financial planning and analysis (FP&A), consulting, or a related function, or an advanced degree.
Experience executing full-cycle FP&A functions including budgeting, forecasting, variance analysis, and modeling within a multinational company setting.
Experience with programming (i.e. SQL).


Preferred qualifications:


Experience writing SQL queries and retrieving data from relational databases.
Ability to self-start and self-directed work in an unstructured environment; comfortable dealing with ambiguity and a fast-paced environment
Ability to plan and manage at both the strategic and operational level.
Ability to create and foster strong cross-functional networks and manage without authority; excellent leadership, influencing and communication skills.
Excellent problem-solving and analysis skills, combined with impeccable business judgment and the ability to communicate and partner with a executive management team.
Excellent spreadsheet skills with experience creating financial dashboards or models.


About the jobThe Media Incentives team ensures that Google makes sound financial decisions for Media Incentives programs. As a Financial Analyst, you will analyze modeling investment scenarios and track performance metrics used by business leaders to make strategic decisions.Individual pay is determined by factors including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

US: $141000 - $206000 (USD) + 15% bonus target + equity + benefits

Responsibilities

Learn more about benefits at Google .


Provide thoughtful leadership, drive insights and analysis to support the Media Incentives team in managing the financial performance of volume-based incentives programs targeting video growth.
Leverage technical expertise to provide quantitative analysis around incentives programs, including support for return on investment and incrementality studies, working in partnership with other teams.
Interact with other Finance, Product, and partner teams to assure strategic and analytical alignment, as well as data consistency and efficiency.
Support regional Finance teams across the globe in crafting incentive portfolios aligned to their business goals.
Support product financial planning and analysis (FP&A) process by forecasting profit and loss (P&L) impact of incentive programs on an ongoing basis.
