Title: Business Intelligence Developer
Description:
Ibotta is looking for a Business Intelligence Developer to join our innovative team and contribute to our mission to Make Every Purchase Rewarding. On our Marketing Analytics team, this person will help empower our Marketing departments to make data-driven decisions by providing the right data at the right time throughout various areas of the organization. You will become a “go to” expert on all things data while working closely with internal stakeholders to support various business initiatives.

This position is located in Denver, Colorado as a hybrid position requiring 3 days in office (Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday). Candidates must live in the United States.

Not based in Denver? We will offer a relocation bonus to help make your move to the Mile High City a smooth one.

What You’ll Be Doing


Provide Reporting and Dashboarding that monitor, track, and analyze business performance and results
Analyze teams reporting and data requirements and architect appropriate solutions, with detailed design documents
Leverage reporting best practices to automate reports and find creative solutions to maximize reporting output
Create and refine visual representation and communication of complex data
Develop a logical, structured approach to conduct ad-hoc analyses and work backwards from hypotheses
Design, develop, maintain, and optimize ETL and pipelines related to Business Intelligence
Sample projects:
Extract, wrangle, and automate large data transfers (on the order of trillions of rows) to democratize data and empower data-driven decision making across the company
Manage, monitor, and provide recommendations on the quality of various data streams
Design and create aggregate and summary tables for reporting efficiency purposes
Analyze and deliver data to provide actionable insights about the effectiveness of the business above and beyond standard analysis
Embrace and uphold Ibotta’s Core Values: Integrity, Boldness, Ownership, Teamwork, Transparency & A good idea can come from anywhere

What We Are Looking For


1+ years of experience in Analytics within an agile organization as well as familiarity with data warehousing and business intelligence platforms
2-5+ years of practical work experience in SQL
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering or a related field or equivalent work experience
Proven ability to develop, maintain, automate, visualize, and communicate reporting for management and internal clients
Experience in end to end BI tool creation, including working closely with business context and stakeholders to outline how business intelligence can inform decision making
Ability to clearly and professionally communicate data, processes, and results to senior leadership
Follow through to complete projects through to end user acceptance and proper documentation for robust and easy to maintain end products
Excellent oral and written communication skills
Detailed oriented team player with an ownership mindset


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with Looker Business Intelligence platform
Experience using Databricks
Experience with business and marketing concepts
Knowledge of pipeline orchestration tools, Github, Python
Experience with event driven architecture


About Ibotta ("I bought a...")

Ibotta (NYSE: IBTA) is a leading performance marketing platform allowing brands to deliver digital promotions to over 200 million consumers through a network of publishers called the Ibotta Performance Network (IPN). The IPN allows marketers to influence what people buy, and where and how often they shop – all while paying only when their campaigns directly result in a sale. American shoppers have earned over $2.6 billion through the IPN since 2012. The largest tech IPO in history to come out of Colorado, Ibotta is headquartered in Denver, and is continually listed as a top place to work by The Denver Post and Inc. Magazine.

To learn more about what our Tech teams are doing day to day, visit Building Ibotta on Medium.com.

Additional Details


This position is located in Denver, CO and includes competitive pay, flexible time off, benefits package (including medical, dental, vision), Employee Stock Purchase Program, and 401k match. Denver office perks include paid parking, snacks, and occasional meals.
Base compensation range: $89,000 - $103,000. Equity is included in overall compensation package. This compensation range is specific to the United States labor market and may be adjusted based on actual experience.
Ibotta is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Ibotta’s employment decisions are made without regard of race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected status.
Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.
Applicants are accepted until the position is filled.
For the security of our employees and the business, all employees are responsible for the secure handling of data in accordance with our security policies, identifying and reporting phishing attempts, as well as reporting security incidents to the proper channels.


Recruiting Agency Notice

Ibotta does not accept agency resumes and is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes. Please do not forward resumes to any Ibotta employees.

Title: Administrative Assistant (Remote – Work From Home | Full-Time / Part-Time)
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates at the forefront of the renewable energy sector, specializing in the design, scaling, and deployment of advanced green hydrogen solutions. The company directly addresses critical global sustainability challenges by engineering infrastructure that reduces production costs and maximizes thermodynamic efficiency, delivering measurable value across the clean energy ecosystem. Driven by a vision of a zero-emission future, the enterprise fosters a forward-thinking, collaborative environment dedicated to accelerating the global transition toward sustainable power.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Administrative Assistant will step into a highly organized, vital support role responsible for managing daily operational workflows, executive correspondence, and documentation pipelines to ensure smooth and efficient functioning across the enterprise. This position serves as a central hub of operational support, enabling seamless cross-departmental coordination and keeping remote teams aligned. Offering excellent flexibility, this remote position accommodates either full-time or part-time scheduling options, making it ideal for a highly organized professional looking to contribute to a mission-driven clean energy company.




3. Responsibilities:

• Manage, maintain, and streamline complex calendars, seamlessly scheduling virtual meetings and corporate appointments across multiple global time zones

• Oversee professional corporate correspondence, monitoring central inboxes and responding to high-priority emails and internal communications promptly and accurately

• Draft, format, edit, and proofread technical documents, operational reports, and executive presentations to an exceptionally high standard of precision

• Organize, catalog, and maintain secure digital filing structures, cloud repositories, and document management systems to ensure data security and instant accessibility

• Execute precise data entry tasks and systematically maintain internal company databases, client registries, and operational logs

• Provide comprehensive, proactive administrative support to team members, project leads, and specialized departments as needed

• Assist in tracking milestones and coordinating virtual team projects, corporate initiatives, and cross-functional team-building schedules




4. Requirements:

• Proven, successful experience operating as an Administrative Assistant, Virtual Assistant, or within a similar administrative support capacity

• Exceptional organizational and time-management capabilities, with a demonstrated ability to prioritize competing tasks independently in a remote setting

• Strong written and verbal communication skills paired with a meticulous, sharp attention to visual and textual detail

• A proactive, highly resourceful problem-solver who takes individual ownership of tasks and operates effectively with minimal supervision

Title: Intelligence Engineer Intern
Description:
Intelligence Engineer Intern


Company: LuMATH

Position Type: Internship

Compensation: Unpaid Internship

Duration: 5 months

Schedule: Flexible

Location: Remote / Flexible




About the Role

LuMATH is looking for an Intelligence Engineer to help build Nova, our next-generation intelligence architecture.

This role is for someone who knows Python and wants to work on real systems that support reasoning, memory, testing, debugging, code improvement, and AI-assisted development.

This is not a normal software internship. You will work directly on Nova and use an AI coding agent to help build code, write tests, find bugs, improve structure, and move faster as a developer.

We are looking for people who can stay involved for approximately 5 months so they have enough time to learn the system, contribute meaningfully, and grow with the project.




About Nova

Nova is LuMATH’s intelligence system. It is designed to go beyond a traditional chatbot by focusing on relationships, constraints, consistency, memory, reasoning, and structural understanding.

Nova is being built as a modular Python-based architecture that helps organize complex information, support better decisions, test ideas, improve code, and work alongside humans and AI agents.

As an Intelligence Engineer, you will help build and improve the systems that allow Nova to reason, check work, organize knowledge, and support development.




What You Will Work On

You may help with:

Python development

Testing and debugging

Unit test creation

Code cleanup and organization

AI-assisted coding workflows

Nova system components

Memory and reasoning systems

Documentation

Automation

Software architecture

Working with an AI coding agent to build and test code




Requirements

Applicants must have:

At least 1 year of Python experience

Created at least one working software project, tool, app, automation, script, prototype, or system that did not previously exist

Ability to write understandable Python code

Interest in AI systems, intelligent tools, software architecture, automation, or agent-based development

Willingness to learn quickly and work carefully

Ability to stay involved for approximately 5 months




Ideal Candidate

This role is a strong fit for someone who:

Has already built something real

Likes difficult problems

Can think independently

Enjoys testing and improving systems

Wants to work with AI coding agents

Is excited by the idea of helping build a new type of intelligence system

Can handle early-stage startup work where the path is not always obvious




Internship Compensation and Future Opportunity

This is an unpaid internship.

LuMATH is looking for people who want to grow with the company. Interns who successfully complete the full 5-month internship will be eligible for a post-graduation employment opportunity with LuMATH.

Our intention is to offer strong-performing interns a future role that includes an equity package with an intended minimum value of $250,000, subject to company approval, role fit, vesting terms, final documentation, and applicable legal requirements.

Title: Analytics AI Strategy and Operations, YouTube
Description:
Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
15 years of experience in management consulting, product management and strategy, or analytics in a technology company.
Experience working with and analyzing data, and managing multiple cross-functional programs or projects.
Experience leading AI products from conception to launch.


Preferred qualifications:


Advanced degree or equivalent practical experience.
Experience with R, SQL, spreadsheets, or similar analytics tools.
Experience managing operations or product strategy for platforms with a user base of millions or billions.
Experience with concurrency systems and global data governance.


About The Job

Product and Business Strategy Leaders bring together teams across Google’s functions to help products execute optimally. Our team pushes Google to scale at key points that refine our products and infrastructure by executing efficiently, bringing solid business sense and sound judgment, and working effectively across organizational lines.

Our roles often include components of strategy (e.g. analyzing and understanding new trends in the industry, building business plans), operations (e.g. running the cadence of organizations, connecting the operating lines between our functions), and communications. Our team partners with senior leadership to run important functions that cross-cut our existing organizations and deliver high impact projects. We help Engineers, PMs, UX, and all of our other functions to build amazing products that delight our users, and then get those products into their hands.

YouTube’s ecosystem is constantly growing and evolving, and it is critical to deploy resources efficiently to best serve our users, creators, and advertisers globally. Our Product Strategy and Operations Team (“ProdOps”) partners with leaders to use data to run our business and drive decisions.

In this role, you will work with industry leaders to build and roll out AI-enabled analytical solutions across the organization at the confluence of product, engineering, and strategy. You will frequently interface with senior leaders to lead through AI-powered analytical excellence and transform how we work.

At YouTube, we believe that everyone deserves to have a voice, and that the world is a better place when we listen, share, and build community through our stories. We work together to give everyone the power to share their story, explore what they love, and connect with one another in the process. Working at the intersection of cutting-edge technology and boundless creativity, we move at the speed of culture with a shared goal to show people the world. We explore new ideas, solve real problems, and have fun — and we do it all together.

Individual pay is determined by factors including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

US: $222000 - $309000 (USD) + 25% bonus target + equity + benefits

Responsibilities

Learn more about benefits at Google .


Develop AI tools and infrastructure to make analytics more efficient by scaling solutions for key analytical and operational workflows.
Deploy AI solutions at scale across analytics and product teams, leading the adoption of AI-assisted SQL and coding solutions.
Lead coordination with other analytics functions to ensure YouTube teams focus AI efforts where they have the most impact.
Oversee expansion of AI-assisted platforms to rapidly diagnose metric changes and correlate movements with product or policy changes.
Train and mentor the team in AI by executing change management strategies and establishing frameworks for documenting insights.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

Title: Technical AIP Data Analyst
Description:
Founded in 2011, Altenar Technology Limited is a leading igaming software provider inspired by the vision of our customers. As our platform has scaled we have begun to generate high volumes of transactional data which can be potentially mined to generate additional insights. The Altenar Intelligence Platform (AIP) is the long term vision for unlocking this potential.

We are seeking a Technical AIP Data Analyst to join the Technology team to work closely with various Product and Business stakeholders to focus on using data pipelines and various analytical techniques to analyse business procedures. The role requires outstanding communication skills, proven ability to build and manage medium-scale modelling projects, define scope of existing infrastructure, identify data requirements, build methodology and tools that are well researched, undertake feasibility studies, and support with documentation. The candidate will be expected to demonstrate familiarity with concepts such as ETL, machine learning and statistics.

The successful applicant should illustrate comfort working in a startup environment, possessing high intellectual curiosity with ability to quickly learn new concepts/frameworks, algorithms and technology that powers Alternar business objectives. We seek someone with a high degree of ownership that can effectively examine flows of data, researching and evaluating to consistently deliver superior quality results on-time. The successful candidate should be able to rapidly prototype analytical models with the goal of generating strategic business insights as per the needs of the AIP roadmap.

Finally, the applicant is expected to display a high degree of independence working across multiple programs and initiatives to un-earth opportunities. The role demands strong data organisation, data structure, and data hierarchy knowledge, and may be suited to incumbents with extensive Data Analysis and/or computing programming experience.

The activities of a Technical AIP Data Analyst include but are not limited to:


Continuously updating core knowledge and skills in SQL and Python or R as applied to mathematical analysis
Liaises with internal stakeholders including the Solution Architect and Business Analysts, and directly with key Product and Business owners on topics for exploration and their description
Liaises with internal stakeholders including Data Engineers, Infrastructure Engineers and Data Services Engineers on data collection, movement and storage strategies
Analyse and Build analytical applications in a structured manner that can be tested and deployed based on stakeholder requirements
Undertake feasibility studies and various types of analytics including descriptive, diagnostic, predictive or prescriptive analytics.
Communicate the impact of technologies by delivering views, dashboards and report configurations using BI tools as required by internal stakeholders
Supporting software architecture processes with data handling and visualisation specifications
Participation in the Architecture Review process (including by providing advice and assistance) to ensure new initiatives or major changes to the Altenar product portfolio have data flow considerations represented at the inception phase
Examine existing business models and flows of data and design functional specifications by participating in the research, test, design of new data services tools for online or warehoused data
Work in a fast-paced, dynamic, multinational, multicultural environment
Creating and maintaining documentation for the solutions provided


Qualifications


Experience working with python and pandas
Strong SQL querying skills
Knowledge of noSQL and ETL operations considered a plus
Strong analytical and forecasting skills using offline methods such as spreadsheets
Strong math and statistics background (relevant Degree education required)
Proven experience with one or more BI tools (Metabase, Apache Superset, Redash, PowerBI, Tableau, Qlikview)
Ability to quickly write ad-hoc scripts to analyze small to medium datasets.
Strong research and validation skills
Motivated and self-driven to work in an environment with minimal supervision
Strong presentation skills
Proficient in spoken and written English
Experience in Technical Data Analytics, Data Science (Computing), and/or Product Management roles
Graduate-level education in Computer Science, Engineering, Math, Statistics, Economics, or other relevant field


Preferred Experience


Experience with Spark core functionality such as Spark SQL, Spark Structured Streaming is very desirable
Some experience in using Git version control in the context of documentation/source code management
Knowledge of Linux CLI tools to perform "quick and dirty" command line analysis to serve as first exploratory review of data
Some knowledge of MLaaS such as Amazon Machine Learning services, Azure Machine Learning, Google AI Platform, and IBM Watson Machine Learning
Experience in building, testing and validating machine learning algorithms
Prior direct or indirect involvement in data warehouse design, tool selection and support
Experience with Data Lab concepts with Hue, Zeppelin, Jupyterhub
Experience with Hadoop/HDFS - CLI, HDFS access from scripts
Knowledge of C# or Java is considered an asset

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

32–40 hours per week | On-site, Malta 

 

Bits Malta Ltd is looking for a commercially-minded, curious Data Analyst; someone who doesn’t just produce charts, but turns data into decisions. An honest admission: our previous Data Analyst delivered many beautiful dashboards. But dashboards don't change a business. Insights do.

 

We're looking for someone who reads every number with the question: "So what?"

 

What you'll do

Own our data warehouse. It's the place where signals surface, and action begins.
Deliver a weekly strategic scorecard to the executive team, with your interpretation and recommendations.
Translate patterns in player behaviour, marketing, compliance and cost into concrete improvements.
Proactively flag what the organisation is missing.
Support marketing, product, customer service and compliance with analyses that actually lead to action.

 

You're strong in

SQL (required), Python or R, Power BI or Tableau, and expert-level Excel.
Commercial thinking. You see data as input for decisions, not as an end in itself.
Working independently, juggling multiple questions in parallel, and owning projects from question to decision.
Excellent English, both verbal and written.
Online gambling / iGaming experience and familiarity with AML / Responsible Gambling is a strong plus.

 

What we offer

Direct exposure to executive decision-making. Your work is seen and used.
International environment in a fast-growing, regulated sector.
Room for autonomy and development.
Competitive salary, strong benefits, private health insurance and performance bonus.
Relocation support for candidates moving to Malta.

 

Screening filter

In your motivation, include one concrete example of an analysis that actually changed a business decision. Without this example, we won't consider your application.

 

Selection process

Introductory interview covering background and motivation.
Data case. You receive an anonymised dataset, formulate three commercial insights with recommendations, and present them in 15 minutes to the management team.
Final interview with the executive team.

 

Practical

On-site position at our Malta office.
Bits Malta Ltd provides operational, compliance and customer service facilitation within the online gambling sector.

 

No agency recruitment, please.

#DataAnalyst #iGaming #Malta #Hiring #BusinessIntelligence #PowerBI #SQL #ResponsibleGambling

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Casumo is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Join us at Casumo, where you are invited to be your authentic YOU-MO!

Company Overview

Welcome to Casumo, your passport to a world of fun, excitement, and responsible gaming. We're an international online casino company with a knack for creating unforgettable gaming experiences. Our secret sauce? A blend of innovation, security, and a dash of playful charm.

Nowadays, we're on the hunt for an analytical and collaborative Business Intelligence Analyst!

Position Overview

The Business Intelligence Analyst is responsible for developing, maintaining, and improving reporting and analytics solutions that support business decision making across the organization. The role works closely with Business Intelligence Analyst Lead, Senior Business Analysts, Data Engineers and business stakeholders to deliver accurate, scalable, and user-friendly reports. The BI Analyst translates defined business requirements into dashboards, reports and analyses.

Responsibilities


Design, develop, and maintain dashboards, reports, and self-service reporting solutions primarily using Tableau.
Develop and maintain SQL queries, reporting views, calculations, and supporting reporting logic.
Validate reporting accuracy and ensure KPI logic is implemented correctly.
Apply dimensional modelling and reporting best practices in analytics solutions
Ensure dashboards and reports are visually clear, user-friendly, and aligned with the best reporting practices.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
BettingJobs is working with a very well established land-based resorts company who also operate a successful online casino brand. They are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join their growing team in Malta.







About The Role




The Data Analyst is accountable for:




Deliver high-quality reporting and insights across online gaming products and platforms in regulated markets
Design and implement automated reporting, dashboards, and scalable data solutions to improve efficiency and reliability
Analyse complex datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities that inform product, commercial, and operational decisions
Monitor and improve data quality, working across internal teams and external vendors to ensure accuracy, integrity, and compliance







About You




You are a commercially minded and detail‑driven Data Analyst with strong experience working in complex, data‑rich digital environments. You thrive on turning large, messy datasets into clear, actionable insights and take pride in building reliable, scalable reporting solutions.




You are a self‑starter who enjoys working across systems, stakeholders, and vendors, and you bring a high level of rigour to data accuracy, documentation, and governance. You are confident engaging with both technical and non‑technical audiences, and you’re comfortable operating in regulated industries where compliance and precision are critical.




You bring proven experience in building dashboards, automating reporting, and analysing trends to support business decisions, ideally within online gaming or a similar regulated digital environment.




What’s in it for you?




Work on high impact digital initiatives shaping the company’s online future
A supportive, flexible, and inclusive workplace where you can thrive
Opportunities for development and career growth
Great company benefits

Title: Sports Data Analyst
Description:
Neo Group is a Malta-based startup established in 2024, built on over 15 years of veteran experience in the iGaming industry. Despite our startup spirit, our team brings a wealth of expertise gained through years of collaboration with industry leaders. With a network of 1,400 talented professionals across 10+ countries, we are committed to delivering innovative, data-driven solutions that empower our clients and foster professional growth within a dynamic and collaborative workplace.

We are hiring a Sports Data Analyst on behalf of our partner in Latvia.

Role Overview:

As a Sports Data Analyst, you will be responsible for monitoring betting activity in real time to protect the sportsbook from harmful exposure while ensuring a fair and safe betting environment for customers. The right candidate will understand that effective risk management is essential to sportsbook profitability and will take that responsibility seriously, balancing sharp analytical judgment with the ability to act decisively under pressure, while also supporting and guiding junior team members.

Responsibilities:


Monitor real-time sports events/results
Investigate and analyze market trends to obtain insights that assists in the prediction of customer behavior patterns
Keep up-to-date with sporting activity by following sports events and new updates
Observe competitor activity and make real-time adjustments where appropriate
Liaise with various internal departments
Remain flexible and support any other tasks needed to maintain a stable and effective team operation
Escalate and report problems that prohibit performing daily tasks


Requirements


Passion for Sports
Knowledge of pre-match and/or live trading will be considered as an advantage
Language proficiency in Latvian & English
Able to multitask and work under pressure
Strong analytical and problem-solving ability
Strong numerical and mathematical skills
Able to work flexible schedules including nights, weekends and public holidays
Team player as well as a self-starter
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills


Benefits


Kick off your birthday month with a bang! Not only do you get heartfelt birthday wishes from your team, but you also enjoy a fully paid day off. Celebrate your way!
Your dedication deserves more than applause. Depending on your tenure, you can snag up to 1,000€ in gift vouchers and unique souvenirs to mark your milestones with us
Prioritize your health with up to 80€ gross per month for wellness activities. Stay fit, unwind, or both - on us!
Invest in your growth with 500€ annually for learning. We're investing in your future!
When you need time off for health reasons, enjoy 2 fully-paid health days, plus additional days off in compliance with Latvian laws
Delight in extras like prescribed vacation, comprehensive post-probation health insurance, annual optical wear reimbursement, free parking, and fun office features like PS5 and massage chairs. Don't forget our lively team-building events - because work should be fun!

Title: Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst
Description:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities


Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.


Required Skills & Qualifications


3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).


Technical Skills Required


Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.


Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:


Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.


What Success Looks Like


Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.


Salary Is Based On Qualifications And Experience Between 60-70K

Extreme Networks Benefits

These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Join our Business Intelligence team, where data is transformed into insights that drive business performance and strategic decisions. You'll work with data from multiple sources, partnering with stakeholders across the business to define meaningful KPIs, build and maintain Tableau dashboards, and use SQL to uncover trends, validate data, and deliver actionable insights. Your work will directly influence decision-making, helping teams better understand performance, identify new opportunities, and continuously improve the way we use data to grow the business. If you're curious, analytical, and enjoy turning complex data into clear, impactful solutions, we'd love to hear from you.




Core responsibilities

Use SQL to extract, transform, and manage data from internal sources, ensuring it’s accurate and ready for analysis.
Analyze complex business problems, uncovering trends, root causes, and opportunities to improve performance.
Build and enhance Tableau reports and dashboards, turning data into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders.
Partner with product, management, and cross-functional teams to define KPIs and support data-driven decision-making.
Collaborate closely with BI teammates and data engineers to improve data pipelines, reduce technical debt, and scale the Data Warehouse.
Proactively explore data beyond assigned requests. Anticipate surface trends, risks, and opportunities to bring insights before issues arise.




Experience and key competencies

At least 2 years of experience in a Data Analyst, BI Analyst, or similar analytical role, supported by a relevant educational background (e.g. Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field).
Strong SQL skills, including querying, transforming, and validating large datasets, writing complex queries, and optimizing performance. Knowledge of Python or R is an advantage.
Hands-on experience building and maintaining dashboards in Tableau (or a similar BI tool), with the ability to present complex data in a clear and actionable way.
Solid understanding of data modeling, KPI design, metrics documentation, and statistical methods, with the ability to apply them to real business challenges.
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to translate business needs into analytical solutions and communicate insights effectively.
Analytical mindset with the ability to balance attention to detail while keeping the bigger picture in focus.
Self-driven and adaptable, with strong ownership, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Fluency in verbal and written communication in English is essential, as it's our primary means of interaction.




Think of this job ad as more of a friendly guide than a rigid 'must-have' list. We're well aware that no one will tick all the boxes, so feel free to apply even if you're not a perfect match – we love close matches too!




Our benefits, Your happiness

Enjoy 28 days of vacation with the option to use your balance on business days only, effectively giving you up to 10 extra days off. Combined with flexible working hours, you can make the most of your time off.
Prioritize your well-being with monthly sports/health compensation, full sick leave coverage for the first 8 days, our in-house gym, and a chill games room to unwind.
Invest in your professional growth with an annual training budget that can be used for courses, certifications, workshops, and learning opportunities relevant to your role.
Our office is a cozy (and dog-friendly) haven right in the heart of the city. Enjoy complimentary fruits and beverages and free parking. It's the perfect place to be productive and feel right at home.
Be part of the most legendary events and make amazing connections with colleagues from across the entire company.
Thrive in a diverse, multicultural environment where innovation and new ideas are welcomed, independence is encouraged, success is celebrated, and you'll have the freedom to make a real impact alongside talented people from across the globe.
Comprehensive relocation support, including arranged travel and initial accommodation, to help you settle in smoothly from day one.
..plus, if you're not already a pro, we'll teach you how to play poker!




Recruitment flow

Interview - Task - Second Interview - Background check - Offer
Start date: ASAP
Open to candidates based in the EU. Residence permit sponsorship available if needed.
Applications are open until we find the one - take the leap and apply now!




We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status.




Compensation

At Coolbet, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining your salary, including market benchmarks, specific job family, background, location, skills, and experience. The typical range for this role is 3000€-3500€ gross per month. This reflects total compensation.




Location

Tallinn, Estonia




Coolbet is an award-winning gaming company delivering both B2C and B2B platforms across international markets. We offer sportsbook, casino, and poker experiences to players in 8 countries across 3 continents and provide advanced B2B solutions to operators in the US. Part of the Sega Sammy Creation gaming segment, our core team of 500+ experts collaborates across borders to create world-class experiences for both players and operators. With a strong presence at our Tallinn HQ, home to a diverse team representing over 50 nationalities, we bring different perspectives together to shape the future of gaming through continuous product development, a collaborative culture, and a strong commitment to responsible play. Find out more about Coolbet!

Title: Digital Analyst - Product
Description:
Patrianna is a fast-scaling product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a dynamic, global team powering our growth. We operate at the intersection of technology and entertainment, building innovative solutions that shape the future of social gaming and deliver outstanding experiences to millions of players worldwide.

We're driven by speed, ambition, and bold ideas. At our core, we're product creators and problem-solvers who thrive in a high-performance environment. We're looking for exceptional talent—smart, adaptable, and motivated individuals eager to make an impact, scale business functions at pace, and continuously improve.

The Role

Transform data into actionable insights that drive product decisions and enhance player experiences across our gaming platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Guide product team members through complex data landscapes, providing valuable insights that support critical decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses of user behavior, app performance, and product features to boost player acquisition, engagement, and retention
Ensure top-tier behavioral data collection by collaborating with analytics developers on tracking implementation and issue resolution
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of key conversion metrics
Present insights clearly and effectively to stakeholders across the organization
Debug data issues and optimize tracking mechanisms to maintain data integrity


What We Are Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
Critical thinking mindset with proactive approach and high reliability
Solid SQL skills for querying and manipulating databases
Experience creating detailed reports and dashboards using tools like Looker or Tableau
Familiarity with digital analytics platforms such as Snowplow and GA4, plus tracking mechanisms and data layers
Experience with data modeling in Dataform and session replay/autotracking platforms is a bonus


Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape gaming experiences for millions of players. In our high-growth environment, you'll have the freedom to take ownership of impactful projects while collaborating with brilliant minds who value your expertise. Every analysis you deliver will drive real business decisions and help us stay ahead in the competitive gaming landscape.

Equal Opportunities

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Euromonitor:

Euromonitor International leads the world in data analytics and research into markets, industries, economies and consumers. We provide truly global insight and data on thousands of products and services; we are the first destination for organisations seeking growth. With our guidance, our clients can make bold, strategic decisions with confidence.

The Analytics team is hiring a Data Analyst to help improve the analytics processes and code underpinning Euromonitor's internal Forecast Model, supporting updates to our Passport data.

The role requires a strong programming foundation in Python or R (preferred), an understanding of cloud computing, and a genuine interest in data analytics. Alongside internal forecasting work, the role may also support client consulting projects, applying analytical solutions from problem definition through to insight delivery.

Key Responsibilities


Develop and enhance code for data processing, mathematical transformations, and statistical analysis
Contribute to peer reviews and solution design discussions
Translate analytical outputs into clear conclusions
Stay up to date with modern programming and cloud technologies
Maintain clear documentation and share best practices across the team


Requirements


Advanced Python knowledge
Proficiency with R (preferred)
Advanced knowledge of SQL
Ability to automate complex data analysis processes
Understanding of data flows, migrations, ETLs, pipelines, and other similar tools
Understanding of process scaling and parallelization
Understanding on how to write a production-ready code
A knowledge in econometrics, statistics and economics.
Knowledge of Dockers, Rest API, JSON data format would be an advantage
Knowledge of cloud computing services platforms providers (Azure, GCS, other)
Experience in SPARQL, JavaScript, HTML or similar would be an advantage
A genuine interest in Artificial Intelligence and related fields like data science and Machine learning would be an advantage.


Nice to have:


M.A. degree in computer science, statistics, econometrics, data science, mathematics, economics, physics or similar field. Highly skilled candidates with a B.A. will also be considered.


Benefits


Flexible work time with the perfect work/life balance
Competitive salary with wide variety of benefits like longer holidays, additional health insurance, social responsibility initiatives you can take part in, office perks
Intelligent and fun colleagues working in a constructive and inspiring atmosphere
Salary: EUR 30,000 - EUR 35,000


Why work for Euromonitor

Our values


We act with integrity
We are curious about the world
We are stronger together
We seek to empower
We find strength in diversity


International: not only do we have a very multinational workforce in each office but we are all dealing with our 16 offices worldwide on a daily basis. With 16 offices globally there are regular opportunities for international transfer.

Hardworking but sociable: our staff know how to work hard but also how to enjoy themselves! We pride ourselves on creating an appropriate work-life balance, with flexible hours and regular socialising including frequent after work meet ups, summer and Christmas parties and a whole range of sports and other groups to be involved with.

Committed to making a difference: We think that people are looking for something worthwhile in a company beyond the workplace. Our extensive Corporate Social Responsibility Programme gives each member of staff two volunteering days a year in addition to holidays. It sees us reaching out into the local community with our mentoring, group volunteering, and fundraising initiatives as well as supporting international charities through our website sales, matching staff sponsorship fundraising, and carbon offsetting all our flights, amongst many other activities.

Excellent benefits: we offer highly competitive salaries, healthcare insurance, food vouchers, saving fund, plus generous holiday allowances and in many offices a Core Hours policy allowing flexible start and finish times to each day.

Opportunities to grow: we offer extensive training and development opportunities at all levels. The vast majority of our managers and directors have been promoted from within and many have moved across departments as well as upwards. We pride ourselves on identifying and rewarding talent.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement: Euromonitor International does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability and genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, or other non-merit factor.

Title: Data Analyst - Internal Audit
Description:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Internal Audit branch of the Compliance & Internal Audit Analytics team at Wise.

You’ll partner with Internal Audit (IA) to develop and implement the analytics strategy across the function.

Internal Audit serves as an independent 3rd line of defence that oversees and provides an independent assessment and reasonable assurance on the effectiveness of the company’s corporate governance structure + internal controls.

The IA Analytics team supports IA by using analytics to provide more coverage, efficiency and accuracy to audit testing, as well as building innovative tools and using automation to support continuous testing and other IA activities beyond the annual audit plan.

This is an IC2 role. For more information on our Analytics Career Map and levelling structure, click here.

What You’ll Be Doing


Partnering with stakeholders across the IA function covering all regions, products and domains of the business (including Financial Crime, Ops, Risk, Finance and Technology)
Identifying & implementing opportunities to do the most impactful analytics within audits and influencing and upskilling your partners in IA to do the same
Working with analytics, engineering, and business specialists to seek out and understand interesting and varied datasets, perform analytics using modern approaches, and develop a strong understanding of operations at Wise
Championing innovation and data-led insights, not just within audits but across all IA activities where analytics can add value
Working on automation and tooling projects to increase efficiency across IA including workflow automation, continuous auditing, leveraging AI, and building MI dashboards


Qualifications

What you’ll bring:


3+ years of experience in data analytics
1+ years of data analytics experience within an audit, risk, or compliance related role
Advanced stakeholder management skills; you know how to leverage multiple different teams to deliver high-impact change including analytics, engineering, and business specialists
Extensive experience using SQL; you can build and maintain complex data sources and can leverage visualisation tools to surface insights


Nice To Have


Experience of working in regulated environments, Fintech or Financial Services.

Title: BI Engineer
Description:
Description

Position at LeoVegas Group

About The Role

Are you driven by data and have a talent for transforming insights into actionable strategies? We're seeking a talented BI Engineer to join our innovative team. In this role, you'll be instrumental in designing and developing business intelligence solutions that empower our organization to make informed decisions.

If you have a passion for data analytics, a knack for problem-solving, and a desire to drive business growth, apply now to be a part of our data-driven journey!

YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:


Creating complex functions and stored procedures, analyse queries, develop security protocols and resolve problems based on business needs
Ensuring data accuracy, performance, and usability of existing dashboards, implementing improvements as needed.
Working closely with data engineers to define data requirements, optimise data pipelines, and ensure efficient data delivery.
Utilising Tableau to create compelling visualisations that effectively communicate insights to stakeholders at all levels.
Adhering to and promoting data quality standards, ensuring data integrity and security.
Developing and maintaining robust BI solutions that meet the evolving needs of the business, exploring and integrating new technologies as needed.
Effectively communicating data-driven insights and recommendations to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Working closely with various departments (e.g., marketing, finance, product) to understand their data needs and provide actionable insights.
Continuously learning and exploring new technologies and methodologies in the BI landscape, sharing relevant advancements with the team.


OUR SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE THE FOLLOWING:

Essential Skills


Experience in the iGaming industry.
2-5 years of relevant experience in a similar role and strong academic background.
Ability to explain complex technical concepts to diverse stakeholders, including non-technical team members and senior management.
Ability to produce clear and concise documentation, ensuring that technical details were easily understandable by all parties involved.
Advanced knowledge of SQL, preferably knowledge of Python and Airflow.
Experience with BI tools, preferably Tableau.


NICE TO HAVES


Experience working with different database systems.


Who We Are

At the core of LeoVegas Group is Team Leo. Our culture is our foundation and is what enables us to innovate, build, and lead as we trailblaze our way through the igaming industry. We’re a team of over 2000 innovators, initiators, and groundbreakers working in a fast-paced and agile environment across 19 offices worldwide.

BENEFITS:


Hybrid work policy
4 weeks of Workation (T&C apply)
Well-being allowance to support your active lifestyle
Private health insurance
Discounts across a range of retailers, gyms, bars & restaurants
We offer an employee assistance program that can provide help and guidance during challenging moments.
For non-Malta residents, we offer a relocation package with 3 weeks of accommodation and flights.


JOIN US!

In our pride, we empower our teammates to find their roar and run with their wildest ideas. We don’t wait for things to happen; we pounce and make it happen!

Would you be a good fit for the Leo Pride - give us a roar!

Title: Commercial Analytics Specialist
Description:
KM Malta Airlines Limited, the national airline of the Maltese Islands, is seeking to recruit a Commercial Analytics Specialist within the Commercial department.




Purpose of Position




The Commercial Analytics Specialist is a key conduit of KPI management and all commercial analytics development that relies on utilising and interpreting data to make effective, reliable business decisions based on accurate information.
Accountable for the setting and management of all metrics for good control of revenue performance and strategic compliance across commercial.
The role is to act as a hub-and-spoke model that delivers analytics and algorithms that deliver competitive advantage.




Responsibilities




Develop all aspects of corporate strategy, data, information and analytics strategy.
Actively contribute to the development of the overall commercial strategy and business plan for the airline.
Take responsibility for construct and distribution of revenue budget targets by partnering with Network Sales and Revenue Management.
Own, create, and develop all technical aspects related to data tracking, delivery, and reporting.
Ensure that regulatory compliance and data quality are maintained throughout the design and management of data and information systems, set standards to manage both physical and logical data.
Create, document and present technical designs, technical solutions to relevant stakeholders.
Ensure communication of sales actions and results within the team and to stakeholders in Sales, Revenue Management, Network Planning, Distribution, and Finance.
Develop and foster relationships with reporting and analytics communities across the organisation identifying opportunities to share best practice and develop collaborative strategies that deliver group benefits.




Experience, Qualifications and Skills




Extensive background in data mining and / or statistical analysis.
Able to understand various data structures and common methods in data transformation.
Experience with programming languages, such as Java / Saas / Python / SQL.
Familiarity in Revenue Forecasting, Revenue & Route Planning, Commercial Data Architectures, Airline Data Warehouse, Revenue Accounting, NDC, One Order.
Experience in the airline industry is desired.
Degree MQF Level 6 or higher or other professional qualification in Computer Science, Statistics, Applied Math / Data Science or related field (essential).
Computing & Analytics: Requires a basic knowledge on Cloud platforms, SQL & NoSQL, Tableau, PowerBI, MicroStrategy, Project Management, Business Analysis, Agile, Data Modeling & Warehousing, Data Mining.
Strong analytical skills, problem-solving and decision-making capability.




Jobsplus Permit: 283/2024




Closing Date: 02nd August 2026

Title: Growth Analyst
Description:
About Bolt

Bolt is shaping a future where cities are built for people, not cars. What started as an ambitious project of a 19-year-old in 2013 has grown into a global mobility platform used by more than 200 million customers and 4.5 million driver and courier partners across 50 countries.

From ride-hailing and food delivery to scooters, e-bikes, and car-sharing, Bolt helps people move through cities every day. Small, autonomous teams drive this work, combining the speed and ownership of a startup with the scale of a global technology company backed by more than €1bn in funding.

And we're just getting started.

Why join Bolt?


Build the future of mobility and help transform how people live and move in cities.
Tackle challenges at scale that span technology, operations, regulation, and growth.
Take real ownership early. Small, lean teams mean your decisions matter from day one, and you'll learn fast alongside some of the best people in the industry.
Join one of Europe's leading technology companies, backed by world-class investors including Sequoia.


About The Role

As a Growth Analyst, you will take ownership of our campaign spending decisions and contribute to the marketplace's success by meticulously analysing the return on investment (ROI). With your expertise, we'll be able to accurately determine the most strategic allocation of resources, including the exact location, timing, method, and amount spent on campaigns.

You will be working closely with other team members of the Growth domain, as well as Expansion Managers, Marketing and Operations teams. A Data Analyst will need to have good attention to detail and communication skills to succeed. Solid business sense and analytical mindset? You might be the campaign magician we’ve been waiting for.

Main Tasks And Responsibilities


Own campaign spending decisions for your assigned markets and optimise such decisions to help the country team reach their growth targets
Wrangle complex data sets to come up with testing hypotheses, setting up A/B tests (and other experiments) and analysing results using statistical techniques in order to guide your decision-making
Proactively working together with Expansion Managers, Marketing, Operations and local teams to drive collaboration and get the most out of our investments
Identify valuable opportunities in the team and contribute to global campaigns team playbook, processes and product requests


About You


BS in Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, Information Management or Statistics
At least 3 years of experience in an analytical role dealing with large volumes of data. Experience in consulting, pricing/discount strategy and/or on-demand is a plus
Solid grasp of statistics and demonstrated experience with A/B testing
Amazing SQL and Excel skills. Experience with Python or R is a plus
Experience with some BI tools and data visualisation like Tableau, Power BI or Looker
Fluent in English


Why You’ll Love It Here


Accelerate your professional growth with unique career opportunities.
Enjoy a rewarding salary and stock options, knowing that as Bolt succeeds, so do you.
Take care of your physical and mental health with our wellness perks.
Celebrate 5 years at Bolt with a 1-month paid sabbatical to recharge.
Connect with colleagues at annual company events and smaller team gatherings.
Balance flexibility and in-person collaboration with our hybrid model, including at least 12 monthly in-office days.


Some perks may differ depending on your location and role.

At Bolt, using AI daily is a baseline expectation for everyone. If there’s a faster, smarter way to do your work, we expect you to find it. You’re still responsible for the output, so the standard stays high when using AI tools.

Experience is great, but what we really look for is drive, intelligence, and integrity. So even if you don’t tick every box, please consider applying!

Everyone is welcome

Bolt is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace where all qualified applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, colour, nationality, religion, gender or gender identity, sexual orientation, age, or disability.

We welcome applications from all backgrounds and are happy to make reasonable accommodations throughout our hiring process. If you require any adjustments, please let us know.

Title: Insight Analyst
Description:
We are looking for an Insight Analyst with strong SQL skills and a passion for turning data into actionable insights. In this role, you will analyze and interpret iGaming data to identify trends, support strategic decision-making, and help optimize business performance and player experience.




In this role, you are going to:

Act as a dedicated analyst for the CRM team, serving as the first point of contact for all data and insight needs
Participate in CRM team rituals and planning meetings to ensure analytical support is aligned with business priorities
Gather, process, and analyze large volumes of iGaming-related data to conduct regular and ad-hoc analyses across various aspects of the business, providing insights into performance, trends, and optimization opportunities
Ensure the accuracy and integrity of data by performing cleaning, validation, and quality checks.
Calculate and evaluate ROI, and potential revenue gains or losses of business initiatives and marketing campaigns
Monitor and analyze assigned financial, behavioral, and engagement KPIs of customers across multiple business units
Translate business questions into analytical approaches and provide actionable recommendations based on findings
Present insights and recommendations to stakeholders in a clear and concise manner using storytelling and/or visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar platforms
Work closely with stakeholders across multiple departments, including marketing, CRM, product, customer support, and commercial teams
Support budgeting and forecasting processes by identifying trends, risks, and growth opportunities through data analysis
Independently manage analytical tasks and communicate progress, risks, and blockers proactively
Contribute to improving analytical processes, reporting quality, and documentation standards




To be successful in this role, you need to have:

Experience in data analysis, business intelligence, product analytics or a similar role
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets
Knowledge of at least one visualization tool: Tableau, Power BI, or Looker
Experience presenting insights and recommendations to business stakeholders
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Experience performing KPI analysis, segmentation, trend analysis, or performance monitoring
Excellent communication skills and proficiency in spoken and written English
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work independently




Preferred skills and experience:

Experience working with CRM data including customer lifecycle, retention/engagement metrics, and familiarity with iGaming mechanics such as bonusing and segmentation
Familiarity with statistical concepts, experimentation methodologies, and A/B testing
Experience working with cloud data warehouses such as BigQuery, Snowflake, or similar
Experience with Python or similar scripting language for data manipulation and automation




We will show our gratitude by:

Offering working tools of employees´ own choice - Company makes sure everyone feels comfortable in the office and has all the necessary tools one might need
Creating an exciting and fun international English-speaking environment
Working in a modern office in Ülemiste City
Organizing different company and team events
Offering extra sports compensation via Stebby + private health insurance
Creating an opportunity to learn and develop
Offering a competitive salary based on experience and a benefits package




If this seems like a challenge you are looking for, apply and let’s have a chat!




Sounds great but what do we do?

We are a growing company focused on the use of data to benefit e-commerce operations. Our Data Scientists provide decision-making assistance and models to improve operational efficiency and customer experience. The Analytics team provides insights on specific topics. Our Business Intelligence team makes information accessible. Together with the right technology we deliver the foundation for highly automated and effective CRM solutions.

Learn more about NabuMinds: https://nabuminds.com




As we strive to achieve our mission we reaffirm our commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI). We are proud to employ people of all backgrounds who possess the talent, energy, and focus to accelerate our vision forward and to tell our story. Each employee is encouraged to bring their whole self to work, knowing that NabuMinds values individuality as much as we value collaboration.

Title: Technical Data Analyst - Data Warehouse Services
Description:
Established in 2004, we are a tech pioneer offering world-class adult entertainment and games on some of the internet’s safest and most popular platforms. With the support of an international team of dynamic and collaborative innovators, we are on a mission to enable safe user experiences and empower our communities by celebrating diversity, inclusion, and expression — all while maintaining robust trust-and-safety protocols.

We embrace the best of both worlds! Local talent can thrive in our collaborative office space with the flexibility of a hybrid work environment, while remote team members play an integral role in shaping our dynamic culture from afar. We have offices in Montreal (Quebec), Austin (Texas) and Nicosia (Cyprus).


A select number of positions require full-time in office attendance*


The Data Warehouse Services team plays a vital role in our organization. They are responsible for storing vast quantities of data from many sources and responsible for disseminating data across the organization to various different groups. They are one of the backbones of data at our organization and they are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to join their team.

What you'll be doing:


Collaborate with internal stakeholders including; Directors, Analysts, and Developers to understand business objectives and requirements, and help define the strategy, content, and features for our reporting environments.
Engage with business partners at all levels to discuss and clarify data analysis, needs and requirements.
Analyze data structures and content from data marts to support business intelligence initiatives.
Thrive in a fast-paced environment, where multitasking and adaptability are essential.
Participate in brainstorming sessions to define functional requirements, organize content, map process flows, and specify report features.
Develop and optimize queries to investigate data issues or new reporting structures for various business units.
Work closely with Database Administrators and Developers to estimate time and effort for report projects and refining these estimates throughout the project.

Title: Data Analyst / SQL Specialist
Description:
We are looking for a strong Data Analyst / SQL Specialist to support our teams with one-off data reports, SQL query writing, and SQL query review across our internal platforms.

This is a hands-on role for someone who is comfortable working directly with databases, understanding business questions, and turning them into accurate and reliable SQL outputs. We need someone pragmatic, detail-oriented, and able to work closely with technical, product, finance, CRM, and management stakeholders to provide clear and trustworthy data.

Ownership is a key element of this role. We are not just looking for someone who can write queries on request, but for someone who can understand the context behind a report, challenge unclear requirements, validate the logic, identify risks in the data and help ensure that the numbers being used by the business are correct.

What You Will Do


Create one-off reports using SQL based on business and operational requests
Translate reporting needs from management, product, finance, CRM, and operations into accurate data outputs
Write, review, and improve SQL queries used in reports, dashboards, exports, and internal tools
Analyze existing queries to identify issues such as incorrect joins, duplicate counting, wrong aggregations, missing filters, or inefficient logic
Review slow or heavy queries and suggest improvements to performance and structure
Help validate that reported numbers are accurate and aligned with business definitions
Recommend improvements to query structure, indexes, views, or reporting approaches where relevant
Document the logic behind important reports and recurring SQL queries
Collaborate with developers and stakeholders to clarify requirements and edge cases
Raise concerns early when data may be incomplete, misleading, or incorrectly interpreted


What We Are Looking For


Strong SQL experience in production environments
Ability to write, review, and optimize complex SQL queries
Good understanding of joins, aggregations, subqueries, CTEs, indexes, and query execution logic
Experience working with relational databases such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, or similar
Ability to understand business requirements and convert them into reliable data outputs
Strong attention to detail and ability to identify inconsistencies in data
Good debugging and investigation skills when numbers do not match expectations
Ability to explain query logic and findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Practical mindset with the ability to improve existing queries without overengineering
Clear communication and a strong sense of ownership


Nice to have


Experience with BI or reporting tools such as Metabase, Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar
Experience working with Laravel/PHP-based applications or product databases
Experience with finance, CRM, payments, user activity, gaming, or high-volume transactional data
Understanding of database performance analysis and execution plans
Ability to suggest improvements to reporting structures, not only individual queries
Experience working with dashboards, exports, and recurring business reports


Who You Are

You are comfortable working with existing databases, understanding how data is structured, and turning business questions into practical SQL reports. You know how to balance speed with accuracy, and you care about producing outputs that people can trust.

You communicate clearly, ask the right questions, raise issues early, and do not just assume that a query is correct because it returns a result. You take responsibility for the logic behind your work, think beyond the request itself, and care about the reliability of the final numbers.

We are looking for someone who is dependable, analytical and comfortable being accountable for the quality of the final output.

This role is a strong fit for someone who likes structure, cares about accuracy and wants to work with real business data rather than simply producing isolated queries.

Salary range: €45,000 – €70,000 gross per year, depending on experience and seniority level.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Eeze is a Malta-based, technology-driven company that has been developing world-leading online gaming solutions since 1999. Underpinned by a history that directly interconnects with the rise of online gambling, the company has been a key pioneer within the international market. Over the years, Eeze has amassed a world-class team of gambling specialists who remain at the frontiers of innovation within this ever-evolving industry. With a focus on being an international B2B Slots and Live Casino provider with our Eeze brand, we have over 900 talented team members based in various locations across Europe, with our headquarters and studio in Malta - Europe’s leading iGaming hub.

Role Overview

The Business Analyst is responsible for providing data-driven insights, performance analysis, and structured reporting to support decision-making across Live Casino operations. The role focuses on analysing studio performance, identifying trends, and delivering actionable recommendations to key stakeholders, including the Director of Live Games Operations and Partner Studio Operations Manager. In addition, the Business Analyst is responsible for compiling and maintaining key operational documentation, ensuring alignment with SOPs, version control standards, and certification requirements. While supporting testing activities where required, the primary focus of the role is to act as the analytical and governance backbone of the Strategy unit.

Key Responsibilities

Performance Analysis & Reporting

Produce Monthly Studio Performance Reports Covering


Operational performance.
Incident trends.
Game Presenter-related insights (where relevant).
Studio comparisons.


Provide clear, structured recommendations based on findings.

Present Insights To


Director of Live Games Operations.
Partner Studio Operations Manager.
Internal Strategy stakeholders.


Data Analysis & Insights

Analyse Data Across


Studio performance.
Incident patterns.
Operational efficiency.
Player-related trends (where available).


Identify:


Root causes recurring issues.
Performance gaps.
Improvement opportunities.


Dashboard Development & Maintenance

Develop And Maintain Performance Dashboards To Provide Visibility On


Key operational KPIs.
Studio-level performance.
Trends over time.
Ensure dashboards are:
Accurate.
User-friendly.
Aligned with business needs.


Documentation & Certification Support

Compile And Maintain Documentation Related To


New dealer clients.
Operational flows.
Incident handling and settlement procedures.


Ensure documentation:


Follows structured version control and history tracking.
It is aligned with approved SOPs.
Supports certification and audit requirements.


Work closely with relevant stakeholders to ensure documentation is:


Accurate.
Up to date.
Consistently applied across operations.


Recommendations & Continuous Improvement:


Translate data into actionable recommendations.


Support Stakeholders In Understanding


Key risks.
Performance gaps.
Areas for optimisation.
Track impact of implemented improvements where possible.


Testing Support

Support Testing Activities When Required, Including


New dealer clients.
Platform releases.
Product updates.
Provide structured feedback based on testing outcomes.


Success Metrics (KPIs)


Quality and clarity of performance reports.
Accuracy and usability of dashboards.
Relevance and impact of recommendations.
Completeness and audit-readiness of documentation.


Essential

Stakeholder satisfaction and adoption of insights.Required Skills & Expereince


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Experience working with data, reporting, and dashboards.
Ability to translate data into clear insights.
Strong organisational and documentation skills.


Desirable:


Experience in live operations or similar environments.
Familiarity with reporting or BI tools.
Experience with SOP-driven or regulated environments.
Familiarity with AI tools for data analytics.


Key Competencies


Analytical thinking
Attention to detail.
Structured communication.
Organisation and documentation discipline.
Stakeholder management.


What’s in it for you?


Experience a dynamic and team-orientated work environment
Opportunities for personal growth and learning
An open, inclusive and supportive team where you will be valued, and your suggestions will be welcome
24 days paid holiday per year, in addition to local public holidays
Birthday Leave, a paid day off in the month of your birthday
