Title: BUSINESS DATA ANALYST
Description:
HELLO! 👋

We are KIKA GROUP – a rapidly growing group of companies with 33 years of market leadership, consisting of:

🏠 KIKA LT – a company managing the KIKA retail chain in Lithuania and Estonia, as well as wholesale operations in Lithuania.

🐾 AKVATERA – a specialized pet food manufacturing company exporting its products to more than 60 countries worldwide.

🥽 AKVATERA W – a modern canned pet food manufacturing facility focused on the highest quality and innovation to ensure the best nutrition for your pets.

🐕 TAURO KENNEL – a dog breeding kennel winning awards at international dog shows.

✨ TAURO GROOMING ACADEMY – premium pet SPA salons and a professional training academy.

🌐 KIKA USA LLC – the group's division in the United States of America.

Your responsibilities:


Analyze large amounts of data and provide insights to support business decisions.
Create and maintain reports and visualizations using Power BI. Work with databases, write and optimize SQL queries.
Develop and refine semantic data models.
Apply machine learning methods for forecasting and analysis.
Integrate artificial intelligence (AI) tools into analytical processes (e.g., for forecasting, segmentation, and anomaly detection).
Develop, implement, and maintain AI solutions and AI agents designed to automate and streamline business processes.
Continuously analyze the latest artificial intelligence technologies, evaluate their potential applications within the organization, and initiate their implementation.
Automate data analysis processes.
Collaborate with other teams to improve data quality and accessibility, and to implement data-driven and AI-powered solutions.

Higher education in Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Statistics, or a related field.
Excellent knowledge of Python and SQL.
Experience working with Power BI.
Ability to create semantic data models.
Understanding of machine learning principles and their application.
Experience working with Big Data solutions.
Experience applying AI tools.
Strong analytical thinking, ability to solve problems independently, attention to detail, and responsibility for data accuracy.
Experience analyzing business needs and providing data-driven insights.
Excellent English language skills.


KIKA offers:

📈 A position in the leading pet products retail and manufacturing company in the Baltic States.

🏡 Freedom to drive change and opportunities to achieve your professional goals.

💰 A supportive, friendly, and professional team that is looking forward to welcoming you!

€3,300–4,500 gross/month.

We will gladly discuss the specific compensation package during the interview, taking into account your experience and qualifications!

Title: Product Data Analyst II
Description:
At F5, we strive to bring a better digital world to life. Our teams empower organizations across the globe to create, secure, and run applications that enhance how we experience our evolving digital world. We are passionate about cybersecurity, from protecting consumers from fraud to enabling companies to focus on innovation.

Everything we do centers around people. That means we obsess over how to make the lives of our customers, and their customers, better. And it means we prioritize a diverse F5 community where each individual can thrive.

The Opportunity

We believe that behind every great product feature is an incredible story told by data. As a Product Data Analyst II within our Product Data Group, you won’t just be pulling numbers—you’ll be the critical bridge between Product Management, Engineering, and our Data Group. By diving deep into product telemetry and logs, you'll help us decode user behavior and directly shape the future of our products.

What You’ll Do


Design for Impact: Collaborate closely with Product Engineering to design data instrumentation that captures genuine user behavior, events, and meaningful outcomes.
Translate & Transform: Act as the ultimate translator—turning high-level business questions and new feature requests into crisp, technical data requirements.
Uncover Insights: Analyze rich product datasets to guide the development of pipelines and new features, working hand-in-hand with Data Engineers and Data Scientists.
Define Success: Help define and track the core metrics and KPIs that truly reflect product health and performance.
Guard Data Quality: Conduct rigorous data quality analyses to spot gaps before they become issues, and contribute to the design of robust monitoring and alerting systems.



What You’ll Bring To The Table


Be kind: F5 has human-first approach
Solid Experience: 2-4 years shaping data into insights, with a focus on statistical analysis, dashboarding, and data quality/profiling.
Technical Chops: Strong proficiency in SQL—you know how to navigate, query, and tame large, complex datasets.
Data Storytelling: Strong ability to shape narratives using data, providing visualization and reports that illuminate the import of the data
Domain Knowledge: Familiarity with product telemetry and observability (knowing how products "talk" to us).
Collaboration: Stellar communication skills. You know how to tell a compelling story with data and collaborate effortlessly across varied teams.



Bonus Points (Nice to Have)


Experience navigating a software development organization, including familiarity with version control (git), the SDLC, and testing frameworks.

At F5, we strive to bring a better digital world to life. Our teams empower organizations across the globe to create, secure, and run applications that enhance how we experience our evolving digital world. We are passionate about cybersecurity, from protecting consumers from fraud to enabling companies to focus on innovation.

Everything we do centers around people. That means we obsess over how to make the lives of our customers, and their customers, better. And it means we prioritize a diverse F5 community where each individual can thrive.

The Opportunity

We believe that behind every great product feature is an incredible story told by data. As a Product Data Analyst II within our Product Data Group, you won’t just be pulling numbers—you’ll be the critical bridge between Product Management, Engineering, and our Data Group. By diving deep into product telemetry and logs, you'll help us decode user behavior and directly shape the future of our products.

What You’ll Do

  • Design for Impact: Collaborate closely with Product Engineering to design data instrumentation that captures genuine user behavior, events, and meaningful outcomes.
  • Translate & Transform: Act as the ultimate translator—turning high-level business questions and new feature requests into crisp, technical data requirements.
  • Uncover Insights: Analyze rich product datasets to guide the development of pipelines and new features, working hand-in-hand with Data Engineers and Data Scientists.
  • Define Success: Help define and track the core metrics and KPIs that truly reflect product health and performance.
  • Guard Data Quality: Conduct rigorous data quality analyses to spot gaps before they become issues, and contribute to the design of robust monitoring and alerting systems.


What You’ll Bring To The Table

  • Be kind: F5 has human-first approach
  • Solid Experience: 2-4 years shaping data into insights, with a focus on statistical analysis, dashboarding, and data quality/profiling.
  • Technical Chops: Strong proficiency in SQL—you know how to navigate, query, and tame large, complex datasets.
  • Data Storytelling: Strong ability to shape narratives using data, providing visualization and reports that illuminate the import of the data
  • Domain Knowledge: Familiarity with product telemetry and observability (knowing how products "talk" to us).
  • Collaboration: Stellar communication skills. You know how to tell a compelling story with data and collaborate effortlessly across varied teams.


Bonus Points (Nice to Have)

  • Experience navigating a software development organization, including familiarity with version control (git), the SDLC, and testing frameworks.
  • Hands-on experience with modern data stack tools like dbt, Databricks, and Tableau.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About Block Labs

Block Labs is a premier technology studio operating at the bleeding edge of Web3, Artificial Intelligence, and iGaming. We don't just ship features; we engineer high-scale, production-grade platforms that power the next generation of digital products.

We are a collective of senior engineers, product strategists, and builders who refuse to compromise on architecture. Whether we are designing autonomous multi-agent AI systems, building decentralized financial infrastructure, or architecting high-frequency iGaming platforms, our standard is excellence.

We move fast, but we build for the long term. If you are looking to work alongside a team that values deep technical expertise, thoughtful system design, and product ownership, Block Labs is where you belong.

About The Role

We’re looking for a data-obsessed BI Analyst to turn blockchain and product data into crisp insights that drive growth, retention, and risk controls across our crypto-enabled iGaming products. You’ll own the end-to-end analytics stack for wallet funnels, wagering behavior, lifecycle marketing, and compliance—using SQL, DBT, Python, Amplitude and Tableau as your primary tools. In the future we planning transition from Tableau to other BI tools alternatives like Metabase or Apache Superset or others.

Key Responsibilities


Drive the business forward through proactive analytics - don't just fulfill dashboard requests; spot anomalies, frame the right question yourself, and bring actionable recommendations to Product / CRM / Risk before they have to ask..
Build, maintain, and iterate dashboards surfacing KPIs (GGR/NGR, ARPU, LTV, churn, deposit→wager conversion, fraud loss rate).
Write production-grade SQL (CTEs, window functions, incremental models) to model clean datasets from Clickhouse and on-chain tables.
Build and maintain AI-agent workflows that automate recurring analytics - anomaly investigation, root-cause loops etc.
Use Python (pandas, seaborn, sklearn, scipy) for analysis, data quality checks, lightweight ETL/backfills, API pulls (e.g., blockchain providers), and automation.
Work with on-chain data: parse EVM logs, token transfers (ERC-20/721), join labeled address datasets, analyze exchange/bridge flows, gas/fee dynamics, and wallet clustering.
Partner with Product, Marketing/CRM, Risk/Compliance, and Engineering to define event schemas and tracking plans; ensure data quality, lineage, and documentation.
Design and analyze experiments, cohort analyses, and attribution within iGaming and regional compliance constraints.
Investigate anomalies (bonus abuse, botting, arbitrage, suspicious wallet rings) and recommend mitigations.
Automate recurring reporting and alerting for KPI movements.


Must-haves


2+ years in BI or Data Analytics; crypto or iGaming exposure prefered.
Ambitions about building great analytics infrastructure.
Good SQL: complex joins, window functions, query optimization, and dimensional modeling.
Solid AI-collaboration craft — prompt & context engineering, spec-driven task framing, and eval-driven iteration. Comfortable decomposing business questions into precise, agent-executable steps and designing the right tools for the agent to call.
BI Versatility: Expert knowledge of Tableau (LODs, parameters, performance tuning) combined with a strong interest or experience in modern, open-source BI tools like Metabase or Apache Superset.
Proficient Python for analytics & data engineering: pandas, modular scripts/notebooks, API integrations, scheduling small jobs, and writing maintainable code.
Solid statistics and probability fundamentals for experimentation - hypothesis testing, sample size and power, confidence intervals, and the discipline to distinguish real signal from noise.
Practical understanding of crypto wallets and transaction flows.


Nice-to-haves


dbt, Great Expectations/Monte Carlo for data quality; orchestration with Airflow/Prefect.
Built or contributed to production AI agents for analytics — ReAct loops, RAG over a knowledge base , MCP servers for data sources, multi-agent orchestration. Knows how to curate domain knowledge for agents so they stop hallucinating.
Ability to produce on-chain analytics (Dune/Flipside/Goldsky/Nansen tables or self-indexed data; contract ABIs; entity labeling).
Warehouse ecosystems knowledge Clickhouse, AWS, S3 buckets.
Data governance & privacy (GDPR, data minimization, retention policies).
Understanding of iGaming economics & KPIs: deposits, wagers, hold, GGR/NGR, RTP, bonus efficiency, RG metrics, and common fraud/AML patterns.
Experience with product analytics services like Amplitude, Mixpanel, Google Analytics.


What kind of culture can I expect?

Mature, mission-driven, and low-ego. We value clarity over noise, outcomes over theatrics, and pace without chaos. If you’re one of the smartest minds in your craft and want to build with other experts, you’ll feel at home here.

Title: Reporting Engineer
Description:
We are looking for a Reporting Engineer to join our Data Warehouse team. In this role, you will develop and maintain regulatory and in-house reporting solutions that ensure compliance, data accuracy, and reliable insights for both internal stakeholders and B2B clients.

You will work closely with software developers and the DWH team to maintain and improve our reporting ecosystem, troubleshoot existing queries, and ensure reporting evolves alongside product and data changes. This role plays an important part in maintaining data integrity, supporting regulatory requirements, and continuously improving reporting performance in a fast-paced environment.




Core responsibilities

Develop high-performing SQL queries and reporting solutions on large datasets.
Build and maintain regulatory, in-house, and B2B client reporting.
Troubleshoot and optimize existing reports, improving performance and data integrity.
Ensure data consistency by analyzing dependencies between reports.
Maintain and improve documentation for reporting logic, KPIs, and data transformations.
Identify and help reduce technical debt in reporting solutions.
Support the DWH team in maintaining and improving the reporting ecosystem.
Participate in agile ceremonies such as sprint planning and retrospectives.
Collaborate with both technical teams and business stakeholders as needed.
Identify and contribute to process and reporting improvements.




Experience and key competencies

Strong experience writing efficient SQL queries on large datasets.
Good understanding of relational databases (PostgreSQL) and database architecture.
Experience in reporting, data analysis, or data engineering roles.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently.
Ability to adapt to changing priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent spoken and written English.
Python or JavaScript/TypeScript knowledge and experience with tools like Prefect or Telerik is a plus.




Coolbet operates in a highly regulated environment. This role may require a gaming license in any jurisdiction in which the company operates, and this position may require the ability to secure and maintain a license in all relevant jurisdictions. A gaming license application utilizes personal data, a criminal and credit check, and fingerprints. Coolbet will cover all costs and provide access to an Identity Protection program.




Our benefits, Your happiness

Enjoy 28 days of vacation with the option to use your balance on business days only, effectively giving you up to 10 extra days off. Combined with flexible working hours, you can make the most of your time off.
Prioritize your well-being with monthly sports/health compensation, full sick leave coverage for the first 8 days, our in-house gym, and a chill games room to unwind.
Invest in your professional growth with an annual training budget that can be used for courses, certifications, workshops, and learning opportunities relevant to your role.
Our office is a cozy (and dog-friendly) haven right in the heart of the city. Enjoy complimentary fruits and beverages and free parking. It's the perfect place to be productive and feel right at home.
Be part of the most legendary events and make amazing connections with colleagues from across the entire company.
Thrive in a diverse, multicultural environment where innovation and new ideas are welcomed, independence is encouraged, success is celebrated, and you'll have the freedom to make a real impact alongside talented people from across the globe.
..plus, if you're not already a pro, we'll teach you how to play poker!




Recruitment flow

Interview - Technical Interview - Police record, and background check - Offer
Start date: ASAP
Open to candidates based in Estonia
Applications are open until we find the one - take the leap and apply now!




We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status.




Compensation

At Coolbet, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining your salary, including market benchmarks, specific job family, background, location, skills, and experience. The typical range for this role is 3000€-3800€ gross per month. This reflects total compensation.




Location

Tallinn, Estonia




Coolbet is an award-winning gaming company delivering both B2C and B2B platforms across international markets. We offer sportsbook, casino, and poker experiences to players in 8 countries across 3 continents and provide advanced B2B solutions to operators in the US. Part of the Sega Sammy Creation gaming segment, our core team of 500+ experts collaborates across borders to create world-class experiences for both players and operators. With a strong presence at our Tallinn HQ, home to a diverse team representing over 50 nationalities, we bring different perspectives together to shape the future of gaming through continuous product development, a collaborative culture, and a strong commitment to responsible play. Find out more about Coolbet!

Title: Data Analyst II
Description:
About Us

GoCardless is a global bank payment company. Over 100,000 businesses, from start-ups to household names, use GoCardless to collect and send payments through direct debit, real-time payments and open banking.

GoCardless processes US$130bn+ of payments annually, across 30+ countries; helping customers collect and send both recurring and one-off payments, without the chasing, stress or expensive fees. We use AI-powered solutions to improve payment success and reduce fraud. And, with open banking connectivity to over 2,500 banks, we help our customers make faster, more informed decisions.

We are headquartered in the UK with offices in London and Leeds, and additional locations in Australia, France, Ireland, Latvia, Portugal and the United States.

At GoCardless, we're all about supporting you! We’re committed to making our hiring process inclusive and accessible. If you need extra support or adjustments, reach out to your Talent Partner — we’re here to help!

And remember: we don’t expect you to meet every single requirement. If you’re excited by this role, we encourage you to apply!

Payment Intelligence

Our mission is to make GoCardless the best way to pay and be paid. Our ambition is to dramatically improve payment performance and safeguard our customers from bad outcomes like payment fraud and failures. You will help find data opportunities and areas to improve our payment intelligence products. You should be comfortable with large data sets, customer data, and machine learning principles. You’ll work alongside Product Managers, Engineers and Data Scientists in our two core Payment Intelligence teams to provide critical insights and help drive product direction.

Our existing Payment Intelligence Products are already one of the key reasons why customers choose GoCardless. These include Protect+, which defends customers from fraud, and Success+, which helps customers intelligently recover failed payments. You will need to own our understanding of these products and identify insights and opportunities to help us develop new intelligent features.

What You'll Do

At GoCardless analytics is at the core of everything we do and drives business performance through data insight. You will play a critical role partnering closely with stakeholders across the business (including product, operations, marketing, and commercial teams) in order to deliver actionable insights to influence decisions at every level of the organisation. The role is focussed on identifying value and driving change throughout the business derived from insights. Your insights will be turned into actions that will help drive the future of GoCardless. You will act as the bridge between the data, technical and business stakeholders informing strategic plans and will own and evolve a suite of reports designed to give a wide range of stakeholders access insights.


Own and iterate on experiments to generate user insight and drive business metrics, allowing the team to quickly take action on business opportunities.
Define how we quantitatively evaluate success, setting KPIs, designing tracking to measure what really matters, delving into the leading indicators of user behaviour
Influence the organisation to increase Product Data coverage and implement user tracking best practices - acting as a mentor to guide other members of the organisation to become data driven
Build models of user-segmentation, customer lifetime value etc in order to inform strategic product & commercial decisions
Continuously innovate on the practices and the tools we rely on to deliver the right insights at the right decision points (A/B Testing, Analysis (SQL), Visualisation (Looker), Tracking (Amplitude), Automation (Python), AI (Gemini)


What Excites You


Become a strategic partner and advisor for the business: you like to proactively seek out gaps in our product capabilities, suggest areas of improvement based on tangible data, as well as owning processes to run and improve testing from the tracking point of view when developing new features.
Act as a catalyst for change across the organization, driving the evolution of our data analytics standards, internal processes, and overall product data strategy to ensure scalability and operational excellence.
Translate complex technical findings into compelling, high-impact narratives. You will bridge the gap between technical data and commercial strategy, influencing product roadmaps and executive decision-making across the entire organization.
Stay current with AI and data trends to solve real-world problems. You like to leverage these technologies to enhance the payment experience, as well as to optimize data analysis, and deliver high-impact insights and strategic recommendations across the business.
Unlocking new layers of payment performance, from uncovering new fraud vectors to maximizing success rates
Serve as a cross-functional mentor, championing a data-driven mindset and elevating the analytical capabilities of team members throughout the company.


What Excites Us


You have a background working in similar fast paced product-driven teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed product decisions
You are a self-starter, focused on results, with a demonstrated background in using analytics to drive the understanding, growth, and success of a product
You have extensive experience with data visualisation tools mentioned above and develop compelling visualisations which influence decision making and business understanding
You have advanced SQL skills and intermediate Python skills & have experience leveraging AI capabilities to enhance data analysis, automate tasks, and improve decision-making processes
You have a background working in similar fast paced teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed decisions.
You are solution-oriented and able to own projects from start to finish. You are comfortable motivating stakeholders across the business and negotiating timelines to unblock yourself and make things happen!


Salary range: the expected base salary range for this position is between €32,000 - €48,000.

Base salary ranges are based on role, job level, location, and market data. Please note that whilst we strive to offer competitive compensation, our approach is to pay between the minimum and the mid-point of the pay range until performance can be assessed in role. Offers will take into account level of experience, interview assessment, budgets and parity between you and fellow employees at GoCardless doing similar work.

(some of) The good stuff


Wellbeing - stay healthy with dedicated support and medical cover
Work away scheme - gives you the option to work away from your country of residence for up to 90 days in any 12 month period
Adaptive Working - allows you to work flexibly, around your lifestyle
Equity - all permanently employed GCs get equity to help you make a valuable contribution
Parental leave - to suit everyone embarking on life's great adventure
Learning Budget - lead your own development with an annual learning budget
Time off - generous holiday allowance, + 3 annual volunteer days, + 4 annual business-wide wellness days (‘GC Fridays’)


Life at GoCardless

We're an organisation defined by our values; We start with why before we begin any project, to ensure it’s aligned with our mission. We act with integrity, always. We care deeply about what we do and we know it's essential that we be humble whilst we do it. Working this way creates the GC magic- the reason we all love showing up to work.

Diversity & Inclusion

As of April 2025, we had 806 employees (GeeCees) globally, with 524 based in the UK, 163 based in Latvia and 119 across our other offices.

To Ensure That We're Representative Of The World Around Us - And To Be Able To Review Relevant Benchmarks - We Ask GeeCees To Voluntarily Disclose Diversity Data. This Year, The Proportion Of GeeCees Providing Data Increased To 88% (up From 79% In 2024). With Regards To Diversity Within GoCardless, We Can See GeeCees Identifying As

Asian, Black, Mixed or Other — 25%

Neurodiverse — 9%

LGBTQIA+ — 9%

Disabled — 1%

Average age — 33

Female — 45%

Male — 55%

We’re rooting for you during your application and GoCardless aims to provide reasonable adjustments to make our recruitment process as remarkable and accessible as we can. Please speak to your Talent Partner if you need extra support.

If you want to learn more, you can read about our Employee Resource Groups and objectives here

Sustainability

We’re committed to reducing our impact on the environment, leaving a more sustainable world for future generations. Check out our sustainability action plan here.

Find out more about Life at GoCardless via Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn.

You will act as the bridge between the data, technical and business stakeholders informing strategic plans and will own and evolve a suite of reports designed to give a wide range of stakeholders access insights.

  • Own and iterate on experiments to generate user insight and drive business metrics, allowing the team to quickly take action on business opportunities.
  • Define how we quantitatively evaluate success, setting KPIs, designing tracking to measure what really matters, delving into the leading indicators of user behaviour
  • Influence the organisation to increase Product Data coverage and implement user tracking best practices - acting as a mentor to guide other members of the organisation to become data driven
  • Build models of user-segmentation, customer lifetime value etc in order to inform strategic product & commercial decisions
  • Continuously innovate on the practices and the tools we rely on to deliver the right insights at the right decision points (A/B Testing, Analysis (SQL), Visualisation (Looker), Tracking (Amplitude), Automation (Python), AI (Gemini)

What Excites You

  • Become a strategic partner and advisor for the business: you like to proactively seek out gaps in our product capabilities, suggest areas of improvement based on tangible data, as well as owning processes to run and improve testing from the tracking point of view when developing new features.
  • Act as a catalyst for change across the organization, driving the evolution of our data analytics standards, internal processes, and overall product data strategy to ensure scalability and operational excellence.
  • Translate complex technical findings into compelling, high-impact narratives. You will bridge the gap between technical data and commercial strategy, influencing product roadmaps and executive decision-making across the entire organization.
  • Stay current with AI and data trends to solve real-world problems. You like to leverage these technologies to enhance the payment experience, as well as to optimize data analysis, and deliver high-impact insights and strategic recommendations across the business.
  • Unlocking new layers of payment performance, from uncovering new fraud vectors to maximizing success rates
  • Serve as a cross-functional mentor, championing a data-driven mindset and elevating the analytical capabilities of team members throughout the company.

What Excites Us

  • You have a background working in similar fast paced product-driven teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed product decisions
  • You are a self-starter, focused on results, with a demonstrated background in using analytics to drive the understanding, growth, and success of a product
  • You have extensive experience with data visualisation tools mentioned above and develop compelling visualisations which influence decision making and business understanding
  • You have advanced SQL skills and intermediate Python skills & have experience leveraging AI capabilities to enhance data analysis, automate tasks, and improve decision-making processes
  • You have a background working in similar fast paced teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed decisions.
  • You are solution-oriented and able to own projects from start to finish. You are comfortable motivating stakeholders across the business and negotiating timelines to unblock yourself and make things happen!

Salary range: the expected base salary range for this position is between €32,000 - €48,000.

Base salary ranges are based on role, job level, location, and market data. Please note that whilst we strive to offer competitive compensation, our approach is to pay between the minimum and the mid-point of the pay range until performance can be assessed in role. Offers will take into account level of experience, interview assessment, budgets and parity between you and fellow employees at GoCardless doing similar work.

(some of) The good stuff

  • Wellbeing - stay healthy with dedicated support and medical cover
  • Work away scheme - gives you the option to work away from your country of residence for up to 90 days in any 12 month period
  • Adaptive Working - allows you to work flexibly, around your lifestyle
  • Equity - all permanently employed GCs get equity to help you make a valuable contribution
  • Parental leave - to suit everyone embarking on life's great adventure
  • Learning Budget - lead your own development with an annual learning budget
  • Time off - generous holiday allowance, + 3 annual volunteer days, + 4 annual business-wide wellness days (‘GC Fridays’)

Life at GoCardless

We're an organisation defined by our values; We start with why before we begin any project, to ensure it’s aligned with our mission. We act with integrity, always. We care deeply about what we do and we know it's essential that we be humble whilst we do it. Working this way creates the GC magic- the reason we all love showing up to work.

Diversity & Inclusion

As of April 2025, we had 806 employees (GeeCees) globally, with 524 based in the UK, 163 based in Latvia and 119 across our other offices.

To Ensure That We're Representative Of The World Around Us - And To Be Able To Review Relevant Benchmarks - We Ask GeeCees To Voluntarily Disclose Diversity Data. This Year, The Proportion Of GeeCees Providing Data Increased To 88% (up From 79% In 2024). With Regards To Diversity Within GoCardless, We Can See GeeCees Identifying As

Asian, Black, Mixed or Other — 25%

Neurodiverse — 9%

LGBTQIA+ — 9%

Disabled — 1%

Average age — 33

Female — 45%

Male — 55%

We’re rooting for you during your application and GoCardless aims to provide reasonable adjustments to make our recruitment process as remarkable and accessible as we can. Please speak to your Talent Partner if you need extra support.

If you want to learn more, you can read about our Employee Resource Groups and objectives here

Sustainability

We’re committed to reducing our impact on the environment, leaving a more sustainable world for future generations. Check out our sustainability action plan here.

Find out more about Life at GoCardless via Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Why this role matters

The Data & Analytics team plays a pivotal role in the success of our CRM Marketing function and whole Marketing division by providing high-quality experimentation and timely analytical insights as well as data-backed recommendations, maintaining data operations and ensuring data quality.

The role is based in our office in Limassol, Cyprus. In case of relocation, we offer full relocation support for you and your family to make your move smooth and worry-free.

What you'll actually do


Design and execute A/B tests, set hypotheses, measure experiment results, apply statistical methodologies and provide actionable recommendations to optimise CRM campaigns.
Perform data analysis from clients and partners to understand user behavior, identify trends, and uncover opportunities for growth and retention.
Work closely with CRM Marketing and other Marketing teams to gather requirements, align on objectives, and define the scope of data tasks.
Understand the CRM Marketing landscape to identify areas of improvement in automated journeys and channels for greater efficiency and impact.
Develop, maintain, and monitor data-driven processes, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and quality across CRM systems.
Provide actionable, data-driven insights, research, and quantitative analyses on both regular and ad hoc basis to support decision-making.
Support the integration of new data sources into the marketing automation system, ensuring seamless deployment of use cases.
Apply advanced analytics and predictive modeling techniques to forecast customer behavior and identify monetization opportunities.


Who We’re Looking For


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Mathematics, Statistics, Physics or a related field
Data Analytics 4+ years (With strong focus on A/B testing)
Strong experience in Python
Strong experience in SQL (complex queries, analytic functions)
Excellent understanding of mathematical statistics
Ability to translate business problems into quantitative analyses and produce insights
Proven ability to efficiently formalize and visualize the results of the data analysis
English language is a must; any other language will be considered as an advantage
Ability to communicate complex statistical & mathematical concepts to a non-technical audience
Ability to organize and prioritize tasks efficiently and to handle multiple, simultaneous deadlines
Ability to adapt to requirement changes when needed and produce fast solutions
Excellent verbal and written communication, upper-intermediate English
Proactive, detail-oriented, and capable of handling multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment


What We Offer Along The Way


Competitive salary and annual performance bonus
Full relocation support for you and your family — flights, housing, visas, and legal assistance included
Top-tier health insurance with full family coverage — medical, dental, vision, mental health — plus life insurance for peace of mind
Unlimited learning opportunities: external courses, English lessons, career and leadership development
Education allowance covering school and kindergarten fees
21 working days of annual leave, plus public holidays and fully paid sick, maternity, and paternity leave
Employee appreciation program: branded gifts, birthday day-offs, celebration budgets for weddings, newborns, and milestones
“Get to know Team” trips — meet colleagues across our global hubs, along with company-wide offsites that raise the bar
Employee share scheme — grow with us
Branded MINI Cooper Countryman company car and private parking
Free in-house sports clubs, Sanctum Club gym access, and jet skis
Access to a Corporate doctor
Exclusive discount program with cafes, gyms, and local services
Expat tax perks: up to 50% income tax exemption
Support with the naturalisation process for relocated employees


What Your Journey Looks Like


Intro call with Recruiter (30 minutes)
English check (for all non-native English speakers)
Test Task (45 minutes)
Tech interview (90 minutes)
Behavioural interview (60 minutes)
Team fit interview (60 minutes)


Sounds like you? Apply.

Please note: We occasionally amend or withdraw Exness jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Before applying, you are advised to read our data protection policy. This policy describes the processing that may be associated with your personal data and informs you that your personal data may be transferred to Exness/Exness Group companies around the world. Exness Group and its approved recruitment consultants will never ask you for a fee to process or consider your application for a career with Exness. Anyone who demands such a fee is not an authorized Exness representative and you are strongly advised to refuse any such demand.

At Exness, we're an equal opportunity employer where every individual is valued. No matter your race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or disability, we welcome you. As an international fintech company, we embrace the richness of our diverse team, respecting each individual and promoting gender equality for all genders in our workforce.

Title: Web Analyst
Description:
We're looking for a Web Analyst with strong GA4, GTM, and SQL expertise who can turn data into actionable insights. You'll build dashboards, analyze performance, and tell compelling data stories that help teams make smarter decisions. If you're independent, collaborative, and enjoy working in an inclusive environment, we'd love to hear from you.




In this role, you are going to:

Establish, maintain, review, and continuously optimize Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Google Tag Manager (GTM) configurations to ensure absolute data accuracy and integrity
Analyze and optimize dataLayer coverage and structure to ensure robust, scalable data collection
Create and implement measurement strategies perfectly aligned with business goals, defining custom dimensions, metrics, and advanced event tracking
Utilize GA4 with all its functionalities to perform deep-dive analyses, uncover trends, and build customized reports and dashboards that turn complex data into actionable business insights
Apply familiarity with modern marketing and tracking practices to safely implement, troubleshoot, and thoroughly test tracking scripts
Partner with marketing, product, and development teams to promote data-driven decision-making and ensure tracking specs are met
Collaborate with cross-functional teams on data analysis and segmentation; support A/B testing initiatives where relevant
Contribute to broader business insights analysis, working alongside the analytics team to connect web data to wider business performance questions
Keep up-to-date with the latest privacy regulations, GA4 features, and analytics best practices




To be successful in this role, you need to have:

Demonstrated experience setting up, maintaining, reviewing, and optimizing complex Google Tag Manager and GA4 environments
Strong understanding of dataLayer architecture, including the ability to analyze coverage gaps and guide developers on optimization
Strong data analysis skills, with the ability to leverage the full suite of GA4 functionalities to answer complex business questions
Hands-on experience implementing various marketing and tracking scripts, with a disciplined approach to testing and QA
Strong SQL skills for querying and analyzing raw GA4 export data
Excellent communication skills and proficiency in spoken and written English - capable of translating intricate tracking setups and raw data into clear, stakeholder-friendly insight




Preferred skills and experience:

Familiarity with iGaming metrics and KPIs
Experience working with Google BigQuery
Knowledge of Google Data Studio / Looker Studio for creating automated, dynamic dashboards
Familiarity with Google Search Console (GSC) to connect search performance with on-site user behavior
Sufficient knowledge of A/B testing and CRO methodologies to support other roles with advanced data validation and analysis
Solid understanding of web development technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and how they impact tracking and DOM scraping
Google Analytics or Google Tag Manager certifications




We will show our gratitude by:

Offering working tools of employees´ own choice - Company makes sure everyone feels comfortable in the office and has all the necessary tools one might need
Creating an exciting and fun international English-speaking environment
Working in a modern office in Ülemiste City
Organizing different company and team events
Offering extra sports compensation via Stebby + private health insurance
Creating an opportunity to learn and develop
Offering a competitive salary based on experience and a benefits package




If this seems like a challenge you are looking for, apply and let’s have a chat!




Sounds great but what do we do?

We are a growing company focused on the use of data to benefit e-commerce operations. Our Data Scientists provide decision-making assistance and models to improve operational efficiency and customer experience. The Analytics team provides insights on specific topics. Our Business Intelligence team makes information accessible. Together with the right technology we deliver the foundation for highly automated and effective CRM solutions.

Learn more about NabuMinds: https://nabuminds.com




As we strive to achieve our mission we reaffirm our commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI). We are proud to employ people of all backgrounds who possess the talent, energy, and focus to accelerate our vision forward and to tell our story. Each employee is encouraged to bring their whole self to work, knowing that NabuMinds values individuality as much as we value collaboration.

Title: BI & Data Developer
Description:
BI & Data Developer

📍 Malta | Hybrid Working Available




A well-established financial services organisation is looking to hire a Business Intelligence / Data Developer to join its growing Data & Information Technology team.




This is an exciting opportunity for a data professional who enjoys working with modern Microsoft data technologies, building scalable ETL solutions, developing reporting platforms, and supporting business intelligence initiatives that drive strategic decision-making.




You will play a key role in designing, developing, and maintaining data solutions that support analytics, reporting, and operational efficiency across the organisation.




The Opportunity

As a Business Intelligence / Data Developer, you will work closely with business stakeholders and technical teams to build reliable data pipelines, reporting solutions, and data warehouse components that enable data-driven decision making.

This role offers exposure to a variety of projects across data integration, reporting, business intelligence, and data management within a highly regulated and technology-driven environment.







What You'll Be Doing

Design, develop, and maintain ETL processes using Microsoft SSIS, SQL Server, and Python
Build and maintain database stored procedures and data integration solutions
Generate and manage XML and CSV exports for internal and external systems
Develop and support business reports using SSRS and Power BI
Ensure data quality, integrity, consistency, and security across data platforms
Design and enhance data warehouse solutions to support reporting and analytics requirements
Document business processes, data flows, and technical solutions
Collaborate with business users to understand reporting and data requirements
Support testing, deployment, and ongoing maintenance of data solutions
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the data and BI environment







What We're Looking For

Bachelor's Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field
3–5 years of experience in data development using Microsoft technologies
Previous experience within the financial services sector is considered an advantage
Strong experience with SQL Server Data Tools (SSDT)
Solid understanding of SQL development and data modelling concepts
Hands-on experience developing and maintaining SSIS ETL solutions
Experience creating and maintaining SSRS reports
Strong Python development experience
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality
Good written and verbal communication skills




Nice to Have

Experience with DBT
Experience building Power BI dashboards and reports
Experience developing .NET applications
Experience with web application development
Exposure to data warehousing and business intelligence best practices




What's On Offer

Hybrid and flexible working arrangements
Ongoing professional development and career growth opportunities
Fully subsidised study loans and support for further education
Private health insurance, with the option to extend coverage to dependants
Life insurance and personal accident cover
Annual wellness allowance (€400)
Additional birthday leave
Annual salary reviews and performance appraisals
Employee wellbeing support, including professional counselling services
Preferential employee financial products and benefits
Corporate discounts and additional employee perks
Stable and supportive working environment within a respected financial institution




This is an excellent opportunity for a BI and Data professional looking to work on meaningful projects, develop their technical expertise, and contribute to the evolution of a modern data environment.




For more information, contact Kristoffer Parkhagen confidentially at +356 9999 8469 or send your CV to kristoffer.parkhagen@boston-link.com

Title: Business Analyst - Masterdata Governance
Description:
Job description:



Business Analyst – Masterdata Governance

Hybrid working

 

 

Tirlán

Tirlán, is a world-class food and nutrition co-operative, with a diverse portfolio of quality ingredients, leading consumer and agri brands.

Tirlán owns leading brands such as Avonmore, Kilmeaden Cheese, Millac, Solmiko, Truly Grass Fed, Premier Milk, Wexford, mymilkman.ie and GAIN Animal Nutrition.

We nurture a talented team across Ireland, the US, MEA and China, delivering annual revenues of circa €3 billion, and circa 2,000 people to manage our network of 11 processing plants and 52 agri branches. Our purpose is to nourish the world, while safeguarding our environment for future generations.

 

Role Profile

We have an opportunity for a Business Analyst to support implementation of a Master Data Governance strategy, with a strong focus on SAP master data analysis, data quality control, governance assurance, and process improvement. This is a highly analytical, control-focused role working across business and IT functions to strengthen the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of master data.

 

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Quality

Analyse SAP master data to identify data quality issues, including missing values, inconsistencies, duplicates, outliers and invalid records
Perform root-cause analysis to understand if issues are caused by system configuration, process gaps, control weakness, user behaviour or data migration activity
Support data cleansing and remediation activity, validating outcomes through profiling, reconciliation and post-remediation checks

Data Controls & Governance

Analyse large datasets from SAP and integrated systems to identify trends, exceptions, control gaps, and data integrity risks
Design and maintain data quality controls, exception checks, and validation routines to support proactive master data governance
Support master data cleansing, remediation, and ongoing control monitoring across key master data objects

Analytics & Control Monitoring

Use SAP Analytics Cloud, BI, Excel, and other analytical tools to monitor data quality, control effectiveness, and remediation progress
Provide insight-led analysis on recurring issues, control failures, process inefficiencies, and opportunities to improve master data governance

 

Requirements

Bachelor’s Degree (Business Information Systems, Accountancy or IT) and/or relevant experience and training
Minimum 3–5 years’ experience working in SAP or enterprise data environments.
Proven experience analysing SAP data, interpreting table-level information, and identifying data quality or control issues
Strong capability in data analysis, profiling, cleansing, root-cause investigation, and governance control techniques
Advanced Excel and data analysis capability, with the ability to manage and interpret large, complex datasets.
Experience developing data quality monitoring, exception analysis, or control assurance solutions
Exposure to SAP testing, data migration, or transformation programmes
Strong analytical and problem-solving ability
High attention to detail and data accuracy
Experience managing large, complex datasets
Ability to engage effectively with technical and business stakeholders

 

We offer

Hybrid working (flex Fridays)
Free parking incl. EV charging – where applicable
On-site gym free to use for employees
Parenting room
Work/life balance and collaborative work culture
Career progression opportunities

 

About us

Through our expertise in research and development, customer insights, food safety and state-of-the-art manufacturing we bring innovative food and nutrition solutions to a global market.  Our new and vibrant headquarters in Kilkenny City operates as a collaboration hub where multi-functional teams come together in a hybrid work environment.

 

Commitment to Diversity & Inclusion
We believe in the value of difference. We know that different perspectives, life experience and backgrounds can drive innovation and deliver growth.

We welcome people from all walks of life without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, or disability status. 

 

Join our Talent Pool

Considering a new career in Tirlán but this isn’t the role for you? Join our talent community and sign up for job notifications at www.tirlan.com/careers

 

Tirlán is a Values Based Organisation

Job description:

Business Analyst – Masterdata Governance

Hybrid working

 

 

Tirlán

Tirlán, is a world-class food and nutrition co-operative, with a diverse portfolio of quality ingredients, leading consumer and agri brands.

Tirlán owns leading brands such as Avonmore, Kilmeaden Cheese, Millac, Solmiko, Truly Grass Fed, Premier Milk, Wexford, mymilkman.ie and GAIN Animal Nutrition.

We nurture a talented team across Ireland, the US, MEA and China, delivering annual revenues of circa €3 billion, and circa 2,000 people to manage our network of 11 processing plants and 52 agri branches. Our purpose is to nourish the world, while safeguarding our environment for future generations.

 

Role Profile

We have an opportunity for a Business Analyst to support implementation of a Master Data Governance strategy, with a strong focus on SAP master data analysis, data quality control, governance assurance, and process improvement. This is a highly analytical, control-focused role working across business and IT functions to strengthen the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of master data.

 

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Quality

  • Analyse SAP master data to identify data quality issues, including missing values, inconsistencies, duplicates, outliers and invalid records
  • Perform root-cause analysis to understand if issues are caused by system configuration, process gaps, control weakness, user behaviour or data migration activity
  • Support data cleansing and remediation activity, validating outcomes through profiling, reconciliation and post-remediation checks

Data Controls & Governance

  • Analyse large datasets from SAP and integrated systems to identify trends, exceptions, control gaps, and data integrity risks
  • Design and maintain data quality controls, exception checks, and validation routines to support proactive master data governance
  • Support master data cleansing, remediation, and ongoing control monitoring across key master data objects

Analytics & Control Monitoring

  • Use SAP Analytics Cloud, BI, Excel, and other analytical tools to monitor data quality, control effectiveness, and remediation progress
  • Provide insight-led analysis on recurring issues, control failures, process inefficiencies, and opportunities to improve master data governance

 

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s Degree (Business Information Systems, Accountancy or IT) and/or relevant experience and training
  • Minimum 3–5 years’ experience working in SAP or enterprise data environments.
  • Proven experience analysing SAP data, interpreting table-level information, and identifying data quality or control issues
  • Strong capability in data analysis, profiling, cleansing, root-cause investigation, and governance control techniques
  • Advanced Excel and data analysis capability, with the ability to manage and interpret large, complex datasets.
  • Experience developing data quality monitoring, exception analysis, or control assurance solutions
  • Exposure to SAP testing, data migration, or transformation programmes
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving ability
  • High attention to detail and data accuracy
  • Experience managing large, complex datasets
  • Ability to engage effectively with technical and business stakeholders

 

We offer

  • Hybrid working (flex Fridays)
  • Free parking incl. EV charging – where applicable
  • On-site gym free to use for employees
  • Parenting room
  • Work/life balance and collaborative work culture
  • Career progression opportunities

 

About us

Through our expertise in research and development, customer insights, food safety and state-of-the-art manufacturing we bring innovative food and nutrition solutions to a global market.  Our new and vibrant headquarters in Kilkenny City operates as a collaboration hub where multi-functional teams come together in a hybrid work environment.

 

Commitment to Diversity & Inclusion
We believe in the value of difference. We know that different perspectives, life experience and backgrounds can drive innovation and deliver growth.

We welcome people from all walks of life without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, or disability status. 

 

Join our Talent Pool

Considering a new career in Tirlán but this isn’t the role for you? Join our talent community and sign up for job notifications at www.tirlan.com/careers

 

Tirlán is a Values Based Organisation

Title: Manufacturing Data Product Analyst
Description:
At Deep DSG, we help global manufacturers unlock the full value of their operational data through modern industrial architecture, connected systems, and advanced analytics.

As we continue to expand our Data Enablement capabilities, we are seeking a Manufacturing Data Product Analyst to support the development and delivery of industrial data solutions that drive smarter, more connected manufacturing operations.

This role will focus on the design, configuration, and implementation of industrial data products, data pipelines, and integration solutions. Working closely with Data Enablement Leads, OT engineers, IT teams, and manufacturing stakeholders, you will help transform operational requirements into scalable data solutions that improve visibility, decision-making, and enterprise connectivity.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone with a strong technical foundation in manufacturing systems, industrial data platforms, and system integration who is looking to further develop their expertise in modern industrial data architectures, including Unified Namespace (UNS), DataOps, MQTT, and enterprise data platforms.

Requirements


Support the design, development, and deployment of Unified Namespace (UNS), Industrial DataOps, and industrial data solutions.
Assist in translating manufacturing and business requirements into scalable data models and integration architectures.
Develop, configure, and maintain industrial data products that support manufacturing, quality, maintenance, and business intelligence initiatives.
Design and support data pipelines that connect plant-floor systems with enterprise applications and analytics platforms.
Implement data transformation, contextualization, validation, and governance processes.
Configure and support industrial data brokers, messaging infrastructure, historians, and integration platforms.
Collaborate with Data Enablement Leads, manufacturing teams, OT engineers, IT personnel, and implementation partners on project delivery.
Contribute to the development and adoption of technical standards, best practices, and data governance frameworks.
Support testing, deployment, troubleshooting, and continuous improvement activities across Data Enablement initiatives.
Stay informed on emerging technologies and industry best practices within industrial data management and smart manufacturing.
Experience working with manufacturing systems, industrial automation, or industrial data solutions.
Knowledge of industrial data architectures, data modelling, and system integration principles.
Familiarity with Unified Namespace (UNS), Industrial DataOps, MQTT, or similar industrial data frameworks.
Experience developing or supporting data pipelines, data integration, or industrial software solutions.
Exposure to platforms such as HighByte Intelligence Hub, HiveMQ, AVEVA PI System (OSIsoft PI), or comparable technologies.
Understanding of manufacturing operations and OT/IT environments.
Familiarity with industrial middleware, historians, and enterprise integration platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to work through technical challenges independently and as part of a team.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
Degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Engineering, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience.

Title: Data Analyst (12 month FTC)
Description:
About Us

GoCardless is a global bank payment company. Over 100,000 businesses, from start-ups to household names, use GoCardless to collect and send payments through direct debit, real-time payments and open banking.

GoCardless processes US$130bn+ of payments annually, across 30+ countries; helping customers collect and send both recurring and one-off payments, without the chasing, stress or expensive fees. We use AI-powered solutions to improve payment success and reduce fraud. And, with open banking connectivity to over 2,500 banks, we help our customers make faster, more informed decisions.

We are headquartered in the UK with offices in London and Leeds, and additional locations in Australia, France, Ireland, Latvia, Portugal and the United States.

At GoCardless, we're all about supporting you! We’re committed to making our hiring process inclusive and accessible. If you need extra support or adjustments, reach out to your Talent Partner — we’re here to help!

And remember: we don’t expect you to meet every single requirement. If you’re excited by this role, we encourage you to apply!

The role

As an Operations Analyst, you will own the data and reporting layer across our Merchant Operations function - replacing manual spreadsheet workflows with automated, self-serve dashboards and scalable data models. You will design and build the analytics infrastructure that our KYC, Customer Support, Technical Operations, Fraud Operations & Monitoring, Complaints and Quality teams rely on to make decisions every day. Working closely with operational managers and our data platform, you will translate messy, multi-source data into clean, trusted reporting - and have the mindset to build things once, properly, rather than maintain endless spreadsheet exports.

This position is located in our HQ2 in Riga, which was recently honored with the prestigious CEE Business Services Award for being the 'Most Vibrant Employer – Latvia,' offering you the chance to thrive in a dynamic and engaging workplace.

In This Role You Will


Build and maintain Looker dashboards and LookML models that serve KYC, CS, Technical Operations, Fraud & Monitoring, Complaints and Quality teams - replacing manual, recurring spreadsheet reports with live, self-serve analytics
Write and optimise BigQuery SQL to power operational reporting: ticket volumes, SLA compliance, agent performance, CSAT, handle times, and quality scores
Audit existing spreadsheet-based workflows and systematically eliminate them through automation and integrated reporting in Looker and Zendesk
Design and implement Zendesk reporting logic (Explore, APIs, or direct querying) that gives operational managers accurate, timely visibility into their queues and team performance
Partner with operational leads across all five teams to define, instrument, and document their key metrics - ensuring a single source of truth across the function
Deliver ad hoc analysis and data pulls to support operational decisions, escalations, and planning cycles


What Excites You


Eliminating spreadsheets - you get genuine satisfaction from replacing a 40-tab Excel file with a clean, automated dashboard that updates itself
Building in Looker - you enjoy modelling data in LookML and know how to structure explores that non-technical stakeholders can use
Writing SQL - BigQuery is your natural environment; you write clean, efficient queries and know how to optimise them
Working across teams - you like understanding how different operational teams work and translating their reporting needs into something scalable
Ownership - you want to own the analytics layer end to end, not just fulfil ticket requests, and you care about data quality and trust
Continuous improvement - you proactively identify where processes are fragile or manual and propose better approaches
You deliver not just plain data, but provide actionable dashboards and reports


What Excites Us


You have strong BigQuery SQL skills and hands-on experience building in Looker, including writing LookML
You have deep familiarity with service desk/help desk systems e.g. Zendesk, Zendesk reporting - whether through Explore, APIs, or direct data access - and understand how Zendesk data is structured
You have a track record of automating manual reporting workflows and reducing dependence on spreadsheets
You are comfortable working across multiple operational teams simultaneously, managing competing priorities and stakeholder expectations
You have experience with BI tools and are comfortable switching between platforms
Familiarity with Zendesk and Bob integrations, or similar tooling ecosystems, is a plus
You can communicate clearly with non-technical stakeholders and translate ambiguous requests into well-defined data problems


Salary range - the expected base salary range for this position is between €2,200 – €3,300 (gross) per month

Base salary ranges are based on role, job level, location, and market data. Please note that whilst we strive to offer competitive compensation, our approach is to pay between the minimum and the mid-point (€2,200 - €2,750) of the pay range until performance can be assessed in role. Offers will take into account level of experience, interview assessment, budgets and parity between you and fellow employees at GoCardless doing similar work.

(some of) The good stuff


Wellbeing - stay healthy with dedicated support and medical cover
Work away scheme - gives you the option to work away from your country of residence for up to 90 days in any 12 month period
Adaptive Working - allows you to work flexibly, around your lifestyle
Equity - all permanently employed GCs get equity to help you make a valuable contribution
Parental leave - to suit everyone embarking on life's great adventure
Learning Budget - lead your own development with an annual learning budget
Time off - generous holiday allowance, + 3 annual volunteer days, + 4 annual business-wide wellness days (‘GC Fridays’)


Life at GoCardless

We're an organisation defined by our values; We start with why before we begin any project, to ensure it’s aligned with our mission. We act with integrity, always. We care deeply about what we do and we know it's essential that we be humble whilst we do it. Working this way creates the GC magic- the reason we all love showing up to work.

Diversity & Inclusion

As of April 2025, we had 806 employees (GeeCees) globally, with 524 based in the UK, 163 based in Latvia and 119 across our other offices.

To Ensure That We're Representative Of The World Around Us - And To Be Able To Review Relevant Benchmarks - We Ask GeeCees To Voluntarily Disclose Diversity Data. This Year, The Proportion Of GeeCees Providing Data Increased To 88% (up From 79% In 2024). With Regards To Diversity Within GoCardless, We Can See GeeCees Identifying As

Asian, Black, Mixed or Other — 25%

Neurodiverse — 9%

LGBTQIA+ — 9%

Disabled — 1%

Average age — 33

Female — 45%

Male — 55%

We’re rooting for you during your application and GoCardless aims to provide reasonable adjustments to make our recruitment process as remarkable and accessible as we can. Please speak to your Talent Partner if you need extra support.

If you want to learn more, you can read about our Employee Resource Groups and objectives here

Sustainability

We’re committed to reducing our impact on the environment, leaving a more sustainable world for future generations. Check out our sustainability action plan here.

Find out more about Life at GoCardless via Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re a team of 500+ professionals who develop cutting-edge proxy and web data scraping solutions for thousands of the world’s best known businesses, including Fortune 500 companies.

What’s in store for you:

You’ll be developing complex products with high coding standards, maintaining our own infrastructure, handling petabytes of data, and solving challenges on a daily basis. We got you covered with a team of strong professionals to support you, a well-built tech stack, and loads of ownership.

In this role, you’ll:


Develop a solid understanding of the relationships between the behaviour of our customers and our growth drivers through data analysis and modelling
Create and maintain self-service dashboards for stakeholders across business, using PowerBI
Utilize analytical tools (SQL, Python, Google Sheets) to prepare and analyze data, supporting key decision-making processes
Carry out ad-hoc analysis to address stakeholder questions and effectively communicating your findings
Ensure data integrity and accuracy by performing regular data audits and validation
Collaborate with business users to determine KPIs and critical metrics to monitor for new business lines
Build forecast models to support business development
Deliver actionable insights and data-driven recommendations



Your skills & experience:


2+ years of experience working on business problems in a Data Analyst/Business Analyst role
Strong knowledge of SQL
Hands-on experience building effective, easy-to-navigate dashboards, ideally with PowerBI (or other data visualization tools such as Tableau, Qlik Sense, Looker, Grafana)
Fluency in English and Lithuanian
Ability to generate actionable insights and recommendations from data analysis with an understanding of how these insights can drive business impact
Problem solving and being a team player.
Pragmatic and impact-focused - you will choose Excel over Python if it is a better solution for the problem and vice-versa
Proven ability to formulate and test hypotheses about business performance
Strong communication skills to effectively collaborate with stakeholders and present findings



Tech Stack:


Google Cloud Platform (GCS, BQ) for storage and warehousing;
dbt and SQL for data modelling;
Python for automation;
PowerBI for data visualisation;
Airflow for orchestration;
Any tool you like for ad-hoc analysis
We don't expect you to master or know all of these technologies today



Salary & Benefits:


Gross salary: 3200 - 4500 EUR/month. Keep in mind that we are open to discussing a different salary based on your skills and experience
Growth & Learning: 40+ internal learning options, external conferences, mentorship, and year-round knowledge-sharing
Health & Well-being: Private health insurance, psychotherapy, on-site well-being consultants, 24/7 gym access, and a wellness app
Celebration & Community: Team events, an overseas workation, quarterly team-building budgets, and plenty of ways to mark milestones together
Plus bonus vacation days, paid life-moment days off, barista coffee, and all the tools you need. See the full list of benefits here: https://career.oxylabs.io/blog/our-work-benefits/



Up for the challenge? Let’s talk!

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Eeze is a Malta-based, technology-driven company that has been developing world-leading online gaming solutions since 1999. Underpinned by a history that directly interconnects with the rise of online gambling, the company has been a key pioneer within the international market. Over the years, Eeze has amassed a world-class team of gambling specialists who remain at the frontiers of innovation within this ever-evolving industry. With a focus on being an international B2B Slots and Live Casino provider with our Eeze brand, we have over 900 talented team members based in various locations across Europe, with our headquarters and studio in Malta - Europe’s leading iGaming hub.




Role overview

The Business Analyst is responsible for providing data-driven insights, performance analysis, and structured reporting to support decision-making across Live Casino operations. The role focuses on analysing studio performance, identifying trends, and delivering actionable recommendations to key stakeholders, including the Director of Live Games Operations and Partner Studio Operations Manager.




In addition, the Business Analyst is responsible for compiling and maintaining key operational documentation, ensuring alignment with SOPs, version control standards, and certification requirements. While supporting testing activities where required, the primary focus of the role is to act as the analytical and governance backbone of the Strategy unit.




Key Responsibilities




Performance Analysis & Reporting

Produce monthly studio performance reports covering:

Operational performance.
Incident trends.
Game Presenter-related insights (where relevant).
Studio comparisons.

Provide clear, structured recommendations based on findings.




Present insights to:

Director of Live Games Operations.
Partner Studio Operations Manager.
Internal Strategy stakeholders.




Data Analysis & Insights

Analyse data across:

Studio performance.
Incident patterns.
Operational efficiency.
Player-related trends (where available).




Identify:

Root causes recurring issues.
Performance gaps.
Improvement opportunities.




Dashboard Development & Maintenance

Develop and maintain performance dashboards to provide visibility on:

Key operational KPIs.
Studio-level performance.
Trends over time.




Ensure dashboards are:

Accurate.
User-friendly.
Aligned with business needs.




Documentation & Certification Support

Compile and maintain documentation related to:

New dealer clients.
Operational flows.
Incident handling and settlement procedures.




Ensure documentation:

Follows structured version control and history tracking.
It is aligned with approved SOPs.
Supports certification and audit requirements.




Work closely with relevant stakeholders to ensure documentation is:

Accurate.
Up to date.
Consistently applied across operations.




Recommendations & Continuous Improvement:
