Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Ocean Finance is a portfolio brand of Intelligent Lending Limited, a consumer credit broker powerhouse operating the TotallyMoney, Ocean Finance, CredAbility Luv Car Loans, and Binq brands. Our mission is to become the UK’s leading credit platform for people not served by the mainstream — helping them make informed decisions, access the right products, and improve their financial wellbeing over their lifetime. We do this by helping our customers make informed decisions and access personal finance products.

If our mission sounds interesting, please keep reading!




You’ll join the Central Intelligence Unit (CIU) within our Data team. CIU sources the most relevant intelligence and ensures its optimal deployment — through machine learning, analytics and decisioning that shape product and corporate strategy. Specifically, you’ll sit in the Ocean Squad, working alongside data scientists, analytics and AI engineers on the analytical frameworks, decisioning models that both elevate our product, and propel our marketing efforts for Ocean.




This is a hybrid role that sits between analytics engineering and data analysis. Roughly half your time will be spent building and maintaining the data foundations the team relies on — clean, reliable, well-modelled datasets — and the other half turning those datasets into dazzling dashboards and gleaning insights that directly inform business decisions.

It’s ideal for someone early in their career (a recent graduate, up to ~2 years’ experience) who enjoys both the engineering craft of making data effective as well as trustworthy, and the curiosity of digging into it to answer real commercial questions. You’ll learn modern data tooling and analytics practice in a fast-moving environment, with strong support from experienced engineers and scientists around you.

The mission statement of CIU is “To improve customer and business outcomes by ensuring the sourcing of the most relevant intelligence, and its optimal deployment”. We aim to be a leading force in creating value for the customer and the business, through best-in-class analytics and decisioning that shape the product offering, as well as corporate strategy.




A day in the life of this role could look like…

Analyzing complex datasets to uncover customer behavior patterns and product performance insights, translating these findings into clear and actionable commercial recommendations for stakeholders
Presenting insights and recommendations to senior stakeholders, telling compelling stories with data that drive action
Writing and optimizing SQL queries to extract and analyze data from multiple sources, ensuring accuracy and efficiency
Collaborating with business teams to understand their analytical needs and develop solutions that drive business value
Designing and helping to build automated dashboards and reports in tools like Power BI or Databricks to track key business metrics and support data-driven decision making
Build and maintain data models and transformation pipelines that turn raw data into clean, well-documented, analysis-ready datasets.
Creating and maintaining BI chatbots that leverage curated datasets to empower non-technical business stakeholders to self-serve
Analysing market trends and competitive landscape




You’ll also have the chance to get involved in other projects like:

Evaluating the potential impact of greenfield projects, such as the integration of new products, verticals, or customer journeys
Set up analytical framework for new products or enhance the existing ones by suggesting new KPIs / analyses
Understanding the key drivers behind past business performance and succinctly articulating the story to the board
Contributing to the design and implementation of our machine learning and predictive analytics initiatives




What we’re looking for

A degree in a quantitative or technical discipline (e.g. Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Engineering) — or equivalent practical experience.
0–2 years’ experience in an analytics, data or engineering role, including internships, placements or strong personal/academic projects.
Solid SQL skills, and confidence working with data to answer questions.
Some exposure to a scripting language (ideally Python) for data manipulation and analysis.
An eye for data quality and structure — you care about getting things right, not just an answer.
Clear written and verbal communication; able to explain analysis simply.
Curiosity and commercial awareness — you want to understand the “why” behind the numbers.
Proactive and ownership-minded; comfortable in a fast-paced environment.




Nice to have

Experience with a cloud data warehouse / lakehouse (e.g. Databricks, BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift).
Familiarity with a BI / visualisation tool (e.g. Looker, Power BI, Tableau).
Exposure to version control (Git) and collaborative analytics workflows.
An interest in the credit market, financial services or consumer finance.




Most importantly, you’ll live and breathe our values: Openness, Trust, Humility, Professionalism, Respect, and Passion.




Benefits

Pay you competitively and review your salary every year
Welcome you into our awesome team (dare we say the best? We do, yes!)
Bring the kind of vibe where we want you to share your ideas, even if it’s not “your area”
Provide you with all the kit you need to work from home. Of course, you can also work from our Manchester or Cyprus offices, too.
Help you plan for life after work with our pension scheme
Take care of your health with eye test vouchers, and our employee assistance programme
Give you 25 days holiday (to start with), as well as local public holidays and a day off on your birthday
Support you with training, mentoring and anything else you need to further your development and hit your career goals
Invite you to regular online and in-person team socials, as well as the occasional team retreat somewhere exotic
Help you help others with optional “community” days for volunteering
Provide you flexibility of benefit with the Ocean Health Plan

Title: Staff Data Analyst
Description:
About Analog Devices

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X).

Staff Data Analyst, Engineering Analytics

Location: Limerick, Ireland

Overview

As a Staff Data Analyst on the Engineering Enablement team, you will own the development and evolution of analytics dashboards that surface insights on product development and engineering tool usage. These dashboards synthesize large amounts of engineering data from various sources and are broadly used by both engineering teams and executive leadership. You will rely on sound judgment, curiosity, and strong communication skills to translate ambiguous engineering workflows into well-defined metrics and clear, meaningful reports.

Responsibilities


Engage directly with engineering domain teams (design, verification, IP, AI etc.) to understand workflows, identify high-value data sources, and define key telemetry and productivity metrics.
Define data schemas, grain, and transformation logic needed for each data source, and communicate those requirements clearly to the Central Data team for implementation.
Design and maintain a Power BI semantic model (data model, relationships, DAX measures) that serves as a shared analytical foundation across multiple thin reports.
Build, publish, and iterate on Power BI dashboards and reports that deliver clear insights to engineering managers and leadership.
Collaborate with Central Data team to scope, prioritize, and validate pipeline work; serve as the functional owner of data correctness and metric definitions.
Document metric definitions, data lineage, and report logic to ensure institutional knowledge is durable and auditable.
Identify opportunities to expand impact — new data sources, new engineering personas, new use cases — and drive those through requirements definition and delivery.


Qualifications


8+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or a related field.
Degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Business Analytics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field.
Expert-level skills in Power BI or similar tool (Qlikview, Tableau, DataStudio, etc.), including data modeling, DAX, report design, workspace/deployment pipeline management.
Strong SQL skills; ability to write and review transformation logic even when not directly authoring pipelines.
Demonstrated experience working directly with non-technical stakeholders to define metrics and translate domain knowledge into data requirements.
Strong written and verbal communication skills; comfortable presenting findings and influencing priorities with engineering and executive leadership.
Experience owning an analytics product end-to-end — from requirements through delivery and ongoing maintenance.


Additional Qualifications (nice To Have)


Familiarity with consuming data from Snowflake, dbt, or similar modern data stack components.
Certifications in Power BI or related Microsoft data tools.
Experience with Python for data validation, exploration, or lightweight transformation.
Experience in the semiconductor industry or familiarity with EDA tools and flows.


For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce - Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State - Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.

Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.

Job Req Type: Experienced

Required Travel: Yes, 10% of the time

Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days

Title: Data Analyst (Dealing / Trading Analytics)
Description:
We are a leading trading platform that is ambitiously expanding to the four corners of the globe. Our top-rated products have won prestigious industry awards for their cutting-edge technology and seamless client experience. We deliver only the best, so we are always in search of the best people to join our ever-growing talented team.

We are hiring a Data Analyst to partner closely with our Dealing team to improve trading and operational decision-making through data. This person will build and maintain analytics and reporting capabilities, translate business needs into well-scoped data solutions, and deliver actionable insights to support product evaluation and day-to-day operations. Strong SQL and Python skills are required, along with proven experience in trading/fintech/financial markets.

Responsibilities:


Partner day-to-day with the Dealing team to support trading and operational workflows with data and insights
Build and enhance analytics and reporting capabilities to support product evaluation, trading performance, and operational monitoring
Gather business requirements and apply market/trading context to scope data requests, define metrics, and recommend practical solutions
Analyze large, disconnected datasets to synthesize insights and highlight risks/opportunities
Collaborate with business leaders and cross-functional stakeholders to enable market-facing solutions powered by data
Communicate findings clearly (written and verbal) and deliver solutions reliably and on time
Ensure data quality by validating inputs, monitoring key metrics, and improving definitions/documentation where needed



Requirements:


Strong SQL and database experience (querying, joining, modeling/structuring data for analysis)
Advanced working knowledge and experience with Python
Basic statistical knowledge and ability to apply it appropriately in analysis and reporting
Demonstrated experience manipulating, processing, and extracting value from large, messy, and/or disconnected datasets
Strong understanding of trading/dealing concepts and market microstructure (execution, pricing, liquidity, slippage, exposure, hedging basics)
Familiarity with anti-fraud / trading abuse patterns and investigation workflows
Experience working with API connections (REST/WebSocket)
Experience with machine learning / deep learning / data science methods (nice to have)



What you will get in return:


Competitive Salary: We believe great work deserves great pay! Your skills and talents will be rewarded with a salary that makes you feel valued and motivated
Work-Life Harmony: Join a company that genuinely cares about you - because your life outside of work matters just as much as your time on the clock
Generous Time Off: Need a breather? Our annual leave policy lets you recharge and enjoy life outside of work without a worry
Employee Referral Program: Love working here? Share the love! Bring your talented friends on board and get rewarded for growing our awesome team
Comprehensive Health & Pension Benefits: From medical insurance to pension plans, we’ve got your back. Plus, location-specific benefits and perks!
Workation Wonderland: Live your digital nomad dreams with 30 extra days to work remotely from anywhere in the world (some restrictions apply). Adventure awaits!
Volunteer Days: Make a difference! Take two additional paid days each year to support causes you care about and give back to the community



Be a key player at the forefront of the digital assets movement, propelling your career to new heights! Join a dynamic and rapidly expanding company that values and rewards talent, initiative, and creativity. Work alongside one of the most brilliant teams in the industry.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our Barbora Data Analytics team. If you’re passionate about turning data into actionable insights and want to work in a motivating, friendly, and ambitious environment, this could be the perfect opportunity for you!

What you'll be doing:


Building data models (AWS Redshift) and Power BI reports. We strive to automate as much as possible.
Delivering ad‑hoc deep dives (monthly/weekly/urgent) to explain data/anomalies and recommend concrete actions.
Examining sales trends, analyzing digital channel performance across the website and app, collaborating closely with the marketing and sales data analysts.
Measuring performance across Lithuania, Latvia, and Estonia by tracking core KPIs, assessing website and app improvements, evaluating the impact of marketing and other initiatives.
Using your research materials to identify the right opportunities for business growth and branding.
Owning end‑to‑end digital channel analytics: events tracking, funnel analytics, customer behaviour.
Communicating insights clearly, keeping reporting reproducible.


What you'll need to succeed:


2+ years of experience in business analytics. Experience with digital data, including website and app events, is a plus but not required.
Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, Quicksight, etc.). Power BI is a plus.
Proficiency in SQL. Experience with Python, R, Matlab is a plus.
Excellent English (written/spoken).
Ownership and autonomy in a fast‑moving, result driven environment.
Collaborative and organized working style. We want to show you the ropes. It is great if we can learn from you as well.


What's in it for you:


Opportunity to accelerate your career by joining the #1 e-commerce grocery company in the Baltics. 🚀
Comprehensive coverage via private health insurance.
Access to Barbora.lt and partners’ discounts.
Your choice of hardware (Mac or Windows) to suit your work style.
Hybrid work model.
A high degree of collaboration and autonomy, motivated colleagues and access to cutting-edge technologies.
Fully equipped kitchen with coffee, cold drinks, and snacks.
Company events and team-buildings!


Salary:

Salary for this position ranges from 3500 EUR to 4200 EUR GROSS and depends on your experience.

Title: Senior IT Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Senior Data Analyst

The Role

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will work closely with business and technical teams to drive the creation of scalable and documented data design solutions. They drive adoption of enterprise and industry standard modeling methodologies. They partner with project teams to deliver practical & resilient data model designs that meet business needs and are capable of easily adapting to future business requirements.

The Expertise and Skills You Bring

Requirements And Analysis


Engage with business and technology partners to understand the business requirements, analyze data gaps, and recommend appropriate solutions.
Assess objectives, use cases, data requirements and specifications documents created by other analysts
Analyze and resolve major data issues that arise during the course of projects or in production environments


Data Design


You are an experienced enterprise data modeler who can define conceptual and logical data models, with strong definitions, to capture organizational data needs across multiple subject areas
Create physical design from logical data models for different types of systems such as operational systems, data warehouses, data lakes and others
Research to identify effective data designs, new tools and methodologies for data analysis
Document and present database solutions to product partners for approval.
Promote common data API adoption and reuse.
Map Technical terms to Business terms in data catalog tool.


Technical Leadership


Ensure technology decisions align with enterprise standards and strategy.
Collaborate with DBAs, architects, subject area owners to create scalable data models.
Find opportunities to utilize enterprise data model for new projects to minimize work and data redundancy


Education And Experience

A Bachelor’s or Master's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or equivalent experience


5+ years’ experience with data design, analysis and development. Development experience weighted towards PL/SQL and data loading tool/concepts.
Demonstrated experience in the following areas: Data API design and implementation, PL/SQL, data warehouses, data modeling and data profiling.


Skills And Knowledge


Demonstrable experience with SAP Power Designer or similar data modeling tool.
Proficient analytical and problem solving skills
Solid understanding of the database development process including analysis, design, coding, testing, problem resolution and planning, and full competency in all relevant coding environments, languages, tools, and platforms
Good interpersonal skills and strong verbal and written skills to effectively communicate with technical and non-technical associates in the company
Proven interpersonal skills, including the ability to work across the organization and interact/influence/negotiate optimally at all levels of management and peers.
Experience working in cross-functional agile development teams.
Understanding of Financial Services.


The Team

Asset Management Technology is seeking a Senior level candidate in the analysis and data modeling organization. The successful candidate will be responsible for leading analysis and data modeling across different projects with diverse scope and complex business and technical requirements in a highly integrated data environment. This role requires working under experienced leaders, for achieving organizational strategic data goals. You must be comfortable working in a fast paced and highly ambiguous agile environment where there is a focus on timely delivery of data models and other data artifacts.

AM Tech is a global organization, with technologists located across the United States, London, India, and Ireland. The team supports a broad range of functions within the Investment Management and Advisory domain, including Portfolio Management, Research, and Trading, by providing reliable, high impact technology solutions.

We are currently working in a hybrid model with some flexibility for remote working. However, Fidelity is gradually transitioning towards a full-time return to the office. We believe this will help strengthen our culture and enhance both the associate and customer experience. At present, this transition is expected from 2027 onwards, with no fixed timeline in place.

Category

Information Technology

Title: Data and Insights Coordinator
Description:
Musgrave is one of Europe’s most successful family-owned businesses, with a rich 150-year legacy in food and brand innovation. We`re proud to serve communities across Ireland and Spain, feeding one in three people every day through 18 iconic brands like SuperValu, Centra, Mace, Frank and Honest, and Musgrave MarketPlace. By supporting thousands of retail and foodservice family businesses, we make a positive impact on the communities they serve. We`re committed to becoming the most trusted and sustainable business in Ireland, and we`re looking for dynamic, forward-thinking individuals to join us on this journey.

As we continue our journey toward becoming Ireland’s most trusted and sustainable business, we have an exciting opportunity for a successful candidate to join our team as a Data and Insights Coordinator.

Please note this is a 12 month contract.

What you’ll be doing:

Post Campaign Reporting


Management of Post Campaign Analysis (PCA) process end-to-end, ensuring



accurate and timely delivery of campaign performance reporting.


Deliver PCA reports within calendar timelines & agreed SLA timelines, maintaining a



high standard of data accuracy and insight quality.


Analyse campaign performance by combining sales data, shopper behaviour insights,



audience engagement metrics, and media channel performance.


Provide actionable recommendations and insights to internal stakeholders and clients



based on campaign results.


Present campaign findings and reporting outcomes to internal teams & clients as required.
Support investigation of sales performance analysis & ad hoc client-related queries.



Data Management


Manage shopper data for targeted marketing campaigns, including



targeted email and coupon activity.


Support execution of "Buy, Scan & Win" competitions in line with



campaign requirements.


Manage campaign barcodes, product & sales data required for reporting and



performance analysis.


Assist with store selection analysis, identifying locations with strong sales opportunities



for retail media activities.


Support data quality assurance and validation processes across campaign reporting



outputs.

Management of Case Studies Library


Co-ordination & management of Elevates’ case study library.
Transform campaign data into compelling commercial case studies for use in client



presentations (both existing & new clients).

Process Improvement & Automation


Continuously identify opportunities to improve reporting processes, workflow efficiency,



and data management practices.


Collaborate with technical & transformation project teams to enhance reporting capabilities.
Create process guideline & standard operating procedures to improve



efficiencies and consistency.

What we’re looking for:

Skills/Experience


Strong problem-solving skills and a team player, experienced in working under tight deadlines and in dynamic environments.
Experience in working with teams, building strong working relationships.
Experience with using analytical and data visualization tools (e.g., Excel, Tableau, Power BI, Looker etc.).
Operational experience with SQL or other programming language desirable.
Strong attention to detail and critical thinking to analyse, evaluate & interpret data.



Salary for this position is 37K per year

Weekly hours are 37.5

What we offer:

Career Development: With a commitment to your personal and professional growth, Musgrave offers numerous opportunities for advancement and learning.

Collaborative Environment: Work alongside a passionate team, where your contributions will make a significant impact

Innovation Focus: Be part of a company that values forward-thinking solutions

Community Focus: Be part of a company that truly values its communities and strives to make a positive impact.

Be Part of a Legacy: Join a company with a long-standing commitment to community and innovation.

Musgrave is an equal opportunities employer. We encourage applications from diverse candidates. If we can make any accommodations to enable you to be the best version of yourself during the interview, please let us know.

Musgrave operate a Work Smart hybrid working model where you can alternate your time between connecting and collaborating in the business and working remotely.

Title: AI Analysis & Performance Analyst
Description:
Responsibilities
About the Role

As a AI Analyst within the Performance & Insights Customer Support team, you will play a key role in developing and executing on analytics and reporting initiatives which deliver data-driven insights to internal and external stakeholders, including strategy, operations, business, engineering, and R&D teams in both globally. You are playing a key role in empowering TikTok platform to achieve AI automation. You will contribute to the development of industry-leading capabilities to optimize system configuration, resource allocation, scheduling, and BPO operations in fast-paced environments while monitoring performance and continuously looking for areas to improve efficiency and enable high standards of Customer Service Support.

Responsibilities

This role is responsible for developing and executing on analytics and reporting initiatives which deliver data-driven insights to internal and external stakeholders, including strategy, operations, business, engineering, and R&D teams in both US and globally. You are playing a key role in empowering TikTok’s platform to achieve AI automation. You will contribute to the development of industry-leading capabilities to optimize human-AI task allocation, enhance operational efficiency through intelligent automation, and drive scalable solutions that improve user and agent experiences globally.You must be able to work both independently and as part of a team to deliver on a dynamic set of needs in a fast-paced environment. Specific responsibilities include:
- Work with large datasets to solve problems that could be ambiguous and nuanced using a variety of analytical and statistical approaches.
- Conduct quantitative analysis using statistical regression, machine learning, as well as hypothesis testing and present analysis and recommendations in a user friendly manner to propel business growth and customer satisfaction.
- Develop a scientific approach to measure KPI fluctuation, impact of business initiatives, and customer experience and design indexes for continuous monitoring.
- Design and implement foundational tests including A/B for measuring the effectiveness of customer support automation and customer service.
- Create documentation articulating the underlying assumptions, design of test, success metrics, and conclusion.
- Have familiarity with best practices and pragmatic approaches for addressing common issues including data quality, seasonality, insufficient training dataset, etc.
- Perform predictive analysis and support business partners with revenue and loss forecasts, budgeting, risk and incident management to proactively address foreseeable challenges.
- Conduct deep dive analysis and diagnosis to identify patterns, concentrations, correlations, root causes to provide transparency into business and product performance.
- Partner with Product, Engineering, Data Science, Operations and cross-functional teams to inform, influence, support, and execute strategy and decisions.
- Demonstrate understanding of common challenges facing TikTok business, key products, and the competitiveness of the marketplace.

Qualifications
Minimum Qualification(s)
- You have experience working on AI chatbots, automation, and measurement and impact in an Operations role within a BPO, Social Media, or Call Center environment.
- You have strong interpersonal and communication skills to engage with operational leadership and xfn stakeholders.
- You are efficient and able to prioritize, plan and multi-task with the ability to translate action steps into a systematic process.
- At least 3 years of experience in development or model/data pipeline development, with hands-on experience applying LLM technologies to real-world problems.
- Strong proficiency in programming languages in both Python and SQL.
- Experience in applied ML, particularly with Large Language Models (LLMs) and a strong track record of translating data into user insights and product impact.

Preferred Qualification(s)
- Experience with Customer Support.
- Clear understanding of data pipeline, model development, model testing and deployment.
- Intellectual curiosity, along with excellent problem-solving and quantitative skills, including the ability to disaggregate issues, identify root causes and recommend solutions.
- Prior experience in working on community insights / content understanding.

Trust & Safety
Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. ​
​
TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. ​
​
We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person-centred, innovative and integrated approach.​


About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.​
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​
​
Diversity & Inclusion​
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

Title: CRM BI Analyst
Description:
About Patrianna

Patrianna is a fast-growing product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a globally distributed team delivering seamless, engaging, and scalable gaming experiences for millions of players worldwide.

We're building a world-class operational engine and seeking talented professionals in CRM, fraud & risk, payments, marketing, and player support who excel in fast-paced, high-growth settings. Our teams prioritize smart execution, continuous improvement, and cross-functional collaboration—empowering you to drive real impact from day one.

What You Will Do


Analyze gaming economy health and player behavior to identify revenue optimization opportunities
Partner with CRM teams to design, track, and analyze campaigns, building player segmentation models to maximize lifetime value
Develop advanced Tableau and Looker dashboards plus automated reports for real-time player lifecycle visibility
Translate data science models into actionable monetization solutions for leadership
Validate features via A/B testing and statistical analysis to champion a testing culture
Forecast impacts of gamification features on engagement and spend, then measure results


What We Need


Gaming industry experience in BI/data analysis for monetization, gamification, or CRM analytics
Proven support for CRM teams on reporting and lifecycle strategies
Advanced SQL for large-scale data, plus Tableau, Looker, or Power BI visualization skills
Knowledge of player behavior, virtual economies, and gamification mechanics
Strong communication to deliver clear recommendations to non-technical stakeholders
Proactive problem-solving for trends and data integrity


Why Join Us

Be the analytical backbone for revenue impact across multiple gaming brands, shaping experiences for millions. Enjoy autonomy to innovate, own critical insights, and influence strategy in a high-growth environment. Collaborate with passionate teams valuing execution and learning, with competitive compensation and career growth.

Collaborate with passionate teams valuing execution and learning, with competitive compensation and career growth.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
We’re looking for a data-driven and curious Business Intelligence Analyst to help turn complex data into actionable insights that support smarter business decisions. In this role, you’ll work closely with stakeholders across the business to identify trends, improve performance, optimize processes, and support strategic initiatives through analytics and reporting.

You’ll play a key role in helping teams leverage data effectively while contributing to the continuous improvement of our BI environment, reporting infrastructure, and data governance practices.




What You’ll Do

Transform business data into actionable insights and recommendations
Support teams in identifying opportunities for growth, optimization, and operational improvement
Create, maintain, and improve BI reporting solutions and dashboards
Develop clear and impactful data visualizations to communicate findings effectively
Analyze complex datasets from multiple sources to uncover patterns, trends, and potential risks
Perform behavioral analysis and support A/B testing initiatives
Build forecasts and provide analytical support for new projects and business initiatives
Help maintain and improve data governance standards and BI best practices
Support data warehouse access layers and collaborate closely with other data-focused teams
Contribute to the administration and maintenance of enterprise reporting tools, including MicroStrategy and Tableau
Support and monitor BI microservices and troubleshoot BI-related technical issues
Provide training and support to users to encourage self-service analytics adoption




What We’re Looking For

2+ years of experience in a Business Intelligence, Data Analyst, or similar analytical role
Strong analytical mindset with excellent attention to detail and accuracy
Solid SQL skills and understanding of relational databases
Good understanding of ETL processes and data workflows
Experience working with data visualization and reporting tools
Familiarity with MicroStrategy and Tableau administration and maintenance is considered a strong advantage
Ability to interpret complex data and present findings clearly to non-technical stakeholders
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English
Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment
Experience in online entertainment, gaming, or other high-volume digital industries is considered an advantage




What We Offer

Paid annual leave (as established by law)
Birthday day off
3 additional wellness days per year
Access to the Salu Digital Healthcare Program
Monthly sports compensation (€55/month via Stebby)
Learning & Development budget in line with company policy
Team event budget

Title: Commercial Data Analyst
Description:
Epic is one of the largest telecommunications companies in Cyprus and a member of a dynamic European family of telecommunications companies. With ability, innovation and agility at our core, we are offering a state-of-the-art network recognized by multiple awards in Cyprus.




At Epic we take care of our people and we enhance a people centric and learning culture for your professional growth. We are always looking for ambitious employees to be a part of our award-winning organisation!

Our team is growing so join us to take your career to the next level and become a part of our team of more than 350 professionals.




The role:

As a Commercial Data Analyst, you'll turn data into actionable insights that shape commercial and marketing decisions. You'll work closely with cross functional teams to analyze customer behavior, identify opportunities, and deliver targeted marketing campaigns that drive customer engagement, increase value, and improve retention. Your insights will directly influence commercial strategies and contribute to the success of the business.




Your Responsibilities:

Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights that inform business decisions
Development, implementation and maintenance of weekly/monthly KPI’s for commercial segments stakeholders (Postpaid, Prepaid, Fixed).
Preparation of data & analysis of all marketing and sales campaigns: Cross Sell, Up Sell, Churn Prevention
Collaborate with broader Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Operations teams to develop and implement strategic initiatives that drive revenue/LTV growth and market expansion
Identify and implement process improvements to enhance data accuracy, reporting efficiency, and overall analytical capabilities.
Provide commercial insight on the month end results, trends and follow up actions. Clear and concise articulation of standard KPIs to segment stakeholders.
Acting as an internal consultant, using analytical skills to answer critical business questions.







Our proposition

Competitive remuneration package
Annual bonus scheme
13th salary
Attractive benefits with comprehensive medical and life insurances and provident fund
Company telecom products with free mobile and internet lines and exclusive discounts for our services with maximum monthly total value of 130 euros
Opportunity for a sustainable career and continuously building skills and expertise in a dynamic technology sector
Hybrid workplace model







Your Profile

2+ years of professional experience as an analyst/senior analyst, preferably in contract-based industry, market or consumer research with a particular focus on business data analysis, insights generation and planning
BSc in Computer Science (Data Science), Business Intelligence, Economics or related field
Postgraduate degree in any data science related field will be considered as strong advantage
Proficient SQL skills and data manipulation with Python (preferred) or R
Experience with any analytical/visualization tool (Power BI, Tableau)
Comfortable utilizing AI tools to improve efficiencies and integrate their usage in daily work
Understanding of data warehouses and ETL principles
Experience with an ETL platform such as Airflow, Informatica etc.
Willingness to learn new skills on the job
Fluent in English, with strong verbal and written communication skills




Interested to join a team of passionate professionals? Apply now! Send your CV to career@epic.com.cy stating the job title in the subject line.

Title: Insight Analyst
Description:
We are looking for an Insight Analyst with strong SQL skills and a passion for turning data into actionable insights. In this role, you will analyze and interpret iGaming data to identify trends, support strategic decision-making, and help optimize business performance and player experience.




In this role, you are going to:

Act as a dedicated analyst for the CRM team, serving as the first point of contact for all data and insight needs
Participate in CRM team rituals and planning meetings to ensure analytical support is aligned with business priorities
Gather, process, and analyze large volumes of iGaming-related data to conduct regular and ad-hoc analyses across various aspects of the business, providing insights into performance, trends, and optimization opportunities
Ensure the accuracy and integrity of data by performing cleaning, validation, and quality checks.
Calculate and evaluate ROI, and potential revenue gains or losses of business initiatives and marketing campaigns
Monitor and analyze assigned financial, behavioral, and engagement KPIs of customers across multiple business units
Translate business questions into analytical approaches and provide actionable recommendations based on findings
Present insights and recommendations to stakeholders in a clear and concise manner using storytelling and/or visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar platforms
Work closely with stakeholders across multiple departments, including marketing, CRM, product, customer support, and commercial teams
Support budgeting and forecasting processes by identifying trends, risks, and growth opportunities through data analysis
Independently manage analytical tasks and communicate progress, risks, and blockers proactively
Contribute to improving analytical processes, reporting quality, and documentation standards




To be successful in this role, you need to have:

Experience in data analysis, business intelligence, product analytics or a similar role
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets
Knowledge of at least one visualization tool: Tableau, Power BI, or Looker
Experience presenting insights and recommendations to business stakeholders
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Experience performing KPI analysis, segmentation, trend analysis, or performance monitoring
Excellent communication skills and proficiency in spoken and written English
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work independently




Preferred skills and experience:

Experience working with CRM data including customer lifecycle, retention/engagement metrics, and familiarity with iGaming mechanics such as bonusing and segmentation
Familiarity with statistical concepts, experimentation methodologies, and A/B testing
Experience working with cloud data warehouses such as BigQuery, Snowflake, or similar
Experience with Python or similar scripting language for data manipulation and automation




We will show our gratitude by:

Offering working tools of employees´ own choice - Company makes sure everyone feels comfortable in the office and has all the necessary tools one might need
Creating an exciting and fun international English-speaking environment
Working in a modern office in Ülemiste City
Organizing different company and team events
Offering extra sports compensation via Stebby + private health insurance
Creating an opportunity to learn and develop
Offering a competitive salary based on experience and a benefits package




If this seems like a challenge you are looking for, apply and let’s have a chat!




Sounds great but what do we do?

We are a growing company focused on the use of data to benefit e-commerce operations. Our Data Scientists provide decision-making assistance and models to improve operational efficiency and customer experience. The Analytics team provides insights on specific topics. Our Business Intelligence team makes information accessible. Together with the right technology we deliver the foundation for highly automated and effective CRM solutions.

Learn more about NabuMinds: https://nabuminds.com




As we strive to achieve our mission we reaffirm our commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI). We are proud to employ people of all backgrounds who possess the talent, energy, and focus to accelerate our vision forward and to tell our story. Each employee is encouraged to bring their whole self to work, knowing that NabuMinds values individuality as much as we value collaboration.

Title: Data Analyst - Internal Audit
Description:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Internal Audit branch of the Compliance & Internal Audit Analytics team at Wise.

You’ll partner with Internal Audit (IA) to develop and implement the analytics strategy across the function.

Internal Audit serves as an independent 3rd line of defence that oversees and provides an independent assessment and reasonable assurance on the effectiveness of the company’s corporate governance structure + internal controls.

The IA Analytics team supports IA by using analytics to provide more coverage, efficiency and accuracy to audit testing, as well as building innovative tools and using automation to support continuous testing and other IA activities beyond the annual audit plan.

This is an IC2 role. For more information on our Analytics Career Map and levelling structure, click here.

What You’ll Be Doing


Partnering with stakeholders across the IA function covering all regions, products and domains of the business (including Financial Crime, Ops, Risk, Finance and Technology)
Identifying & implementing opportunities to do the most impactful analytics within audits and influencing and upskilling your partners in IA to do the same
Working with analytics, engineering, and business specialists to seek out and understand interesting and varied datasets, perform analytics using modern approaches, and develop a strong understanding of operations at Wise
Championing innovation and data-led insights, not just within audits but across all IA activities where analytics can add value
Working on automation and tooling projects to increase efficiency across IA including workflow automation, continuous auditing, leveraging AI, and building MI dashboards


Qualifications

What you’ll bring:


3+ years of experience in data analytics
1+ years of data analytics experience within an audit, risk, or compliance related role
Advanced stakeholder management skills; you know how to leverage multiple different teams to deliver high-impact change including analytics, engineering, and business specialists
Extensive experience using SQL; you can build and maintain complex data sources and can leverage visualisation tools to surface insights


Nice To Have


Experience of working in regulated environments, Fintech or Financial Services.
Knowledge of financial crime.
Experience using dbt, Superset, or Looker.
Knowledge of advanced analytics techniques, including the use of Python and visualisation techniques.


Additional Information

For everyone, everywhere. We're people building money without borders — without judgement or prejudice, too. We believe teams are strongest when they are diverse, equitable and inclusive.

We're proud to have a truly international team, and we celebrate our differences.

Inclusive teams help us live our values and make sure every Wiser feels respected, empowered to contribute towards our mission and able to progress in their careers.

If you want to find out more about what it's like to work at Wise visit Wise.Jobs.

Keep up to date with life at Wise by following us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re a team of 500+ professionals who develop cutting-edge proxy and web data scraping solutions for thousands of the world’s best known businesses, including Fortune 500 companies.

What’s in store for you:

You’ll be developing complex products with high coding standards, maintaining our own infrastructure, handling petabytes of data, and solving challenges on a daily basis. We got you covered with a team of strong professionals to support you, a well-built tech stack, and loads of ownership.

In this role, you’ll:


Develop a solid understanding of the relationships between the behaviour of our customers and our growth drivers through data analysis and modelling
Create and maintain self-service dashboards for stakeholders across business, using PowerBI
Utilize analytical tools (SQL, Python, Google Sheets) to prepare and analyze data, supporting key decision-making processes
Carry out ad-hoc analysis to address stakeholder questions and effectively communicating your findings
Ensure data integrity and accuracy by performing regular data audits and validation
Collaborate with business users to determine KPIs and critical metrics to monitor for new business lines
Build forecast models to support business development
Deliver actionable insights and data-driven recommendations



Your skills & experience:


2+ years of experience working on business problems in a Data Analyst/Business Analyst role
Strong knowledge of SQL
Hands-on experience building effective, easy-to-navigate dashboards, ideally with PowerBI (or other data visualization tools such as Tableau, Qlik Sense, Looker, Grafana)
Fluency in English and Lithuanian
Ability to generate actionable insights and recommendations from data analysis with an understanding of how these insights can drive business impact
Problem solving and being a team player.
Pragmatic and impact-focused - you will choose Excel over Python if it is a better solution for the problem and vice-versa
Proven ability to formulate and test hypotheses about business performance
Strong communication skills to effectively collaborate with stakeholders and present findings



Tech Stack:


Google Cloud Platform (GCS, BQ) for storage and warehousing;
dbt and SQL for data modelling;
Python for automation;
PowerBI for data visualisation;
Airflow for orchestration;
Any tool you like for ad-hoc analysis
We don't expect you to master or know all of these technologies today



Salary & Benefits:


Gross salary: 3200 - 4500 EUR/month. Keep in mind that we are open to discussing a different salary based on your skills and experience
Growth & Learning: 40+ internal learning options, external conferences, mentorship, and year-round knowledge-sharing

  • Problem solving and being a team player.

Title: CRM BI Analyst
Description:
About Patrianna

Patrianna is a fast-growing product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a globally distributed team delivering seamless, engaging, and scalable gaming experiences for millions of players worldwide.

We're building a world-class operational engine and seeking talented professionals in CRM, fraud & risk, payments, marketing, and player support who excel in fast-paced, high-growth settings. Our teams prioritize smart execution, continuous improvement, and cross-functional collaboration—empowering you to drive real impact from day one.

What You Will Do


Analyze gaming economy health and player behavior to identify revenue optimization opportunities
Partner with CRM teams to design, track, and analyze campaigns, building player segmentation models to maximize lifetime value
Develop advanced Tableau and Looker dashboards plus automated reports for real-time player lifecycle visibility
Translate data science models into actionable monetization solutions for leadership
Validate features via A/B testing and statistical analysis to champion a testing culture
Forecast impacts of gamification features on engagement and spend, then measure results


What We Need


Gaming industry experience in BI/data analysis for monetization, gamification, or CRM analytics
Proven support for CRM teams on reporting and lifecycle strategies
Advanced SQL for large-scale data, plus Tableau, Looker, or Power BI visualization skills
Knowledge of player behavior, virtual economies, and gamification mechanics
Strong communication to deliver clear recommendations to non-technical stakeholders
Proactive problem-solving for trends and data integrity


Why Join Us

Be the analytical backbone for revenue impact across multiple gaming brands, shaping experiences for millions. Enjoy autonomy to innovate, own critical insights, and influence strategy in a high-growth environment. Collaborate with passionate teams valuing execution and learning, with competitive compensation and career growth.

Title: Business Analyst - Masterdata Governance
Description:
Job description:



Business Analyst – Masterdata Governance

Hybrid working

 

 

Tirlán

Tirlán, is a world-class food and nutrition co-operative, with a diverse portfolio of quality ingredients, leading consumer and agri brands.

Tirlán owns leading brands such as Avonmore, Kilmeaden Cheese, Millac, Solmiko, Truly Grass Fed, Premier Milk, Wexford, mymilkman.ie and GAIN Animal Nutrition.

We nurture a talented team across Ireland, the US, MEA and China, delivering annual revenues of circa €3 billion, and circa 2,000 people to manage our network of 11 processing plants and 52 agri branches. Our purpose is to nourish the world, while safeguarding our environment for future generations.

 

Role Profile

We have an opportunity for a Business Analyst to support implementation of a Master Data Governance strategy, with a strong focus on SAP master data analysis, data quality control, governance assurance, and process improvement. This is a highly analytical, control-focused role working across business and IT functions to strengthen the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of master data.

 

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Quality

Analyse SAP master data to identify data quality issues, including missing values, inconsistencies, duplicates, outliers and invalid records
Perform root-cause analysis to understand if issues are caused by system configuration, process gaps, control weakness, user behaviour or data migration activity
Support data cleansing and remediation activity, validating outcomes through profiling, reconciliation and post-remediation checks

Data Controls & Governance

Analyse large datasets from SAP and integrated systems to identify trends, exceptions, control gaps, and data integrity risks
Design and maintain data quality controls, exception checks, and validation routines to support proactive master data governance
Support master data cleansing, remediation, and ongoing control monitoring across key master data objects

Analytics & Control Monitoring

Use SAP Analytics Cloud, BI, Excel, and other analytical tools to monitor data quality, control effectiveness, and remediation progress
Provide insight-led analysis on recurring issues, control failures, process inefficiencies, and opportunities to improve master data governance

 

Requirements

Bachelor’s Degree (Business Information Systems, Accountancy or IT) and/or relevant experience and training
Minimum 3–5 years’ experience working in SAP or enterprise data environments.
Proven experience analysing SAP data, interpreting table-level information, and identifying data quality or control issues
Strong capability in data analysis, profiling, cleansing, root-cause investigation, and governance control techniques
Advanced Excel and data analysis capability, with the ability to manage and interpret large, complex datasets.
Experience developing data quality monitoring, exception analysis, or control assurance solutions
Exposure to SAP testing, data migration, or transformation programmes
Strong analytical and problem-solving ability
High attention to detail and data accuracy
Experience managing large, complex datasets
Ability to engage effectively with technical and business stakeholders

 

We offer

Hybrid working (flex Fridays)
Free parking incl. EV charging – where applicable
On-site gym free to use for employees
Parenting room
Work/life balance and collaborative work culture
Career progression opportunities

 

About us

Through our expertise in research and development, customer insights, food safety and state-of-the-art manufacturing we bring innovative food and nutrition solutions to a global market.  Our new and vibrant headquarters in Kilkenny City operates as a collaboration hub where multi-functional teams come together in a hybrid work environment.

 

Commitment to Diversity & Inclusion
We believe in the value of difference. We know that different perspectives, life experience and backgrounds can drive innovation and deliver growth.

We welcome people from all walks of life without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, or disability status. 

 

Join our Talent Pool

Considering a new career in Tirlán but this isn’t the role for you? Join our talent community and sign up for job notifications at www.tirlan.com/careers

 

Tirlán is a Values Based Organisation

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our Barbora Data Analytics team. If you’re passionate about turning data into actionable insights and want to work in a motivating, friendly, and ambitious environment, this could be the perfect opportunity for you!

What you'll be doing:


Building data models (AWS Redshift) and Power BI reports. We strive to automate as much as possible.
Delivering ad‑hoc deep dives (monthly/weekly/urgent) to explain data/anomalies and recommend concrete actions.
Examining sales trends, analyzing digital channel performance across the website and app, collaborating closely with the marketing and sales data analysts.
Measuring performance across Lithuania, Latvia, and Estonia by tracking core KPIs, assessing website and app improvements, evaluating the impact of marketing and other initiatives.
Using your research materials to identify the right opportunities for business growth and branding.
Owning end‑to‑end digital channel analytics: events tracking, funnel analytics, customer behaviour.
Communicating insights clearly, keeping reporting reproducible.


What you'll need to succeed:


2+ years of experience in business analytics. Experience with digital data, including website and app events, is a plus but not required.
Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, Quicksight, etc.). Power BI is a plus.
Proficiency in SQL. Experience with Python, R, Matlab is a plus.
Excellent English (written/spoken).
Ownership and autonomy in a fast‑moving, result driven environment.
Collaborative and organized working style. We want to show you the ropes. It is great if we can learn from you as well.


What's in it for you:


Opportunity to accelerate your career by joining the #1 e-commerce grocery company in the Baltics. 🚀
Comprehensive coverage via private health insurance.
Access to Barbora.lt and partners’ discounts.
Your choice of hardware (Mac or Windows) to suit your work style.
Hybrid work model.
A high degree of collaboration and autonomy, motivated colleagues and access to cutting-edge technologies.
Fully equipped kitchen with coffee, cold drinks, and snacks.
Company events and team-buildings!


Salary:

Salary for this position ranges from 3500 EUR to 4200 EUR GROSS and depends on your experience.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Back to all jobs

Data Analyst

About Nexters

Nexters is an international game development company that strives to introduce the joy of core gaming experiences to casual players. In 2021 we went public and listed on the NASDAQ (GDEV). Nexters is part of the GDEV holding, which includes not only Nexters, but also Cubic Games, Royal Ark, Game Gears studios.Our people work worldwide from offices in Cyprus, Armenia, and Kazakhstan or remotely. We want brilliant professionals who love building great games in a team with one of the best cultures.

About Your Role

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst at the intersection of data and marketing to join the Data Ecosystem team and help transform data into actionable business tools. This role is not about traditional analytics and ad-hoc requests, but about building solutions - from research and prototyping to implementation of tools that are actively used by the marketing team in their daily work.

What You’ll Be Doing


Developing and improving data-driven tools to increase marketing team efficiency
Designing and prototyping automated solutions (including AI-based ones) to accelerate creative production and traffic acquisition
Working on algorithmic approaches to media buying and campaign optimization
Integrating with external data sources and advertising platforms
Conducting data research, identifying growth opportunities, and transforming them into practical tools
Promoting a data-driven culture and expanding access to useful analytics for marketing
Working end-to-end: from problem definition and research to implementation and impact on business metrics
Collaborating with marketing, UA leads, analysts, and engineers, aligning solutions across teams
Quickly diving into problem contexts and taking ownership of results


We hope that you have


Expert-level SQL and experience working with large-scale data (ClickHouse / BigQuery / Redshift / PostgreSQL)
Strong Python skills (pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels, sklearn, etc.)
Deep understanding of marketing metrics and unit economics (LTV, CAC, ROI, retention, payback)
Hands-on experience with advertising platforms (Meta, Google Ads, Axon, etc.) and MMPs (AppsFlyer, Adjust)
Experience in taking analytical solutions through to implementation and measurable business impact
Ability to work in ambiguity: independently define approaches and drive tasks to completion
Ability to rapidly prototype solutions and validate hypotheses
Proactivity: willingness to suggest improvements in processes, tools, and approaches
Strong communication skills: ability to discuss solutions, align with stakeholders, and collaborate across teams


Nice to have


Experience building production-ready solutions
Experience with PySpark / Databricks
Experience designing ETL pipelines (Airflow, Luigi, or similar)
Experience automating processes (including with AI)
Experience building tools for marketing or UA teams
Experience in mobile products or the gaming industry
Strong systems thinking: ability to structure problems, define logic, and design processes


What We Offer


Healthcare Medical care program (including dental service) or medical care compensation are available for you wherever you wish to work. The basic medical care program is also available for your family members if you choose to work from our official locations – Cyprus, Armenia, and Kazakhstan.
Work, life, and balance We offer a fair amount of paid vacation days and sick leave. You can work at one of our comfortable offices in official locations or from home.
Sports You can choose various sports activities like yoga, football, and volleyball in our official locations.
Relocation support A relocation package is available for you if you decide to join us in one of our official locations.
Skills improvement Let's learn languages: you can visit online English language courses. Greek and Armenian language lessons are available for everyone who works in Cyprus or Armenia. Our company encourages people of Nexters to take part in game jams, hackathons, and relevant conferences and meetups as expert speakers.
Community & ESG We are proud of our local communities and support employees’ social initiatives. Dream big, act big: Nexters contribute to gaming’s positive impact on the world, hold a sustainability strategy, and take part in charity projects all over the world.


Work format

In one of the company's offices in Armenia, Cyprus, Kazakhstan or remotely.

Title: BI & Data Developer
Description:
BI & Data Developer

📍 Malta | Hybrid Working Available




A well-established financial services organisation is looking to hire a Business Intelligence / Data Developer to join its growing Data & Information Technology team.




This is an exciting opportunity for a data professional who enjoys working with modern Microsoft data technologies, building scalable ETL solutions, developing reporting platforms, and supporting business intelligence initiatives that drive strategic decision-making.




You will play a key role in designing, developing, and maintaining data solutions that support analytics, reporting, and operational efficiency across the organisation.




The Opportunity

As a Business Intelligence / Data Developer, you will work closely with business stakeholders and technical teams to build reliable data pipelines, reporting solutions, and data warehouse components that enable data-driven decision making.

This role offers exposure to a variety of projects across data integration, reporting, business intelligence, and data management within a highly regulated and technology-driven environment.







What You'll Be Doing

Design, develop, and maintain ETL processes using Microsoft SSIS, SQL Server, and Python
Build and maintain database stored procedures and data integration solutions
Generate and manage XML and CSV exports for internal and external systems
Develop and support business reports using SSRS and Power BI
Ensure data quality, integrity, consistency, and security across data platforms
Design and enhance data warehouse solutions to support reporting and analytics requirements
Document business processes, data flows, and technical solutions
Collaborate with business users to understand reporting and data requirements
Support testing, deployment, and ongoing maintenance of data solutions
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the data and BI environment







What We're Looking For

Bachelor's Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field
3–5 years of experience in data development using Microsoft technologies
Previous experience within the financial services sector is considered an advantage
Strong experience with SQL Server Data Tools (SSDT)
Solid understanding of SQL development and data modelling concepts
Hands-on experience developing and maintaining SSIS ETL solutions
Experience creating and maintaining SSRS reports
Strong Python development experience
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality
Good written and verbal communication skills




Nice to Have

Experience with DBT
Experience building Power BI dashboards and reports
Experience developing .NET applications
Experience with web application development
Exposure to data warehousing and business intelligence best practices




What's On Offer

Hybrid and flexible working arrangements
Ongoing professional development and career growth opportunities
Fully subsidised study loans and support for further education
Private health insurance, with the option to extend coverage to dependants
Life insurance and personal accident cover
Annual wellness allowance (€400)
Additional birthday leave
Annual salary reviews and performance appraisals
Employee wellbeing support, including professional counselling services
Preferential employee financial products and benefits
Corporate discounts and additional employee perks
Stable and supportive working environment within a respected financial institution




This is an excellent opportunity for a BI and Data professional looking to work on meaningful projects, develop their technical expertise, and contribute to the evolution of a modern data environment.




For more information, contact Kristoffer Parkhagen confidentially at +356 9999 8469 or send your CV to kristoffer.parkhagen@boston-link.com
