Title: Manager, Advanced Data Analytics and Insights
Description:
Job Description

Note: Fidelity will not provide immigration sponsorship for this position.

The Role

As a Manager, Advanced Data Analytics in the Advanced Analytics and Customer Insights organization you would have the opportunity to directly contribute towards this future. We are looking for an analytics professional to be a creative and collaborative team member, helping develop and lead data and hypothesis driven initiatives. You'll be embedded in our Digital Assets business and will partner together to help support and evolve Fidelity Digital Assets® offerings that demonstrate Fidelity’s commitment to customer value and high customer experience standards.

This role requires a dedicated, committed, and passionate individual who can:


Create and summarizes large datasets in structured and unstructured data.
Collect and analyze data / develop insights and reporting capabilities to empower organizational leaders to make decisions.
Create solutions leveraging deep statistical analysis to drive thought leadership across the Digital Assets organization
Work across diverse skilled teams to find success for Fidelity and our customers.
Have a natural intellectual curiosity, initiative, and love for learning new skills and capabilities.


The Expertise And Skills You Bring


Bachelor’s degree required, Advanced Degree a plus.
4 plus years of experience generating analytical insights to drive business actions.
Experience with a range of analytical tools and programming languages such as SQL, Python, R, etc.
Experience in designing and developing dashboards and reports by using Tableau, Power BI, Qlik, etc; Business Objects experience a plus.
Experience using and querying big data technologies such as Snowflake, Athena, etc.
Experience with digital analytics using tools such as Adobe Analytics.
Experience in Digital Assets a plus.


The Team

Fidelity Digital Assets® is dedicated to building products and services that help institutions adopt digital assets and innovate within the increasingly digital world of finance. Fidelity Investments began researching blockchain technology and digital assets solutions in 2014, where years of research and development have provided us with the deep expertise necessary to build the future of finance.

The Fidelity Digital Assets® Advanced Analytics and Customer Insights team is central to enhancing and delivering reporting and data analysis capabilities that serve Fidelity Digital Assets® Product and Operations stakeholders with insights on our products and customer segments. Members of our team are highly dynamic, team-oriented and partner with each other, business partners and their teams. We lead by example, seek to continuously improve, deliver creative solutions that solve complex challenges and/or advance the business, and seek to increase the value we provide through our business insights practice.

Fidelity’s Onsite Working Model

Fidelity is transitioning to a full-time onsite working model through a phased rollout across regions and roles. Currently, some roles and locations require 100% onsite presence, while others require less. Onsite expectations are likely to evolve as the rollout continues. This transition does not apply to fully remote roles.

Certifications

Category:

Data Analytics and Insights

Please be advised that Fidelity’s business is governed by the provisions of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, the Investment Company Act of 1940, ERISA, numerous state laws governing securities, investment and retirement-related financial activities and the rules and regulations of numerous self-regulatory organizations, including FINRA, among others. Those laws and regulations may restrict Fidelity from hiring and/or associating with individuals with certain Criminal Histories.

Title: Data Reporting Analyst - Remote
Description:
Put your Insurance Experience to work – FROM HOME!




At WAHVE, we value significant insurance experience and want to revolutionize the way people think about phasing into retirement by offering qualified candidates the opportunity to continue their career working from home. As we say – retire from the office but not from work. Our unique platform provides you with real work/life balance and allows you to choose a full or part-time work schedule while continuing to utilize your insurance expertise in a remote, long-term position which includes company benefits!




WHAT YOU’LL LOVE ABOUT WAHVE




We created a welcoming place to work with friendly and professional leadership. We are known for the great care we take with our staff and our clients. We are passionate and determined about delivering the best customer service, preserving insurance industry knowledge, and making a difference by the work that we do.




WHAT WE ARE SEEKING




We have assignments available to help our insurance carrier or MGA clients in Data Reporting Analyst positions for Commercial Lines. Responsibilities may include:




Support the preparation of ISO claims and premium reports through detailed analysis of ISO statistical plans.
Collaborate with IT, TPA Services and other departments to meet ISO reporting requirements.
Maintain clear, thorough documentation of processes for data extraction, formatting, and submission to appropriate bureaus.
Retrieve data from multiple systems, perform necessary Excel manipulation and submit reports to state regulatory agencies.
Analyze business challenges and data issues related to ISO and state reporting and recommend solutions to reduce downstream errors.
Partner with cross-functional teams (IT, Underwriting, etc.) to identify reporting impacts and implement necessary updates.
Ensure issues are properly identified, tracked, reported and resolved promptly.




TO BECOME A WORK-AT-HOME VINTAGE EXPERT, WE REQUIRE




25 years of full-time work experience
10 recent years of Property & Casualty insurance experience




BENEFITS OF BECOMING A WAHVE VINTAGE EXPERT




Health insurance based on eligibility.
401(k) with a 4% match.
Retire from the office but not from work.
Eliminate the office stress and the commute.
Choose the work you would like to do now.
Available in either full or part-time schedules.
Utilize your years of insurance industry knowledge.
Be part of our dynamic yet virtual team environment and connect with other experienced insurance professionals like yourself!




HOW TO GET STARTED




Click APPLY NOW to complete our simple preliminary profile. Be sure to include your preferred contact information as one of our Qualification Specialists will connect with you promptly.




WE LOOK FORWARD TO MEETING YOU!

Title: Timberland: Analyst, Long Range Planning (Americas)
Description:
At Timberland, we’re a force of nature. We’re a team that steps outside, works hard together, and moves the world forward. The brand is widely recognized as the arbiter of boot culture. With a rich heritage of craftsmanship and an eye toward the future, Timberland has a decades-long commitment to make products responsibly, protect the outdoors, and build community.

So, before we get to the job details, take a minute to learn a little more about us – our values and our culture. If you can see yourself working side-by-side with an team of fun, adventure-loving people, Timberland just may be the place for you.

To learn more about our values and our culture, visit Timberland Careers or www.timberland.com.

Analyst, Long Range Planning (Timberland Americas)

What will you do?

A day in the life of a Analyst, Long Range Planning (LRP) at Timberland looks a little like this.

You will support the Timberland Americas organization’s development of 5‑year strategic plans across regions and channels. This role supports growth through data management, analytics, and forecasting, which provide insights that align Americas plans with global long‑range strategies. Your work will directly support the annual budget process, KPI communication, marketing initiatives and operational planning to help achieve revenue and margin goals and drive product growth strategies.

As a trusted member of the Americas Business Planning team, you will report to the Manager, Americas Marketplace Reporting and Analytics.

Let’s break down that day-in-the-life a bit more.


Support the development of 5-Year long-range plans aligned to global strategy meeting decisions by sub-brand, country, channel and global brand architecture.
Provide data to support deep dive analyses at the key account and retail channel levels to support sustainable growth.
Perform KPI variance analysis, monitor performance against strategic objectives, and communicate risks and opportunities to stakeholders.
Support the analysis of business trends and help translate insights into actionable recommendations to inform management’s strategic decision-making.
Support the development and maintenance of operational dashboards and reporting tools to improve visibility into KPIs; assist in creating clear, impactful presentations for leadership and cross-functional teams.
Support the maintenance and updates of excel models for the annual budget and 5‑year strategic plan, with a focus on sales, margin, KPI performance, and product mix analysis.
Assist in aggregating and analyzing large datasets using advanced Excel and Power BI; help translate insights into clear, actionable summaries and presentations.
Collaborate and partner cross‑functionally with merchandising, marketing, finance, and operations to support the identification of trends, risks, and growth opportunities.
Contribute to store-level and e-commerce KPI analyses to help validate long-range plan assumptions and “what‑must‑be‑true” scenarios.
Support multiple projects and priorities simultaneously; assist in coordinating with stakeholders to help ensure alignment and timely delivery.


What do you need to succeed?

We all have unique skills that we bring to work and celebrate every day. For this role, there are foundation skills you’ll need to succeed and excel. Additionally, while formal education in a related field is great to have, we are most interested in your 3+ years of experience in the wholesale and/or retail industry preferred

Formal education and subsequent undergraduate/graduate degrees, such as a bachelor’s or business administration degree, preferably in Finance or a related field, are nice to have, but we are most interested in your total experience and professional achievements.

The foundation skills you will need in this position are:


Experience in sales and margin forecasting and modeling.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Power BI (required).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment.
Adeptness in demonstrating strong and effective written and verbal communication with all levels of the organization.
Curious, self‑motivated, and eager to learn.

Are you in?

Title: Alpha Account Services Data Analyst, Officer
Description:
Who We Are Looking For

The Alpha Strategy and Implementation Services Team at State Street is seeking a qualified Account Services Data Analyst to support client account onboarding, maintenance, and configuration activities across the Alpha Platform. This role is focused on collecting and validating client account reference data, supporting system configuration, and applying data controls that help enable accurate, timely, and consistent account setup across front-, middle-, and back-office services.

Key Responsibilities


Support the collection, review, and organization of client account data required for new account launches, lifecycle events, and static data maintenance.
Assist with account data validation, enrichment, and control activities to identify gaps, inconsistencies, and required client or internal follow-up om the Account Matrix/Conversion Data.
Support system configuration activities across Account Services workflow tools and downstream Alpha applications based on approved client account data.
Partner with clients, implementation leads, data leads, product, technology, and downstream operations teams to resolve data issues and support readiness for testing, conversion, and production use.



Why this role is important to us

The team you will be joining plays an important role in the overall success of the organization. Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. To make that happen, implementation teams are needed to help clients navigate employees and the organization as a whole during their initial onboarding. In your role you will strive for cutting-edge solutions, that are straightforward and scalable. You will help us build resilience and execute day to day deliverables at our best.

Join us if making your mark in the financial services industry from day one is a challenge you are up for.

What You Will Be Responsible For

As an Alpha Account Services Data Analyst, Officer you will


Collect, organize, and analyze client account data required to support client conversions.
Review client-provided data for completeness, consistency, and alignment to Account Services data requirements, templates, and agreed operating models.
Maintain and update client account data artifacts account setup data dictionaries, configuration trackers, validation logs, and related implementation documentation.
Support configuration of account attributes, dropdown values, lookups, business rules, checklist items, and other client-specific settings within Account Services workflow and administration tools.
Perform data profiling, reconciliation, and validation exercises to confirm that account data is accurately reflected across Account Services tools and downstream Alpha systems.
Investigate data exceptions, configuration breaks, and validation failures; document root cause, proposed resolution, ownership, and status through closure.
Partner with implementation leads, project managers, product owners, technology teams, and downstream service teams to clarify data requirements and resolve configuration dependencies.
Support testing cycles by preparing test data, reviewing expected results, validating configuration outcomes, and documenting defects or required remediation.
Apply data controls to ensure accurate, complete, and timely account setup, maintenance, and conversion activity.
Document field-level definitions, population rules, required values, impacted workstreams, and downstream usage to support consistent data interpretation.
Monitor configuration readiness, data quality issues, and open action items; escalate blockers that may impact testing, conversion, or production timelines.
Support post-go-live remediation and change activity by analyzing data impacts, validating updates, and coordinating with relevant stakeholders.
Contribute to reusable standards, templates, control checks, and best practices that improve the consistency and scalability of Account Services implementations.



What We Value

These skills will help you succeed in this role


Strong analytical skills with the ability to review large sets of data for completeness, accuracy, and consistency.
Experience supporting data collection, data validation, system configuration, testing, and operational readiness within financial services or investment operations.
Working knowledge of Python, SQL, Excel, data profiling, reconciliation techniques, and reporting tools used to support data analysis and control activities.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate data issues into clear actions for clients, implementation teams, technology partners, and operations stakeholders.



Education & Preferred Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Business, Accounting, Finance, MIS, Information Technology, Data Analytics, or a related field of study
5+ years of experience in data analysis, investment operations, client implementation, account services, or financial services technology
Experience with account onboarding, system configuration, data controls, or implementation testing preferred



Salary Range

$70,000 - $118,750 Annual

The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

Employees are eligible to participate in State Street’s comprehensive benefits program, which includes: our retirement savings plan (401K) with company match; insurance coverage including basic life, medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and other optional additional coverages; paid-time off including vacation, sick leave, short term disability, and family care responsibilities; access to our Employee Assistance Program; incentive compensation including eligibility for annual performance-based awards (excluding certain sales roles subject to sales incentive plans); and, eligibility for certain tax advantaged savings plans.

For a full overview, visit https://hrportal.ehr.com/statestreet/Home.

About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers

Read our CEO Statement

Job Application Disclosure

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Title: Analyst II, Marketing Analytics
Description:
At DraftKings, AI is becoming an integral part of both our present and future, powering how work gets done today, guiding smarter decisions, and sparking bold ideas. It’s transforming how we enhance customer experiences, streamline operations, and unlock new possibilities. Our teams are energized by innovation and readily embrace emerging technology. We’re not waiting for the future to arrive. We’re shaping it, one bold step at a time. To those who see AI as a driver of progress, come build the future together.

The Crown Is Yours

As an Analyst II on the Digital Marketing Analytics team, you'll leverage data-driven insights to inform and optimize channel strategy across DraftKings’ digital marketing ecosystem. You will play a key role in evaluating and enhancing marketing performance across channels including Paid Social, Programmatic, and Paid Search. In this role, you will also help advance the use of emerging technologies, including LLM-driven tools, to improve analytics capabilities and support our marketing partners in making smarter, faster decisions.

What you'll do as an Analyst II, Marketing Analytics


Partner closely with marketing stakeholders to understand business objectives, anticipate downstream impacts, and ensure analyses drive decision-making.
Own end-to-end analytics for key marketing initiatives, including identifying high-impact opportunity areas, developing analyses to quantify their value, and driving recommendations through to implementation.
Develop structured analytical frameworks to evaluate marketing performance, identify drivers of change, and simplify complex business problems.
Communicate findings effectively to cross-functional partners and senior leadership, using data storytelling to align on strategy and next steps.
Iterate and maintain data products and tools, including a marketing data agent to provide plain text answers to questions from analysts and stakeholders about marketing KPIs.



What you'll bring


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Business Analytics, or another relevant discipline.
At least 2 years of analytics or data science experience.
Proficiency in SQL/Snowflake and Excel for large-scale data analysis.
A strong understanding with A/B testing, experimental design, and analytical testing methodologies.
Experience working with marketing data and attribution methodologies, or an eager willingness to learn it.



Join Our Team

We’re a publicly traded (NASDAQ: DKNG) technology company headquartered in Boston. As a regulated gaming company, you may be required to obtain a gaming license issued by the appropriate state agency as a condition of employment. Don’t worry, we’ll guide you through the process if this is relevant to your role.

The US base salary range for this full-time position is 76,800.00 USD - 96,000.00 USD, plus bonus, equity, and benefits as applicable. Our ranges are determined by role, level, and location. The compensation information displayed on each job posting reflects the range for new hire pay rates for the position across all US locations. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific pay range and how that was determined during the hiring process. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Title: Commercial Data Operations Specialist
Description:
VIA is making an impact, and so can you.

VIA is the digital infrastructure company mission-critical organizations rely on when speed and security matter most. The Department of War, government agencies, and global enterprises depend on VIA to disrupt corruption, defend critical infrastructure, and deliver humanitarian aid. VIA’s products span agentic AI, zero-trust identity, quantum-resistant data, and offline stablecoin payments. Every human and agentic AI action is intentionally captured, attributed, and defensible. Designed for sub-second speed and multi-decade defensibility. Learn more: solvewithvia.com.

Data-driven growth is the backbone of our marketing mission.

An impressive mission requires an equally impressive, analytical mind. VIA is looking for a Commercial Data Operations Specialist who will own, develop, and optimize our entire inbound and outbound analytics from end to end. You will actively build and manage the infrastructure that connects our first digital touchpoint to recurring enterprise customers.

VIA is deeply committed to user privacy, having intentionally eliminated tracking cookies from our site, you will be the mastermind navigating our multi-touch attribution in a cookie-less, hyper-secure B2B and B2G ecosystem. You will track the full lifecycle of how users learn about us (from YouTube and the VIA Knowledge Hub podcasts) to how they engage on our website, and ultimately how they sync into a CRM solution as qualified opportunities. Your insights will directly shape our marketing strategy and critical decisions.

In This Role, You Will:


Architect and maintain the funnel infrastructure: Fully evaluate and optimize our CRM (e.g. HubSpot) and other marketing automation tools alongside privacy-first analytics tools (e.g. Plausible for website analytics) to paint a clear, un-siloed picture of our marketing attribution
Map the full customer acquisition lifecycle: Trace the complete journey of prospective clients from discovery to recurring customers. You’ll figure out exactly what happens when an enterprise lead moves from a YouTube video or podcast episode to our website, into a system and ultimately with the sales team and tracking their metrics
Inform strategy and decisions: Turn raw behavioral and inbound data into actionable marketing intelligence. You’ll guide decisions on outbound email cadence, target audiences, and high-ROI ad placements
Own the full analytics and reporting lifecycle: Manage end-to-end performance tracking across all channels including social (LinkedIn, X, Instagram, Youtube), content (Substack), website, and events. Translate these metrics into actionable reports you present to the team alongside strategic recommendations for stakeholders to optimize marketing and sales performance
Preserve data integrity: Ensure our data is handled in an encrypted, secure manner that mirrors VIA’s enterprise-grade standards, respecting our zero-cookie architecture while maintaining pristine lead-source tracking


Requirements

What you will bring to this role:


The tools of the trade: 3+ years of experience in Commercial Operations, Growth Marketing, or Data Analytics, with advanced, hands-on mastery of the minutiae within CRM platforms like HubSpot (e.g. workflows, attribution mapping, and CRM architecture)
Privacy-first expertise: Strong familiarity with cookie-less, privacy-preserving web analytics platforms like Plausible. You know how to build attribution structures without relying on invasive tracking scripts
Multi-touch mentality: A proven track record of tracking complex, multi-channel B2B user journeys across disparate media types (video, audio, text, web, email)
An architectural mindset: Experience setting up clean, automated data pipelines that eliminate manual lead-tracking bottlenecks
Cross-functional collaboration: Meet with sales and commercial teams to identify pain points with their sales tracking and fully get them on board with marketing analytics processes


What does it take to be a successful VIAneer? Let’s break it down, our VIAneers are:


Self-motivated and passionate about leaving everything they touch better than how they found it
Firm believers that people should love what they do and are eager to build a culture that enables them to do their best work
Creative problem solvers who respectfully challenge the status quo in the pursuit of excellence
People who lead discussions with curiosity and value diverse perspectives
Eager to explore new ideas, understand the power of feedback, and constantly seek opportunities to grow and develop their skills
Strong team players who thrive in collaborative environments and celebrate the success of others


Benefits

What can VIA do for you?

VIA Offers Competitive Benefits, Flexible Work Options, And Individualized Mentoring And Growth Opportunities. Here Are Just a Few Of Our VIAneers’ Favorite Perks:


Compensation:
This role offers a salary range of $80,000 - $100,000 USD per year
401K plan with up to 5% employer contribution
Health: A fully funded, top-tier health benefits plan, including vision and dental coverage, fully covered from day one, for your whole family
Family Support: Up to 24 weeks paid parental leave, a 4-week paid ramp-back program, and a $10K family forming benefit (covering fertility treatments, adoption, and surrogacy)
Time off: Flexible Vacation Policy with no set annual limit or accrual period, Summer Fridays, and an extended holiday period in December
Remote work: Ability to enjoy the best of both worlds with flexibility to work from home as needed, as well as access to three well-located offices, designed for collaboration and stocked with everything you could need
Opportunities to work remotely from eligible locations for up to 2 months per year
Growth: Individualized growth opportunities, including internal and external mentorship panels, custom goals and feedback sessions, and/or access to learning and development programs, including VIA’s unrivaled leadership program
A dedicated wellness advisor to help you navigate the programs and opportunities available at VIA
Transit: Benefits to support commuting costs
Connection: In-person events to foster team bonding and collaboration across different teams

Read More About Our Benefits And Perks here.

Our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

HTML:
VIA is making an impact, and so can you.

VIA is the digital infrastructure company mission-critical organizations rely on when speed and security matter most. The Department of War, government agencies, and global enterprises depend on VIA to disrupt corruption, defend critical infrastructure, and deliver humanitarian aid. VIA’s products span agentic AI, zero-trust identity, quantum-resistant data, and offline stablecoin payments. Every human and agentic AI action is intentionally captured, attributed, and defensible. Designed for sub-second speed and multi-decade defensibility. Learn more: solvewithvia.com.

Data-driven growth is the backbone of our marketing mission.

An impressive mission requires an equally impressive, analytical mind. VIA is looking for a Commercial Data Operations Specialist who will own, develop, and optimize our entire inbound and outbound analytics from end to end. You will actively build and manage the infrastructure that connects our first digital touchpoint to recurring enterprise customers.

VIA is deeply committed to user privacy, having intentionally eliminated tracking cookies from our site, you will be the mastermind navigating our multi-touch attribution in a cookie-less, hyper-secure B2B and B2G ecosystem. You will track the full lifecycle of how users learn about us (from YouTube and the VIA Knowledge Hub podcasts) to how they engage on our website, and ultimately how they sync into a CRM solution as qualified opportunities. Your insights will directly shape our marketing strategy and critical decisions.

In This Role, You Will:

  • Architect and maintain the funnel infrastructure: Fully evaluate and optimize our CRM (e.g. HubSpot) and other marketing automation tools alongside privacy-first analytics tools (e.g. Plausible for website analytics) to paint a clear, un-siloed picture of our marketing attribution
  • Map the full customer acquisition lifecycle: Trace the complete journey of prospective clients from discovery to recurring customers. You’ll figure out exactly what happens when an enterprise lead moves from a YouTube video or podcast episode to our website, into a system and ultimately with the sales team and tracking their metrics
  • Inform strategy and decisions: Turn raw behavioral and inbound data into actionable marketing intelligence. You’ll guide decisions on outbound email cadence, target audiences, and high-ROI ad placements
  • Own the full analytics and reporting lifecycle: Manage end-to-end performance tracking across all channels including social (LinkedIn, X, Instagram, Youtube), content (Substack), website, and events. Translate these metrics into actionable reports you present to the team alongside strategic recommendations for stakeholders to optimize marketing and sales performance
  • Preserve data integrity: Ensure our data is handled in an encrypted, secure manner that mirrors VIA’s enterprise-grade standards, respecting our zero-cookie architecture while maintaining pristine lead-source tracking

Requirements

What you will bring to this role:

  • The tools of the trade: 3+ years of experience in Commercial Operations, Growth Marketing, or Data Analytics, with advanced, hands-on mastery of the minutiae within CRM platforms like HubSpot (e.g. workflows, attribution mapping, and CRM architecture)
  • Privacy-first expertise: Strong familiarity with cookie-less, privacy-preserving web analytics platforms like Plausible. You know how to build attribution structures without relying on invasive tracking scripts
  • Multi-touch mentality: A proven track record of tracking complex, multi-channel B2B user journeys across disparate media types (video, audio, text, web, email)
  • An architectural mindset: Experience setting up clean, automated data pipelines that eliminate manual lead-tracking bottlenecks
  • Cross-functional collaboration: Meet with sales and commercial teams to identify pain points with their sales tracking and fully get them on board with marketing analytics processes

What does it take to be a successful VIAneer? Let’s break it down, our VIAneers are:

  • Self-motivated and passionate about leaving everything they touch better than how they found it
  • Firm believers that people should love what they do and are eager to build a culture that enables them to do their best work
  • Creative problem solvers who respectfully challenge the status quo in the pursuit of excellence
  • People who lead discussions with curiosity and value diverse perspectives
  • Eager to explore new ideas, understand the power of feedback, and constantly seek opportunities to grow and develop their skills
  • Strong team players who thrive in collaborative environments and celebrate the success of others

Benefits

What can VIA do for you?

VIA Offers Competitive Benefits, Flexible Work Options, And Individualized Mentoring And Growth Opportunities. Here Are Just a Few Of Our VIAneers’ Favorite Perks:

  • Compensation:
    • This role offers a salary range of $80,000 - $100,000 USD per year
    • 401K plan with up to 5% employer contribution
  • Health: A fully funded, top-tier health benefits plan, including vision and dental coverage, fully covered from day one, for your whole family
  • Family Support: Up to 24 weeks paid parental leave, a 4-week paid ramp-back program, and a $10K family forming benefit (covering fertility treatments, adoption, and surrogacy)
  • Time off: Flexible Vacation Policy with no set annual limit or accrual period, Summer Fridays, and an extended holiday period in December
  • Remote work: Ability to enjoy the best of both worlds with flexibility to work from home as needed, as well as access to three well-located offices, designed for collaboration and stocked with everything you could need
    • Opportunities to work remotely from eligible locations for up to 2 months per year
  • Growth: Individualized growth opportunities, including internal and external mentorship panels, custom goals and feedback sessions, and/or access to learning and development programs, including VIA’s unrivaled leadership program
    • A dedicated wellness advisor to help you navigate the programs and opportunities available at VIA
  • Transit: Benefits to support commuting costs
  • Connection: In-person events to foster team bonding and collaboration across different teams

Title: DATA COORDINATOR
Description:
Description

Job Overview

The Entwistle Company is seeking a detail-oriented and highly organized Data Coordinator to build a long-term career in our collaborative and innovative environment.

In this role, you’ll help deliver mission-critical components that support U.S. and Allied defense, with your skills, creativity, and impact directly contributing to national and global security. We value talent, encourage innovation, and promote professional growth.

Ready to make a real difference? Join us.

Our Mission Statement


To be an essential provider of mission-critical defense components & systems, spanning air, land, sea and space, recognized for our specialized capabilities, quality and dependability.
To design, manufacture & build-to-print innovative and cost-effective products.
To help our DoD, Prime and Foreign Military customers and US warfighter succeed in their missions.


At The Entwistle Company, our values—Trust, Respect, Accountability, Collaboration, and Commitment—guide everything we do, from daily decisions to long-term strategy.

Key Responsibilities


Enter, update, and maintain procurement database, supplier recordkeeping per FAR and DFARS regulatory environment, verify pricing and part numbers in ERP/MRP systems.
Monitor and reconcile data inconsistencies in procurement documentation, contracts, and supplier communications.
Generate reports related to supplier performance, order status, lead times, and delivery metrics for internal use. Advanced reporting and KPI monitoring at the direction of Management.
Manage and maintain Safety Data Sheets.
Collaborate with Buyers, IS managers, and Supply Chain/Procurement management.
Assist with audit preparation and document preparation for regulatory or customer reviews.
Provide administrative support to the Purchasing team, including database, IS management, and document filing.


Qualifications


Associate’s degree in Supply Chain, Data Analytics, Computer Science or related field; Bachelor’s preferred.
2–4 years of experience in procurement or supply chain support, ideally in a defense manufacturing environment.
5+ years of demonstrated DBM/Advanced Excel including Pivot Tables and custom formula creation
Proficient in ERP Systems (Global Shop Solutions preferred) with strong data entry, reporting, and analysis skills.
Knowledge of manufacturing procurement processes, vendor management best practices, MRP, and defense industry compliance (FAR, DFARS).
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities while maintaining data accuracy and confidentiality.
Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across departments.


Related Military Job Codes (HR USE)

We value military experience and recognize transferable skills. Candidates with the following military job codes or similar may have relevant experience for this position:


Army MOS: 51C – Acquisition, Logistics, and Technology NCO
Navy NEC: LS – Logistics Specialist
Air Force AFSC: 3A1X1 - Administration
Marine Corps MOS: 3043 – Supply Administration and Operations Specialist


What We Offer


401k – 4% match on 5% deferrals
PTO – Up to 5 weeks
Employer Paid Life Insurance
Employer Paid Short-term and Long-term Disability
Employer provided Paid Family/Medical Leave
Generous Health Insurance Coverage
Dental Insurance AND Vision Insurance
Safety Shoes & Glasses reimbursements
Employee Appreciation Events


Requirements

Competitive pay based on experience.

Title: Manager, Data Analytics and Insights
Description:
Job Description

Note: Fidelity is not providing immigration sponsorship for this position

The Role

Fidelity Institutional (FI) is seeking a Data Governance Operations Analyst to support the operational execution of the Data Governance program across Fidelity Institutional (FI) and Fidelity Institutional Wealth Management Services (IWMS). Our Data Governance team enables the responsible, ethical, and permissible use of data by establishing and maintaining governance processes that support trusted data, informed decision-making, regulatory compliance, and innovation. This role serves as a key operational partner to governance leadership, ensuring governance activities are executed efficiently and consistently while supporting strategic priorities and risk-informed decision-making.

The Data Governance Operations Analyst will own and coordinate critical governance processes, including Data Oversight Committee (DOC) intake management, governance forum operations, meeting coordination, documentation, and action item tracking. The role is responsible for ensuring governance submissions meet established standards, facilitating stakeholder engagement, maintaining governance records, and supporting audit readiness and transparency. In partnership with technology, business, and data management teams, the analyst will also coordinate access and provisioning activities, validate access requests for completeness and proper approvals, and help ensure governance decisions are implemented effectively across supported platforms.

Additionally, the role will support data catalog and metadata operations by maintaining accurate data asset inventories, ownership assignments, classifications, business glossary terms, and metadata standards. Working closely with business and technology stakeholders, the analyst will help drive adoption of data cataloging practices that strengthen data stewardship and governance maturity. The position will also monitor governance activity and operational performance metrics, identify opportunities to improve processes and workflow efficiency, and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives that enhance governance effectiveness and reduce operational risk across the organization.

The Expertise And Skills You Bring


Education: Bachelor’s degree in Data Management, Information Systems, Business, Finance, or a related field
Experience 2–4 years of experience in data governance, data operations, access management, or a related discipline; experience in a regulated industry preferred.
Specific certifications (only include if required or nice-to-have)
Skills required for the role: Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple workflows and deadlines. Familiarity with governance and access tools (e.g., Collibra, MyAccess, Snowflake) and DG policy framework is a plus


The Team

This role is pivotal to the success of the Data Governance organization within the FI Data Management team. We enable the responsible, ethical, and permissible use of data across Fidelity Institutional (FI) and IWMS by partnering closely with Legal, Risk, and Compliance (LRC) colleagues and business stakeholders. Our team helps ensure that data is governed as a trusted enterprise asset, supporting regulatory compliance, risk mitigation, operational efficiency, and data-driven decision-making. The role collaborates across business, technology, governance, risk, compliance, and data management functions to advance Fidelity's commitment to strong data stewardship, innovation, and client-centric outcomes while contributing to a culture of inclusion, learning, and continuous growth.

Fidelity’s Onsite Working Model

Fidelity is transitioning to a full-time onsite working model through a phased rollout across regions and roles. Currently, some roles and locations require 100% onsite presence, while others require less. Onsite expectations are likely to evolve as the rollout continues. This transition does not apply to fully remote roles.

The base salary range for this position is $80,000-$153,000 per year.

Placement in the range will vary based on job responsibilities and scope, geographic location, candidate’s relevant experience, and other factors.

Base salary is only part of the total compensation package. Depending on the position and eligibility requirements, the offer package may also include bonus or other variable compensation.

We offer a wide range of benefits to meet your evolving needs and help you live your best life at work and at home. These benefits include comprehensive health care coverage and emotional well-being support, market-leading retirement, generous paid time off and parental leave, charitable giving employee match program, and educational assistance including student loan repayment, tuition reimbursement, and learning resources to develop your career. Note, the application window closes when the position is filled or unposted.

Please be advised that Fidelity’s business is governed by the provisions of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, the Investment Company Act of 1940, ERISA, numerous state laws governing securities, investment and retirement-related financial activities and the rules and regulations of numerous self-regulatory organizations, including FINRA, among others. Those laws and regulations may restrict Fidelity from hiring and/or associating with individuals with certain Criminal Histories.

Title: Methods Analyst I -ECOMB Methods Analyst
Description:
The Edmonton Police Service requires one (1) permanent full-time organized, detail-oriented, individual to provide analysis and call center reporting to the Data and Technology Unit of the Emergency Communications and Operations Management Branch. Responsibilities will include:


Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports and dashboards to support data driven decision making by the leadership team.
Produce ad-hoc reporting on-demand to support internal initiatives, projects, and investigations.
Lead the design, development, automation and maintenance of statistical data and information for a wide range of internal and external stakeholders.
Provide expert advice and guidance on statistical analytics and establish robust mechanisms to assess the effectiveness of the emergency call center.
Manage the Extraction, Translation, and Loading of data from various sources, including telephony and dispatch data, to serve the needs of Reporting and Analytics.
Develop and maintain Key Performance Indicators by applying statistical and mathematical modelling techniques to inform operational efficiency and effectiveness.
Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports and dashboards to support data driven decision making by the leadership team.
Present complex concepts and analyses to stakeholders leveraging sound written and verbal communication skills complemented by striking and informative visual storytelling.
Provide subject matter expertise regarding data literacy, use of reporting tools, statistical concepts, and practices.
Create and maintain process maps, documentation, and training material; deliver training to stakeholders as needed.
Liaise with internal and external partners to research and share best practices and methodologies.
Other related duties as required.



Qualifications:


University graduation in Criminology, Sociology and Applied Mathematics or other related fields of study.
A minimum of two (2) years directly related working experience is required.
Familiarity with Microsoft applications and services including Word, Excel, Access, Fabric, and Power BI.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, and ability to effectively communicate complex technical details to non-technical audiences.
Familiarity with Python, R, SQL-based query languages, DAX, and PowerShell languages an assets.
Experience providing analysis of metrics for call centers or public safety answering points (PSAP) would be an asset.
Strong organizational, interpersonal and time management skills.
Proven technical ability working with various computer software programs and databases.
Demonstrated self-initiative and the ability to work with minimal supervision.
Mature individual with the ability to adapt to change and work in a multidisciplinary team setting.
Experience transforming data within Snowflake would be an asset.



Hours of Work: 40 hours per week, Monday – Friday.

Salary Range: 21B, Salary Grade: 024, $41.17 - $51.44 (Hourly), $3,294.20 - $4,115.50 (Biweekly), $85,977.58 - $107,415.07 (Annually). The rates quoted are in accordance with a collective agreement between the Union and the City of Edmonton.

General:


Civic Service Union 52 members are asked to send a copy of their application to the union office.
The City of Edmonton thanks applicants for their interest in this opportunity. Candidates considered for the position will be contacted.
We are an equal opportunity employer. We welcome diversity and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.
This option to work remotely is not available at this time.
As part of your pre-hire screening, you will be required to complete an Enhanced Security Clearance. Should you accept employment with the Edmonton Police Service you acknowledge and agree to participate in future Enhanced Security Re-Clearances at scheduled intervals or in any other circumstances, exigent or otherwise, as required by Human Resources Division.
Applicants may be tested.



Applicants who have completed post-secondary education outside of Canada are encouraged to obtain an assessment of their credentials through the International Qualifications Assessment Service (IQAS) ( https://www.alberta.ca/international-qualifications-assessment.aspx) or another recognized Canadian credential evaluation service. For a list of designated evaluators, please visit the Alliance of Credential Evaluation Services of Canada ( https://canalliance.org/en/default.html ). Including an IQAS certificate or other credential assessment certificate from a recognized Canadian credential evaluation service with your application is recommended to support the evaluation of the applicant’s educational qualifications.

Title: Intermediate Business Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Fleetway is seeking a qualified Intermediate Data Analyst to join our Technical Publications team, which is leading the development of cutting-edge documentation for the next generation of Royal Canadian Navy ships. We offer a modern, collaborative environment that leverages advanced technology to produce high-quality technical publications.

As an Intermediate Data Analyst, you will play a key role in supporting Irving Shipbuilding’s strategic goals and operational efficiency by analyzing data, identifying business needs, and recommending actionable solutions. This position is central to the development and delivery of the Technical Data Package (TDP) for the River Class Destroyer Program.

You’ll work closely with cross-functional teams to coordinate and maintain TDP deliverables, metrics, and dashboards. Your insights will help stakeholders and leadership make informed decisions at both project and management levels.

We're committed to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Fleetway is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals, including those from underrepresented groups, and we strive to create an environment where everyone feels respected, supported, and empowered to thrive.

We encourage applicants to self-identify if they belong to an underrepresented group, as part of our commitment to equitable hiring practices and fostering a truly inclusive workforce.

If you require accommodations during the recruitment process, please let us know—we're happy to help.

Responsibilities


Collaborate with stakeholders to gather and document business requirements.
Analyze data to support the development and delivery of Technical Data Packages (TDPs).
Maintain dashboards and reports to track project progress and performance.
Support project planning, testing, and change management activities.
Identify process improvements and recommend data-driven solutions.
Ensure alignment with compliance standards and organizational goals.


Qualifications

Education


A post-secondary degree in Computer Science, Business, Information Systems, or a related field.


Skills & Experience


2–5 years of experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar role.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Ability to work collaboratively across teams and disciplines
Detail-oriented with a focus on accuracy and quality
Adaptable and proactive in a dynamic project environment


Due to the nature of our work, successful candidates must be eligible to obtain required government security clearances.

About Us

Founded in 1882, J.D. Irving, Limited (JDI) operates in Eastern Canada (New-Brunswick, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, Québec, Ontario, Newfoundland and Labrador), as well as in USA. We are a team of 20,000 dedicated employees. At J.D. Irving, Limited, you are part of a dynamic team of professionals who lead with integrity and are passionate about delivering results. With a culture that nurtures talent and creates an inclusive workplace, you can grow your career across our various operating divisions.

We are committed to creating accessible environments for our colleagues, candidates, and customers. Requests for accommodation due to a disability (which may be visible or invisible, temporary, or permanent) can be made at any stage of application and employment. We encourage candidates to make their accommodation needs known so that we can provide equitable opportunities.

Worried you do not meet all the requirements? Studies have shown that many applicants apply only to job opportunities when they feel they meet 100% of the criteria. We promote an inclusive environment where everyone is appreciated, recognized, and empowered to contribute fully. If you are interested in this position but are worried you did not meet all the requirements, we would encourage you to still apply.

Our recruitment advisors and hiring teams will utilize human screening combined with AI technology to help identify the skills and qualities that matter most to our business, while safeguarding your privacy and using AI responsibly.

We appreciate your interest in our company however only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

About The Team

We are Fleetway! We believe Canada’s maritime fleets need to be reliable and fit for purpose, with crews that are fully trained and supported. We work as a trusted technical partner during design, construction, in-service, and end of life. We are proud to deliver quality professional technical services including Engineering, Integrated Logistics Support, Technical Editing, Configuration Management, Project Management, Training, Technical Translation, and Marine Technical Services.

Over the past 40+ years, we have built an outstanding team of people and partners, invested in the tools, and earned an exemplary track record servicing our customers. Everything we do is aimed at developing our people and improving our use of innovation to be the leading professional services provider in the marine industry. We are proud to have earned national recognition as a Nova Scotia and Atlantic Canada’s Top Employer.

All successful applicants must meet requirements for Canadian Controlled Goods Program (CGP), and Canadian Government Security Clearance.

What We Offer:

The successful candidate will receive a starting salary based on their applicable experience and demonstrated knowledge, skill, and abilities. Additionally, we offer the following:


We are regularly adding new departments, positions, and functions within Fleetway, as such, we can offer you growth and development opportunities for future career advancement
We provide direct support to your training and development plans, including support towards your professional certifications, memberships, or designations
Flexible work hours (with core hours)
We have Mental Health first aid trained staff in all offices
A flexible medical, dental, and vision plan, with an enhanced focus on mental health and wellness
Ability to save for your future with our company matching RRSP program
15-week maternity leave top-up program
Employee & Family Assistance Program
Annual health & wellness reimbursements
Business casual work environment, with casual Fridays
A slick company jacket that will definitely make you the envy of your friends

Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
The Pricing Analyst is instrumental in providing key analytical, maintenance, and costing support to the organization. You will help drive efficiencies within the organization and drive profitable growth into the future.

As the Pricing Analyst you are a real Data Intelligence Analyst. This role is instrumental in providing key analytical, maintenance and costing support to the organization. Specifically, you will become the catalyst which will drive efficiencies within the organization. Your detail-oriented nature will promote and sustain the profitable growth of the company.

Responsibilities


Review market trends for pricing opportunities to optimize revenues
Monitor cost and raw material fluctuations
Interpret data into compelling insights and present to a variety of levels of leadership
Analyze price relationships between products
Manage new product listings in the system with appropriate cost and other key data elements
Assist in development of new key insights by constructing reports which can be utilized throughout the organization
Maintain data integrity within the system by conducting vendor reviews to ensure accurate information is present
Manage vendor price adjustments by uploading data within the system while maintaining accuracy
Maintain accurate vendor contracts and audit monthly denials


Requirements


Bachelor's degree with a specialization in Business or Mathematics
2-3 years previous experience in similar role
Microsoft Office expertise, especially in Excel (v-lookup, index match, Pivot table)
Excellent communicator – speaks and writes clearly and to the point
Analytical and organized with relentless attention to detail
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Team player who displays personal leadership
Basic knowledge of financial statements and trial balance information
Proven track record of process optimization an asset
Ability to work in a fast-paced national distribution environment


Bunzl Canada Inc. (bunzlcanada.ca) provides the food and retail packaging, cleaning and hygiene products and equipment, safety and industrial supplies which keep over 58,000 Canadian businesses running optimally, every day. We bring our customers the advantage of global sourcing, product innovation and national scale combined with responsive local service and deep category expertise. Bunzl Canada is a certified Great Place to Work®. We are committed to creating an inclusive, equitable and positive working environment for all employees so that they experience a strong sense of belonging, while developing individually and professionally. Bunzl Canada is an operating company of Bunzl plc (BNZL.L), a FTSE100 company listed on the London Stock Exchange. With 27,000 employees in over 30 countries, our global presence spans the Americas, Europe, Asia Pacific, and the UK and Ireland. At Bunzl, you’ll find Unlimited Potential… Your Career - - Our Future!

Bunzl Canada has a tradition of commitment to equal employment opportunity. It is our established policy to attract and retain the best qualified people without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex/gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, age, disability or veteran status as provided by law.

Title: Retirement Analyst
Description:
We are searching for a Retirement Analyst to join our National Indigenous Solutions Practice in Winnipeg, Burlington, or Burnaby.

The Retirement Analyst plays a central role in advancing the Group Retirement practice within National Indigenous Solutions (NIS) Practice. This role blends data management, pricing support, market analysis, and insight generation to support both client retention and growth.

Working closely with the National Director, Group Retirement Strategy, the Retirement Analyst will act as a key contributor to how we collect, analyze, and apply data across a national block of business. The role is equal parts analytical and strategic. They will automate processes by leveraging AI, and deliver differentiated insights to Indigenous organizations and communities.

This is an excellent opportunity for a high-potential analyst to develop deep expertise in group retirement while helping shape a modern, data-driven practice.

We strongly encourage First Nations and Indigenous applicants to self-declare in your cover letter.

The Retirement Analyst Will

Data Management & Integrity


Maintain and validate client and member data across group retirement programs, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and completeness
Identify and resolve data gaps, inconsistencies, and anomalies across multiple data sources
Build and maintain structured datasets to support reporting, pricing, and insight generation
Establish disciplined processes to improve data quality and accessibility across the practice


Reporting & Benchmarking


Develop and maintain reporting frameworks and benchmarking models across the NIS block of business
Produce recurring and ad-hoc reports for client reviews, internal leadership, and new business opportunities
Leverage AI tools to automate reporting processes and enhance data visualization
Translate complex datasets into clear, client-ready insights that demonstrate value and market positioning


Pricing & New Business Support


Coordinate the preparation and management of group retirement quote requests with insurance carriers
Analyze pricing proposals, prepare comparisons, and support recommendation development
Build impactful, client-facing proposal materials for both existing and prospective clients
Support RFP responses and finalist presentations in partnership with business development and client management teams


Insights & Strategy Enablement


Analyze plan performance, member behaviour, and utilization trends to identify opportunities for improvement
Develop data-driven insights that inform plan design, investment menus, and member engagement strategies
Research industry trends and competitive positioning to strengthen NIS market differentiation
Utilize AI tools to accelerate analysis and surface insights across large and complex datasets


Operational Support & Collaboration


Partner closely with the National Director, Group Retirement Strategy, and broader NIS leadership team
Prepare materials for client meetings, plan reviews, and internal strategic discussions
Support governance processes and ensure readiness of documentation and reporting requirements
Contribute to a collaborative, high-performing team environment aligned to NIS priorities


To be successful as a Retirement Analyst with the National Indigenous Solutions Practice, you will need:


Minimum 2 years of experience in data analysis, financial services, group retirement, or a related field
Foundational understanding of group retirement programs, pricing, and reporting (or strong ability to learn quickly)
Advanced Excel skills and experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI)
Demonstrated hands-on experience using AI tools (e.g., Copilot, automation tools) to enhance analysis, improve workflows, and generate insights
Strong ability to work with large, multi-source datasets and translate findings into clear business narratives
Experience supporting pricing, proposals, or client-facing deliverables is an asset
High attention to detail, organization, and ability to manage multiple priorities
Strong communication and presentation skills, including building client-ready materials
Curiosity, adaptability, and a mindset of continuous improvement through technology


What’s In It For You


An opportunity to make a real impact in Indigenous communities across Canada
Involvement in meaningful work supporting Truth and Reconciliation efforts
Ongoing learning and collaboration with a supportive and experienced team
Competitive compensation
Comprehensive employee benefits and retirement programs
A fun, flexible, and productive work environment


Our Commitment

National Indigenous Solutions is committed to building a team that reflects the communities we serve. We welcome and encourage applications from First Nations, Métis, and Inuit candidates. Applicants are invited to self-declare Indigenous identity in their application.

The National Indigenous Solutions Practice, a division of People Corporation, is a trusted Group Insurance & Retirement practice proudly serving Indigenous employers across Canada. The National Indigenous Solutions Practice is comprised of three sister companies – Eagle Bay Financial (Burnaby, BC), Life Benefit Solutions (Winnipeg, MB) and WP Pension and Benefits (Burlington, ON) who all specialize in designing benefit and group retirement programs that thoughtfully integrate Non-Insured Health Benefits (NIHB), supporting equity between Status and non-Status employees. Their mission is to deliver customized, community-conscious solutions that meet the unique needs of Indigenous and non-Indigenous employees alike.

We are committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive, and accessible environment where all employees and clients feel supported and valued. We are proud to build a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities we serve and to create an environment where everyone has the opportunity to thrive.

Providing an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and clients feel valued, respected and supported is something we're committed to. We are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities in which we live, and to creating an environment where every employee has the opportunity to reach their potential. If you require an accommodation or an alternative format of any posting please reach out to careers@peoplecorporation.com.

The base salary range for this position is $55,000 - $75,000 per annum.

This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, etc). The actual base salary will vary depending on local market conditions, geography and relevant job-related factors such as knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience, and education/training. If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, the Talent Acquisition Specialist will be able to provide additional details of the total compensation for this role.

Title: Supply Chain Data Analyst
Description:
Convergint is looking for a team-oriented, results-driven, Supply Chain Data Analyst, to join our amazing culture. As the Supply Chain Data Analyst, you will be responsible for maintaining vendor item master catalogs and provide reporting and analytics to support the source to pay process. This role involves tasks like data entry, updates, and resolving discrepancies, with a strong focus on data integrity and efficiency in operations. This role has an emphasis on support of the supply chain and sales teams, and motivation in building effective business relationships with vendors and customers alike.

Who You Are

You have a passion for providing world-class service to customers, colleagues and communities. You are a person of integrity with a commitment to growth, accountability and delivering results. You want to join an organization with a positive culture that embraces inclusion and allows everyone to be the best version of themselves at work and home.

Who We Are

With 20-years of proven growth and exceptional performance, our mission is to be our customer’s best service provider. We realize the importance of diversity in achieving that goal. Our company was built upon a solid foundation of 10 Values and Beliefs which drive our unmatched culture, making us the #1 global, serviced-based systems integrator in the industry. We take great pride in protecting the lives and assets of our customers and their communities with the solutions we provide.

What you’ll do with “Our Training and Your Experience”


Creating, updating, and managing item records within relevant systems.
Ensure the accuracy, completeness, and consistency of item data, including descriptions, classifications, units of measure, vendor information, pricing, and other relevant details.
Conduct regular audits to identify and correct errors, duplicates, or outdated records.
Manage the lifecycle of item records, including creation, updates, and decommissioning.
Support for item master data management, including troubleshooting issues and assisting with system enhancements.
Interact with suppliers, manufacturers, and distributors on an on-going basis, which may include negotiating prices, discounts, credit terms, and logistics.
Participating in the development and implementation of best practices for item master data management.
Work with Supply Chain leadership to develop and implement best practices, demand planning, and other optimizations in order to help grow the business and to ensure process transparency, fairness, impartiality, and confidentiality.
Collaborate with global counterparts to align with process standardization and consistency in reporting across all regions.
Provide reporting and data analytics to Supply Chain leadership to show trends, address discrepancies, and generate accurate forecasting.
Develop constructive and cooperative working relationships with internal stakeholders and vendors alike and maintain them over time.
Executes all work in accordance with Provincial/State, local regulations, general best practices as well as company health and safety policies and procedures.
Acts as “our customer’s best service provider” at all times thereby ensuring Convergint Technologies is the customer’s first choice for service.
Communicates with and works with the other team members consistently to improve overall operations of the Convergint Technology Center, and company.
Keeps up to date and informed on all company policies.
Presents a professional image of Convergint Technologies at all times in appearance and behavior.
Performs other duties and responsibilities as requested or required.


What You’ll Need


Strong attention to details.
Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills and ability to deal with a wide cross section of people both internal and external to the business.
Excellent Excel, Word, and Outlook skills.
Excellent proficiency in financial software specifically related to purchase order entry, accounts payable, and inventory management.
Strong negotiation skills.
Strong financial knowledge and skills—ability to understand basic financials such as accounts receivable/payable, purchase orders, work orders, etc.
Excellent organizational skills and the ability to handle multiple projects tasks simultaneously.
Excellent attention to detail.
Strong flexibility to adapt to changing priorities, work processes, department structure, environment and technology in a dynamic work environment.
Self-driven and motivated to meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Strong analytical skills are necessary to resolve problems and look for solutions.
Strong ability to demonstrate independence, resourcefulness and tenacity.
Shows initiative – regularly engages in proactive behavior and looks for opportunities.


Requirements


Diploma and/or certificate in Business Administration or Purchasing required.
SCMP designation highly desirable.
Minimum of five years of procurement experience including elements of the supply chain
preferred.
Minimum of five years of negotiation and vendor management experience preferred.
Minimum of five years of customer service experience is required.
Experience in Ivalua, Salesforce, and NetSuite highly desirable.
Relevant industry experience in electrical, security, building automation, or fire is required


Company Benefits And Perks

Convergint fosters a supportive, accessible, and inclusive environment in which individuals of different backgrounds and identities are able to realize their maximum potential within the company. We offer a variety of programs and exceptional benefits to all of our colleagues:


Company Holidays and Paid Time Off
Immediate comprehensive benefit plan (medical, dental, etc.)
Employer contribution to RRSP
Work environment committed to safety.
Colleague Assistance Program
Tuition reimbursement
Competitive salary and compensation plan
Corporate Social Responsibility Day
And much more…


Convergint is committed to a culture of Inclusion and Diversity and is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Visit our Convergint careers site to learn more about the company and the exciting opportunities available.
